candidates.label: Jury
candidates.definition: A body of persons (jurors) temporarily selected from a jurisdiction's citizenry that is invested with power to present or indict a person for a public offense or to try a question of fact.

candidates.label: Individual Debtor
candidates.definition: An individual who owes money or other obligations to another party and is personally responsible for repayment.

candidates.label: Individual Contributor
candidates.definition: An Individual Contributor is an employee who specializes in performing specific tasks or roles within an organization but does not have managerial responsibilities over a team. Individual Contributors focus on executing their specialized skill sets and are evaluated based on their own performance rather than the performance of a group.

candidates.label: Decedent
candidates.definition: A decedent is a legal term used to refer to a deceased person, particularly in the context of estate planning and probate law. The decedent's estate encompasses all assets, liabilities, rights, and obligations left behind, which are subject to administration and distribution according to the decedent's will or state intestacy laws.

candidates.label: Legatee
candidates.definition: A legatee is an individual or entity named in a will to receive personal property or money from the estate of the deceased. This term differentiates from a devisee, who inherits real property.

candidates.label: Employment Title
candidates.definition: Employment Title refers to the formal designation given to a specific job position within an organization, such as "Software Engineer," "Marketing Manager," or "Legal Counsel." The title is often used to signify the roles and responsibilities of the position, and may also indicate the employee's rank or level within the organizational hierarchy

candidates.label: Roving Employment
candidates.definition: Roving Employment refers to a work arrangement where an employee does not have a fixed work location but instead moves between different sites, offices, or geographic areas as part of their job responsibilities. This setup may require extensive travel and adaptability, and is often seen in roles like sales, consulting, and field services.

candidates.label: Employment Location Type
candidates.definition: Employment Location Type describes where an employee performs their job duties and can include categories such as onsite, offsite, remote, roving, or third-party placement. These classifications help define the employee's working environment and may impact job responsibilities, oversight, and eligibility for certain benefits or allowances

candidates.label: Active Employment Status
candidates.definition: Active Employment refers to the current, ongoing employment relationship between an employee and an employer, where the employee is actively engaged in work and fulfilling job responsibilities. This status usually means that the employee is eligible for all the benefits, salary, and protections provided by the employer

candidates.label: Employment Classification
candidates.definition: Worker Classification refers to the categorization of an employee's work status within an organization, often defined as full-time, part-time, or contractor. These classifications dictate the terms of employment, including benefits eligibility, tax implications, and legal obligations between the employer and employee.

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Individual Rights Law
candidates.definition: Individual rights such as privacy and the freedom of thought, speech, religion, press, assembly, and movement.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Employment Immigration Law
candidates.definition: National statutes, and regulations governing employment-based immigration into and deportation from a country.

candidates.label: Employment Discrimination Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to protected class employment discrimination including retaliation and harassment
synonyms: Loi sur la discrimination en matière d'emploi

candidates.label: Employment Health and Safety Law
candidates.definition: Law governing the health and safety of employees.
synonyms: Droit de la santé et de la sécurité au travail
Lei de Saúde e Segurança no Trabalho
Ley de Salud y Seguridad en el Empleo

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Mass Torts Law
candidates.definition: Mass torts law is an area of law that deals with legal actions involving numerous plaintiffs against one or a few defendants, where the plaintiffs allege harm caused by common actions or products. These cases often involve complex negotiations and litigation due to the large number of claimants, extensive evidence, and significant legal and financial stakes.

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Retirement Account
candidates.definition: A Retirement Account is a financial account designed to help individuals save for their retirement, often offering tax advantages. Types of retirement accounts include 401(k)s, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), and pension plans, among others, and they typically come with restrictions on withdrawals to incentivize long-term saving. Relevant to several areas of law (e.g., Bankruptcy Law).

candidates.label: Chattels
candidates.definition: Chattels are personal property items that are movable and do not include land or interests in land. This legal term encompasses tangible and intangible assets that a person owns, which can be physically relocated or transferred from one place to another, as opposed to real property or immovable assets.

candidates.label: Estate
candidates.definition: An Estate refers to all the possessions, property, and legal rights owned by an individual, including land, tenements, and personal assets. It also encompasses the total assets and liabilities left by a person at death, which are subject to distribution according to the terms of a will or the laws of intestacy.

candidates.label: Personal Watercraft
synonyms: A Personal Watercraft (PWC) is a small, motorized watercraft designed for individual use, where the rider typically sits or stands on the vessel rather than inside it. PWCs are commonly used for recreational activities, including racing, touring, and water sports.

candidates.label: Wild Animal
candidates.definition: A Wild Animal is a species that lives and thrives in its natural habitat without direct dependence on or control by humans. These animals have not been domesticated and typically rely on their instincts and the environment to find food, shelter, and survive.

candidates.label: Domesticated Animal
candidates.definition: A Domesticated Animal is a species that has been bred and adapted over generations to live in close association with humans, often providing companionship, labor, or products such as milk, eggs, and wool. These animals are typically dependent on humans for food, shelter, and care.

candidates.label: Vertebrate Animal
candidates.definition: A Vertebrate Animal is a member of the subphylum Vertebrata within the phylum Chordata, characterized by having a backbone or spinal column. Vertebrates include a diverse group of animals such as mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and fish, all of which possess a well-defined internal skeleton and complex organ systems.

candidates.label: Invertebrate Animal
candidates.definition: An Invertebrate Animal is a member of the animal kingdom that lacks a backbone or spinal column. Invertebrates encompass a vast array of species including insects, mollusks, crustaceans, arachnids, and echinoderms, and they constitute the majority of animal diversity on Earth.

candidates.label: Animal Domestication Type
candidates.definition: Animal Domestication Type refers to the classification of animals based on their relationship and dependence on humans, distinguishing between those that have been domesticated for companionship, labor, or product provision, and those that live and thrive in the wild without human intervention.

candidates.label: Personal Information
candidates.definition: Personal Information refers to any data that relates to an identified or identifiable individual. This includes both sensitive personal information, which requires greater protection due to its potential to cause harm if disclosed, and personally identifiable information (PII), which can be used to identify, contact, or locate a specific individual.

candidates.label: Employment Record
candidates.definition: An "Employment Record" is a structured historical record that documents an individual's employment-related information, including details about their work history, positions held, employers, dates of employment, and relevant achievements.

candidates.label: Employment History
candidates.definition: Employment History refers to the comprehensive record of an individual's past and current work experiences, including roles, responsibilities, and durations at various employers. This record serves as a vital element in assessing an individual's qualifications, skills, and career progression for various legal and professional purposes.

candidates.label: Employment Document
candidates.definition: An Employment Document refers to any formal paperwork or electronic record that is generated, used, or managed within the context of an individual's employment relationship with an organization. These documents may include, but are not limited to, items like disciplinary records, job applications, promotion letters, and performance evaluations.

candidates.label: Employment Agreement
candidates.definition: An Employment Agreement is a legally binding contract between an employer and an employee that outlines the terms and conditions of their working relationship, including compensation, benefits, and responsibilities.

candidates.label: Employment Eligibility Verification Form
candidates.definition: The Employment Eligibility Verification Form (in the U.S., the I-9) is a form required by the government for all employees to verify their identity and authorization to work in the country. Employers must complete and retain an Employment Eligibility Verification Form for each person they hire, and the form must be available for inspection by authorized government officers.

candidates.label: Employment Authorization Document Application
candidates.definition: The Employment Authorization Document (EAD) application, commonly known as Form I-765 in the United States, is used by certain non-immigrants, refugees, and asylees who are eligible to work legally in the U.S. The EAD, also referred to as a work permit, allows individuals to engage in employment during their authorized stay in the U.S. This application typically requires the submission of necessary documentation, appropriate fees, and proof of eligibility. Once approved, the applicant receives an EAD card that authorizes them to work for a specified period

candidates.label: Objection to Application for Professional Employment
candidates.definition: An objection made specifically in response to an application for professional employment.
synonyms: Einspruch gegen die Bewerbung auf eine professionelle Beschäftigung
Objection to Application for Professional Employment
Objeção à solicitação de emprego profissional
Oposición a la solicitud de empleo profesional
Opposition à la demande d'emploi professionnel

専門的雇用申請に対する異議

hierarchy_path.label: Objection to Application for Professional Employment

candidates.label: Motions and Filings to Disqualify Decisionmaker
candidates.definition: A Motion or Filing to Disqualify Decisionmaker is a legal request made by a party to remove a judge, arbitrator, or other decisionmaker from a case due to a perceived conflict of interest or bias.
synonyms: Anträge und Anmeldungen zur Ablehnung des Entscheidungsträgers
Mociones y presentaciones para descalificar al tomador de decisiones
Motions and Filings to Disqualify Decisionmaker
Motions et dépôts pour disqualifier le décideur
Petições e requerimentos para desqualificar o tomador de decisão
निर्णय लेने वाले को अयोग्य ठहराने के लिए आवेदन और दाखिले
撤销决策者的动议和申请书
決定者を拒否するための動議と申立書

hierarchy_path.label: Motions and Filings to Disqualify Decisionmaker

candidates.label: Civil Cover Sheet
candidates.definition: A Civil Cover Sheet is a document used in trial court practice to provide basic information about a civil case, such as the parties involved and the nature of the case.
synonyms: 民事訴訟カバーシート

candidates.label: Insured Matter
candidates.definition: An insured matter refers to the specific risk or subject covered under an insurance policy, including the circumstances, conditions, and objects that the insurance agreement protects against.

candidates.label: Matter Net Fees
candidates.definition: Matter Net Fees are the fees charged for legal services provided in a specific matter, after deducting any expenses or disbursements incurred by the lawyer.

candidates.label: Matter Total Due
candidates.definition: The Matter Total Due is a specific detail within the broader category of Engagement Fee Details, referring to the total amount owed for legal services provided in a particular matter.

candidates.label: Matter Final Bill
candidates.definition: A "Matter Final Bill" is a detailed record of the final charges for legal services provided in a specific matter, and is a subcategory of the broader concept of "Engagement Fee Detail".

candidates.label: Matter Description
candidates.definition: The textual description of the work performed in the billing period.

candidates.label: Matter Tax on Fees
candidates.definition: A Matter Tax on Fees is a type of Tax Detail and Engagement Fee Detail that refers to a tax imposed on the fees charged for legal services provided in a specific matter or case.
synonyms: Impuesto sobre la materia gravable de las tarifas
Taxe sur les frais de dossier

candidates.label: Matter Net Expenses
candidates.definition: Matter Net Expenses refers to the costs incurred by a law firm in providing legal services to a client, which are then passed on to the client as part of the engagement fee.

candidates.label: Matter Funds Applied
candidates.definition: The concept of "Matter Funds Applied" refers to the portion of an engagement fee that is specifically allocated to cover expenses related to a particular legal matter, as opposed to the fee being applied generally to the overall engagement.

candidates.label: Matter Accumulated Fees
candidates.definition: Matter Accumulated Fees: Fees accumulated for a specific legal matter within an engagement for providing legal services.

candidates.label: Criminal Matter Identifiers
candidates.definition: Criminal Matter Identifiers are unique codes or numbers used to track and identify criminal cases, including information such as law enforcement arrest tracking numbers, fatality indicators, commercial motor vehicle indicators, law enforcement incident tracking numbers, and traffic accident indicators.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Employment Events
candidates.definition: Employment Events refer to specific milestones in an employee's work history with an organization, such as the date of hiring, date of termination, and date of re-hiring. These events are important for both administrative record-keeping and compliance with employment laws and regulations

candidates.label: Employment Offer
candidates.definition: A job offer is a formal proposal made by an employer to a candidate selected for a position, detailing the terms and conditions of employment. This includes information about the role, salary, benefits, start date, and other pertinent details.

candidates.label: Employment Written Warning
candidates.definition: An Employment Written Warning is a formal document issued by an employer to an employee, outlining specific instances of misconduct or performance issues. It serves as an official notice that continued violations may result in further disciplinary action, including termination.

candidates.label: Employment Mandatory Training
candidates.definition: Employment Mandatory Training is a compulsory educational program required by an employer to address specific skills, knowledge gaps, or compliance issues among employees. This training aims to improve performance, ensure adherence to company policies, or meet legal and regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Voluntary Employment Termination Event
candidates.definition: Voluntary employment termination event refers to the act of an employee willingly leaving their job, either through resignation or retirement. This is distinct from involuntary termination, where the employer initiates the separation due to reasons like downsizing, performance issues, or misconduct.

candidates.label: Involuntary Employment Termination Event
candidates.definition: Involuntary employment termination event refers to the act of an employer initiating the separation of an employee from their job. Reasons can range from downsizing and organizational restructuring to performance issues or misconduct.

candidates.label: Mergers and Acquisitions Events
candidates.definition: Mergers and Acquisitions (M&A) Events refer to the specific events and phases involved in the process of merging or acquiring one company by another, including the due diligence and closing events.

candidates.label: Alternative Dispute Resolution Events
candidates.definition: Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) Events refer to the events or phases involved in resolving legal disputes outside of traditional litigation, including arbitration and mediation.
synonyms: Eventos de Resolución Alternativa de Controversias
Eventos de Resolución Alternativa de Disputas
Eventos de resolução alternativa de disputas
Événements de règlement alternatif des litiges
代替紛争解決イベント

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: Employment Benefits
candidates.definition: Employment Benefits are additional compensations or advantages provided to employees over and above their regular salary, often as part of an employment contract. These benefits may include healthcare, retirement plans, paid time off, and other perks designed to attract and retain employees.

candidates.label: Civil Court
candidates.definition: A Civil Court is a type of court forum that has jurisdiction over non-criminal cases involving private disputes between individuals or organizations.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court
candidates.definition: A court in New York State that handles civil legal matters.

candidates.label: New York City - Civil Court
candidates.definition: N.Y. City Civ. Ct.

candidates.label: Texas Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: The Texas Court of Civil Appeals is a specific court within the Texas State Courts system that hears appeals in civil cases.

candidates.label: Alabama Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Ala. Civ. App.

candidates.label: Oklahoma Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Okla. Civ. App.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County
candidates.definition: A court located in Bronx County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Kings County
candidates.definition: A court located in Kings County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Kings County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Queens County
candidates.definition: A court located in Queens County, New York that handles civil legal disputes within its jurisdiction.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Queens County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - New York County
candidates.definition: A court located in New York County that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - New York County

candidates.label: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
candidates.definition: The U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) is a federal agency that administers and enforces civil rights laws against workplace discrimination.
synonyms: Comisión de Igualdad de Oportunidades de Empleo de los Estados Unidos
Comisión para la Igualdad de Oportunidades en el Empleo de los Estados Unidos
Comissão de Igualdade de Oportunidades de Emprego dos EUA
Commission américaine pour l'égalité des chances en matière d'emploi
US-amerikanische Kommission für Chancengleichheit am Arbeitsplatz
הוועדה לשוויון הזדמנויות בעבודה של ארצות הברית
米国労働機会均等委員会
美国平等就业机会委员会

candidates.label: Office of Special Counsel for Immigration-Related Unfair Employment Practices
candidates.definition: The Office of Special Counsel for Immigration-Related Unfair Employment Practices (OSC) investigates and prosecutes allegations of national origin and citizenship status discrimination in hiring, firing, and recruitment or referral for a fee, as well as unfair documentary practices during the employment eligibility verification process and retaliation under the anti-discrimination provision of the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA). In addition, OSC conducts outreach aimed at educating employers, workers and the general public about their rights and responsibilities under the INA's anti-discrimination provision.
synonyms: Bureau du conseiller spécial pour les pratiques d'emploi injustes liées à l'immigration
Büro des Sonderberaters für Einwanderungsbezogene Unfaire Beschäftigungspraktiken
Escritório do Conselheiro Especial para Práticas de Emprego Injustas Relacionadas à Imigração

Oficina del Consejero Especial para Prácticas de Empleo Injustas Relacionadas con la Inmigración
המשרד לייעוץ מיוחד לפעילויות עסקיות בתחום ההעסקה עם עובדים זרים
विशेष जांच अधिकारी कार्यालय फॉर इमिग्रेशन-संबंधित अन्यायपूर्ण रोजगार प्रथाओं के लिए
移民相关不公平就业实践特别顾问办公室

candidates.label: Civil Rights Center
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor Civil Rights Center (CRC) develops, administers, and enforces Departmental policies, practices, and procedures pursuant to Title VI and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Section 188 of the Workforce Investment Act (WIA), as amended; the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended; the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, as amended; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; the Equal Pay Act of 1963; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, as amended; Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, as amended; the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978; the Civil Rights Act of 1991; the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation (No FEAR) Act; and related statutes and Executive Orders.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Division
candidates.definition: The Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice, created in 1957 by the enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1957, works to uphold the civil and constitutional rights of all Americans, particularly some of the most vulnerable members of our society. The Division enforces federal statutes prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, disability, religion, familial status and national origin.

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
candidates.definition: The Office for Civil Rights (OCR) is a sub-agency of the U.S. Department of Education that is primarily focused on enforcing civil rights laws prohibiting schools from engaging in discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or membership in patriotic youth organizations.
synonyms: Bureau des droits civils - Ministère de l'Éducation
Escritório de Direitos Civis - Departamento de Educação
Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
Oficina de Derechos Civiles - Departamento de Educación
市民権事務局 - 教育省
民权办公室 - 教育部

hierarchy_path.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - HHS
candidates.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) enforces federal civil rights laws, conscience and religious freedom laws, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules, and the Patient Safety Act and Rule, which together protect fundamental rights of nondiscrimination, conscience, religious freedom, and health information privacy.

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
candidates.definition: The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is a bipartisan, independent commission of the United States federal government, created in 1957, that is charged with the responsibility for investigating, reporting on, and making recommendations concerning civil rights issues in the United States.
synonyms: Comisión de Derechos Civiles de los Estados Unidos
Comissão de Direitos Civis dos EUA
アメリカ合衆国市民権委員会

candidates.label: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
candidates.definition: The Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) is an agency of the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) based in Riverdale, Maryland responsible for protecting animal health, animal welfare, and plant health. APHIS is the lead agency for collaboration with other agencies to protect U.S. agriculture from invasive pests and diseases.
synonyms: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
Dienst für Inspektion von Tier- und Pflanzengesundheit
Service d'inspection de la santé des animaux et des plantes
Servicio de Inspección de Sanidad Agropecuaria y Forestal

hierarchy_path.label: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service

candidates.label: Smithsonian Institution
candidates.definition: The Smithsonian Institution is a group of museums and research centers administered by the Government of the United States.

candidates.label: U.S. Dept. of Education
candidates.definition: The United States Department of Education (ED or DoED) is a Cabinet-level department of the United States government. The primary functions of the Department of Education are to "establish policy for, administer and coordinate most federal assistance to education, collect data on US schools, and to enforce federal educational laws regarding privacy and civil rights."
synonyms: Departamento de Educación de EE. UU.
アメリカ合衆国教育省

candidates.label: Specialty Canning
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing canned specialty foods. Examples of products made in these establishments are canned baby food, canned baked beans, canned soups (except seafood), canned spaghetti, and other canned nationality foods.

hierarchy_path.label: Fruit and Vegetable Preserving and Specialty Food Manufacturing
Fruit and Vegetable Canning, Pickling, and Drying

candidates.label: Specialty Food Retailers
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing specialized lines of food not for immediate consumption.

candidates.label: Other Specialty Food Retailers
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing specialty foods (except fruits and vegetables, meat, fish, and seafood) not for immediate consumption and not made on the premises.

candidates.label: Other Specialty Trade Contractors
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in site preparation activities and in specialized trades (except foundation, structure, and building exterior contractors; building equipment contractors; and building finishing contractors). The specialty trade work performed includes new work, additions, alterations, maintenance, and repairs.

candidates.label: All Other Specialty Food Retailers
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing miscellaneous specialty foods (except fruit and vegetables, meat, fish, seafood, confections, nuts, popcorn, and baked goods) not for immediate consumption and not made on the premises.

candidates.label: Frozen Specialty Food Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing frozen specialty foods (except seafood), such as frozen dinners, entrees, and side dishes; frozen pizza; frozen whipped topping; and frozen waffles, pancakes, and French toast.

hierarchy_path.label: Fruit and Vegetable Preserving and Specialty Food Manufacturing

candidates.label: Specialty Trade Contractors Industry
candidates.definition:
The Specialty Trade Contractors subsector comprises establishments whose primary activity is performing specific activities (e.g., pouring concrete, site preparation, plumbing, painting, and electrical work) involved in building construction or other activities that are similar for all types of construction, but that are not responsible for the entire project.  The work performed may include new work, additions, alterations, maintenance, and repairs.  The production work performed by establishments in this subsector is usually subcontracted from establishments of the general contractor type or for-sale builders, but especially in remodeling and repair construction, work also may be done directly for the owner of the property.  Specialty trade contractors usually perform most of their work at the construction site, although they may have shops where they perform prefabrication and other work.  Establishments primarily engaged in preparing sites for new construction are also included in this subsector.

There are substantial differences in types of equipment, work force skills, and other inputs required by specialty trade contractors.  Establishments in this subsector are classified based on the underlying production function for the specialty trade in which they specialize.  Throughout the Specialty Trade Contractors subsector, establishments commonly provide both the parts and labor required to complete work.  For example, electrical contractors supply the current-carrying and noncurrent-carrying wiring devices that are required to install a circuit.  Plumbing, heating, and air-conditioning contractors also supply the parts required to complete a contract. 

Establishments that specialize in activities primarily related to heavy and civil engineering construction that are not normally performed on buildings, such as the painting of lines on highways, are classified in Subsector 237, Heavy and Civil Engineering Construction.

Establishments that are primarily engaged in selling construction materials are classified in Sector 42, Wholesale Trade, or Sector 44-45, Retail Trade, based on the characteristics of the selling unit.

candidates.label: All Other Specialty Trade Contractors
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in specialized trades (except foundation, structure, and building exterior contractors; building equipment contractors; building finishing contractors; and site preparation contractors). The specialty trade work performed includes new work, additions, alterations, maintenance, and repairs.

candidates.label: All Other Specialty Trade Contractors
candidates.definition: See industry description for 238990.

candidates.label: Power, Distribution, and Specialty Transformer Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing power, distribution, and specialty transformers (except electronic components). Industrial-type and consumer-type transformers in this industry vary (e.g., step up or step down) voltage but do not convert alternating to direct or direct to alternating current.

candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.

Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels

candidates.definition: The AAA Employment Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedures are a set of guidelines and procedures established by the American Arbitration Association specifically for resolving employment-related disputes through arbitration and mediation.

candidates.label: Specialty Rulesets
candidates.definition: Specialty Rulesets are a subset of Rule Type in legal knowledge management, consisting of specific sets of rules and regulations related to a particular area of law, such as Personal Injury or COVID.

candidates.label: Civil Law System
candidates.definition: Civil Law is a legal system that is primarily based on written codes and statutes, rather than case law. This system emphasizes comprehensive sets of laws that are codified and accessible, guiding judges in applying the law to individual cases.

candidates.label: Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: A court's body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Specialty Court Rules
candidates.definition: Procedural rules governing specialty courts (e.g., bankruptcy courts, immigration courts)

candidates.label: Rule Type
candidates.definition: A Rule Type is a specific type of Litigation Procedural Rule that outlines the procedures and requirements for a particular type of legal case, such as a pilot case or a case with complex issues.

candidates.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing Multidistrict Litigation (MDLs).

candidates.label: Contusion
candidates.definition: Moderate traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in brain function caused by an external mechanical force, leading to a loss of consciousness lasting from 30 minutes to 24 hours, or post-traumatic amnesia lasting from 1 to 7 days. It requires medical intervention and often results in lasting cognitive, physical, and emotional impairments that may necessitate rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Concussion
candidates.definition: Mild traumatic brain injury (concussion) is a form of TBI resulting from a blow or jolt to the head that temporarily disrupts brain function. Symptoms can include headache, confusion, dizziness, and memory loss, and while typically short-term, they may require rest and medical monitoring.

candidates.label: Individual Person
candidates.definition: An individual or natural person is a person that is an individual human being, as opposed to a legal person or entity, which may be a private or public organization.

candidates.label: Sole Proprietorship
candidates.definition: A sole proprietorship, also known as the sole trader, individual entrepreneurship or proprietorship, is a type of enterprise that is owned and run by one person and in which there is no legal distinction between the owner and the business entity. The sole trader receives all profits (subject to taxation specific to the business) and has unlimited responsibility for all losses and debts. Every asset of the business is owned by the proprietor and all debts of the business are the proprietor's. It is a "sole" proprietorship in contrast with partnerships (which have at least two owners).

candidates.label: Civil Union
candidates.definition: A civil union is a legally recognized arrangement similar to marriage, which provides some of the legal and social protections afforded to married couples. However, it may lack some of the federal benefits and recognition that come with marriage.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Partnership
candidates.definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

candidates.label: Individual Person Characteristics
candidates.definition: Individual person characteristics refer to the distinct attributes or qualities that define a person's identity and differentiate them from others. These characteristics can include things like age, gender, race, ethnicity, national origin, and disability.

candidates.label: Legal Entity
candidates.definition: A person, company, organization, or other entity that has legal rights and obligations.

candidates.label: Burns
candidates.definition: Burns are injuries to the skin or other tissues caused by exposure to heat, chemicals, electricity, or radiation. They are categorized by severity into first, second, third, and fourth degrees based on the depth and extent of tissue damage.

candidates.label: Matter Management
candidates.definition: Matter Management refers to the process of organizing and overseeing legal cases and related tasks, such as document management and budgeting, within a legal operations framework.

candidates.label: Litigation Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Management involves the management of various aspects of a legal case, including but not limited to, retention and litigation hold management, redaction management, expert witness management, brief analysis management, jury selection management, citation check management, remote trial management, eDiscovery services, exhibit management, and costs and settlement management.

candidates.label: Litigation Finance Management
candidates.definition: Litigation finance management refers to the process of managing the financial aspects of a legal case, including funding, budgeting, and accounting, in order to maximize the potential return on investment for the parties involved.

candidates.label: Litigation Analytics Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Analytics Management is the systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and utilizing data and insights from legal disputes to inform and improve decision-making, strategy, and performance in legal practice, often through the use of specialized software and tools.

candidates.label: Litigation Collaboration Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Collaboration Management refers to the process of managing and coordinating the efforts of multiple parties involved in a legal dispute, with the goal of achieving a successful outcome.

candidates.label: Retention and Litigation Hold Management
candidates.definition: Retention and Litigation Hold Management refers to the process of preserving and managing electronic data that may be relevant to a legal matter, in order to comply with legal obligations and avoid spoliation sanctions.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A textual narrative of a matter's factual and legal details.

candidates.label: Matter Complexity
candidates.definition: Classification for a given' matter's complexity, either textually (e.g., low, medium, high) or numerically (e.g., 1 through 10).

candidates.label: Public Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A public version of a matter narrative.

candidates.label: Generic Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A generic version of a matter narrative. Usually used to further describe a category of matters.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Audience
candidates.definition: A Matter Narrative Audience is a group or individual for whom the factual and legal details of a matter are presented in a textual narrative.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Description
candidates.definition: A textual narrative description, detailing a matter's factual and legal details.

candidates.label: Confidential Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A confidential version of a matter narrative.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Format
candidates.definition: The file format containing the narrative description of a matter.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Personal Injury Claims are civil claims or tort claims seeking compensation for physical or emotional harm caused by the negligence or intentional actions of another party, including but not limited to slip-and-fall accidents, medical malpractice, and motor vehicle accidents.

candidates.label: Liability for Injury to Pets
candidates.definition: Liability for Injury to Pets refers to the legal responsibility for harm caused to a domesticated animal, such as a dog or cat, and the resulting damages that may be awarded to the pet owner.
synonyms: Responsabilidade por lesões em animais de estimação
Responsabilité pour les blessures aux animaux de compagnie
पालतू जानवरों के घायल होने के लिए दायित्व
ペットの負傷に対する責任

candidates.label: Partnership Liability for Injury
candidates.definition: Partnership Liability for Injury refers to the legal responsibility of a partnership for injuries caused to a third party due to the actions or negligence of the partnership or its partners.
synonyms: Responsabilidad de la sociedad por lesiones
Responsabilité de la société de personnes pour blessures
घोषणा द्वारा घात के लिए भागीदारी दायित्व
パートナーシップの責任における傷害

candidates.label: Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury
candidates.definition: Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury: A claim for damages resulting from a personal injury that has not yet manifested, but is likely to occur in the future as a direct result of the initial injury.
synonyms: Blessure future résultant d'un préjudice corporel actuel
Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury
Lesiones futuras a partir de una lesión personal ilícita presente
Lesão futura decorrente de lesão pessoal ilícita presente

פציעה עתידית מפגיעה אישית עוינת נוכחית

candidates.label: Industrial Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Industrial Injury Claims refer to legal claims made by individuals who have suffered harm or injury while working in an industrial setting, such as a factory or construction site.
synonyms: Reivindicações de indenização por acidentes de trabalho

candidates.label: Education Liability
candidates.definition: Education Liability refers to the legal responsibility and accountability of educational institutions and their staff for any harm or injury caused to students or their families, including civil claims related to privacy of school records, corporal punishment, compulsory education, and prayer in public school.

candidates.label: Construction Liability
candidates.definition: Construction Liability refers to the legal responsibility of parties involved in construction projects for damages or injuries resulting from their actions or inactions, including claims related to construction defects or negligence.

candidates.label: Agent Liability
candidates.definition: Agent Liability refers to the legal responsibility of an individual or entity acting as an agent for another party, for any harm caused to a third party due to their negligence or breach of fiduciary duty.

candidates.label: Family Liability
candidates.definition: Family Liability refers to the legal responsibility of family members for the actions or debts of other family members, including spousal and parent-child liability, as well as agency and vicarious liability.

candidates.label: Spousal Liability
candidates.definition: Spousal Liability refers to the legal responsibility of one spouse for the debts incurred by the other spouse.

candidates.label: Fee and Employment Practice
candidates.definition: Fee and Employment Practice (Bankruptcy) refers to the legal work related to the compensation of professionals and employment issues in the context of bankruptcy proceedings.

candidates.label: Employment Objection Process
candidates.definition: The Employment Objection Process (Bankruptcy) refers to the legal procedure in bankruptcy cases where an objection is raised regarding the employment of a professional, such as an attorney or accountant, by the debtor.
synonyms: 雇用異議申し立てプロセス

candidates.label: Employment Application Process
candidates.definition: The Employment Application Process (Bankruptcy) refers to the legal procedures and requirements involved in filing for bankruptcy as it relates to employment of bankruptcy professionals.

candidates.label: Employment Transactions Practice
candidates.definition: Employment Transactions Practice refers to the area of law that involves the structuring, negotiation, and documentation of agreements related to employment relationships. This includes contracts, compensation plans, and other legal frameworks governing the employee-employer dynamic.

candidates.label: M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits Practice
candidates.definition: A legal practice area that focuses on employment, labor, and employee benefits issues related to mergers and acquisitions, including executive compensation matters.
synonyms: M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits
M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits Service

candidates.label: Individualized Restructuring
candidates.definition: Individualized restructuring involves private negotiations between a debtor and one or more creditors to modify the terms of existing debt agreements. This process allows parties to tailor solutions to their specific needs without court intervention, often resulting in revised payment schedules, reduced interest rates, or partial debt forgiveness.

candidates.label: M&A J Post-Closing Requirements, Disputes & Adjustments Practice
candidates.definition: A practice area within M&A law that deals with the requirements, disputes, and adjustments that arise after a merger or acquisition has been completed.

candidates.label: Civil Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Litigation Docket Number
candidates.definition: A Litigation Docket Number is a unique identifier assigned to a legal case that is used to track and manage the case within the court system.

candidates.label: Driver
candidates.definition: A driver is an individual who operates a motor vehicle, controlling its direction and speed on public or private roads. This role requires adherence to traffic laws and regulations to ensure safety and order on thoroughfares.

candidates.label: Motorcyclist
candidates.definition: A motorcycle driver is an individual who operates a motorcycle, controlling its mechanisms to navigate roads and other environments. This role entails a distinct set of skills and adherence to specific traffic laws to ensure the safety of the driver and other road users.

candidates.label: Incident Reconstruction Expert
candidates.definition: An expert who inspects an accident scene, examining evidence and applying the laws of physics to determine the accident's cause. Cruso v. Greater Lakeside Corp., 425 So.2d 953 (La. App. 1983)
synonyms: インシデント再現専門家

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Single Asset Real Estate Debtor
candidates.definition: A Single Asset Real Estate Debtor (SARE Debtor) refers to a specific classification in bankruptcy proceedings for debtors whose primary business involves a single real estate property. This classification impacts how bankruptcy cases are handled, particularly by expediting the process and applying unique rules concerning automatic stays and reorganization plans.

candidates.label: Governmental Criminal Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Criminal Law Division is a government entity responsible for prosecuting criminal cases on behalf of the public. It conducts investigations, files charges, represents the government in criminal trials, and ensures the enforcement of criminal laws.

candidates.label: Role in Matter
candidates.definition: An individual or entity's role, responsibility, or function in executing the legal matter.

candidates.label: Common Carrier
candidates.definition: A common carrier is an individual or business that advertises to the public to transport people, offer goods, or provide services to the public. Common carriers are legally obligated to provide services to any paying customer without discrimination.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Law
candidates.definition: Law covering all the aspects of registering and titling motor vehicles, and licensing drivers.

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Asset Forfeiture Law
candidates.definition: Asset forfeiture or asset seizure is a form of confiscation of assets by the state.
synonyms: Gesetz über die Einziehung von Vermögenswerten
Loi sur la confiscation des avoirs

candidates.label: Criminal Law
candidates.definition: Law concerned with the punishment of individuals who commit crimes.

candidates.label: Transportation Law
candidates.definition: Law involving transportation infrastructure and all forms of road, railway, water and air transport.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Asset
candidates.definition: A Motor Vehicle is a self-propelled vehicle that runs on the surface and not on rails, primarily used for the transportation of people or goods. Types include cars, trucks, motorcycles, and buses, and they are usually powered by internal combustion engines or electric motors. This item is relevant to several areas of law (e.g., Bankruptcy).

candidates.label: Van (Motor Vehicle)
candidates.definition: A van is a motor vehicle typically larger than a car, designed to transport passengers or cargo. Vans come in various sizes and configurations and are commonly used for transporting goods, people, or both, depending on their purpose and design.

candidates.label: Sport Utility Vehicle
candidates.definition: A Sport Utility Vehicle (SUV) is a type of motor vehicle designed to combine the capabilities of a passenger car with features typically found in off-road vehicles. SUVs are characterized by their larger size, higher ground clearance, and often, four-wheel-drive or all-wheel-drive systems.

candidates.label: Off-Road Vehicle
candidates.definition: An off-road vehicle is a type of motorized vehicle specifically designed for use on rough and unpaved terrain. Examples of off-road vehicles include All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs), commonly known as four-wheelers, and dune buggies.

candidates.label: Recreational Vehicle
candidates.definition: A recreational vehicle (RV) is a motorized or towable vehicle equipped with living accommodations, typically including sleeping, cooking, and bathroom facilities. RVs are used for travel, camping, and recreation, providing a mobile home away from home for individuals and families.

candidates.label: Autonomous Vehicle
candidates.definition: An autonomous or connected vehicle, also known as a self-driving or driverless car, is a vehicle equipped with technology that allows it to navigate and operate with varying levels of human intervention (Level 1 through Level 4), or no human intervention (Level 5). Such vehicles rely on sensors, cameras, and artificial intelligence to perceive their environment and make decisions based on pre-programmed algorithms and real-time data.

candidates.label: Motorcycle
candidates.definition: A motorcycle is a two-wheeled motorized vehicle typically powered by an internal combustion engine. Motorcycles are designed for personal transportation and come in various styles, including sport bikes, cruisers, touring bikes, and off-road motorcycles.

candidates.label: Investment Funds
candidates.definition: Investment Funds are pooled investment vehicles that collect capital from multiple investors to invest in a diversified portfolio of assets. These funds include various types such as mutual funds, hedge funds, and real estate investment trusts (REITs), each with distinct investment strategies and regulatory structures.

candidates.label: Car
candidates.definition: A car is a motorized vehicle designed for the transportation of passengers or cargo. It comes in various body styles, including sedan, coupe, convertible, and hatchback, each with distinct features and configurations.

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Driver's License
candidates.definition: A driver's license is a legal document issued by governmental authorities that authorizes an individual to operate a motor vehicle on public roads. The license is typically granted after the individual has passed both written and practical driving tests, and it may come with restrictions based on age, type of vehicle, or other factors.

candidates.label: Automobile Registration
candidates.definition: "Automobile Registration" refers to the official process of recording essential information about an automobile, such as its make, model, and ownership, with a government authority. This registration, often represented by a certificate or license plate, is required for the vehicle to be legally driven on public roads.

candidates.label: Automobile Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: An automobile insurance policy is a contract between a policyholder and an insurance company that provides financial protection against losses arising from accidents, theft, or other events affecting a motor vehicle. The policy specifies the types of coverage included, such as liability, collision, and comprehensive, and requires the payment of premiums to maintain this coverage.

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: Personal Information
candidates.definition: Personal Information refers to any data that relates to an identified or identifiable individual. This includes both sensitive personal information, which requires greater protection due to its potential to cause harm if disclosed, and personally identifiable information (PII), which can be used to identify, contact, or locate a specific individual.

candidates.label: Sensitive Personal Information
candidates.definition: Sensitive Personal Information refers to personal data that requires greater protection due to its nature and the potential harm that could result from its unauthorized disclosure. This includes data such as health records, financial information, racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, and biometric data.

candidates.label: Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction
candidates.definition: A Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is a legal request made by a defendant to dismiss a case on the grounds that the court does not have the authority to hear the case due to the defendant not having sufficient contacts with the jurisdiction.
synonyms: Antrag auf Abweisung wegen fehlender persönlicher Zuständigkeit
Moción de Desestimación por Falta de Jurisdicción Personal
Moción de Inadmisión por Falta de Jurisdicción Personal
Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction
Pedido de arquivamento por falta de jurisdição pessoal
Requête en rejet pour absence de juridiction personnelle
תביעה לדחיית התביעה עקב חוסר סמכות אישית
व्यक्तिगत अधिकरण की कमी के कारण खारिजी के लिए आवेदन
個人的管轄権の欠如による却下の動議
因缺乏个人管辖权而驳回动议

hierarchy_path.label: Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Jurisdiction
Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction

candidates.label: Transportation Agreement
candidates.definition: A Transportation Agreement is a legally binding contract between two or more parties that outlines the terms and conditions for the transportation of goods or people from one place to another.

candidates.label: Commercial Motor Vehicle Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N code identifying whether the incident occurred while the driver was operating a commercial vehicle.
synonyms: 商用車インジケーター

candidates.label: Traffic Accident Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N - True if an incident involved a traffic accident; false otherwise.

candidates.label: Criminal Matter Identifiers
candidates.definition: Criminal Matter Identifiers are unique codes or numbers used to track and identify criminal cases, including information such as law enforcement arrest tracking numbers, fatality indicators, commercial motor vehicle indicators, law enforcement incident tracking numbers, and traffic accident indicators.

candidates.label: Fatality Indicator
candidates.definition: T/F - True if an incident involves a fatality

candidates.label: Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment refers to user taxes levied on motor vehicles owned by households. These taxes are typically paid at regular intervals and may include vehicle registration fees, road use taxes, and other related charges that households must pay to legally operate their motor vehicles.
synonyms: Taxes paid by households in respect of motor vehicles

hierarchy_path.label: Recurrent Use and Permission Tax Payment

candidates.label: Non-Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Non-Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment refers to user taxes levied on motor vehicles owned by entities other than households, such as businesses, organizations, and government agencies. These taxes are typically paid at regular intervals and may include vehicle registration fees, road use taxes, and other related charges required for the legal operation of motor vehicles by non-household entities.
synonyms: Taxes paid by others in respect of motor vehicles

hierarchy_path.label: Recurrent Use and Permission Tax Payment

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Capital Goods Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Capital Goods Tax Payment, also known as Taxes on Investment Goods, refers to taxes levied on investment goods such as machinery. These taxes can be imposed for an extended period or temporarily for counter-cyclical purposes. Unlike taxes on industrial inputs that are also levied on consumers, capital goods taxes specifically target goods used for investment purposes in industries and businesses.

hierarchy_path.label: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment

candidates.label: Goods and Services Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Goods and Services Tax Payment encompasses all taxes and duties levied on the production, extraction, sale, transfer, leasing, or delivery of goods, as well as the rendering of services. This also includes taxes on the use of goods or permissions to use goods or perform activities, ensuring comprehensive taxation of economic activities related to goods and services.
synonyms: Payment of Tax on Goods and Services

candidates.label: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment includes excises, profits generated and transferred from fiscal monopolies, customs and import duties, as well as taxes on exports, foreign exchange transactions, investment goods, betting stakes, and special taxes on services. These taxes do not form part of a general consumption tax but are instead targeted at specific goods and services.
synonyms: Taxes on specific goods and services

hierarchy_path.label: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment

candidates.label: Asset Sale
candidates.definition: The concept of Asset Sale refers to the transfer of ownership of assets for a regulatory purpose (e.g., selling bankrupt or financially distressed company's assets to another party in order to raise funds and pay off creditors; e.g., to increase public benefits).

candidates.label: Criminal Lineup
candidates.definition: A criminal lineup is a procedure used by law enforcement in which a witness or victim of a crime is presented with a series of individuals, one of whom is the suspect, to determine if the witness can identify the perpetrator. The lineup can be live, with individuals standing in person, or photo-based, using pictures of potential suspects. Proper protocols are essential to ensure fairness and avoid unintentional suggestion or bias.

candidates.label: Criminal Proceedings
candidates.definition: Criminal Proceedings refer to the legal process of prosecuting and punishing individuals who have been accused of committing a crime, including all related proceedings such as indictment, grand jury, sentencing, and parole.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: Asset Appreciation
candidates.definition: Asset Appreciation is the increase in the value of an asset over time. This growth in worth can occur due to various factors such as market demand, scarcity, improvements, or economic conditions, and represents a gain in the asset's potential sale price or intrinsic value.

candidates.label: Asset Depreciation
candidates.definition: Asset Depreciation is the systematic decrease in the recorded value of an asset over time, typically due to wear and tear, obsolescence, or the passage of time. This accounting method allocates the cost of tangible assets over their useful life, reflecting the reduction in the asset's economic value.

candidates.label: Copyright Claims Board
candidates.definition: A Copyright Claims Board (CCB) is a specialized administrative body within the United States Copyright Office that is responsible for resolving disputes related to copyright claims.

candidates.definition: The United States Transportation Command (USTRANSCOM) is one of ten unified commands of the United States Department of Defense.
synonyms: Comando de Transporte de EE. UU.
Comando de Transporte de los Estados Unidos
アメリカ合衆国輸送軍団

candidates.label: Surface Transportation Board
candidates.definition: The Surface Transportation Board (STB) of the United States is a federal, bipartisan, independent adjudicatory board. The STB was established in 1996 to assume some of the regulatory functions that had been administered by the Interstate Commerce Commission when the ICC was abolished.

candidates.label: U.S. Dept. of Transportation
candidates.definition: The United States Department of Transportation (USDOT or DOT) is a federal Cabinet department of the U.S. government concerned with transportation.
synonyms: Departamento de Transporte de EE. UU.
Departamento de Transporte de los Estados Unidos
アメリカ合衆国運輸省

candidates.label: Bureau of Transportation Statistics
candidates.definition: The Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS), as part of the United States Department of Transportation, compiles, analyzes, and makes accessible information on the nation's transportation systems.
synonyms: Bureau de la statistique des transports

candidates.label: National Transportation Safety Board
candidates.definition: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) is an independent U.S. government investigative agency responsible for civil transportation accident investigation.
synonyms: Conseil national de la sécurité des transports
Conselho Nacional de Segurança nos Transportes
Junta Nacional de Seguridad del Transporte
Junta Nacional de Seguridad en el Transporte

candidates.label: Transportation Security Administration
candidates.definition: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) is an agency of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security that has authority over the security of the traveling public in the United States.
synonyms: Administración de Seguridad en el Transporte
Administration de la sécurité des transports

candidates.label: Bureau of Industry and Security
candidates.definition: The Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) is an agency of the United States Department of Commerce that deals with issues involving national security and high technology.
synonyms: Bureau de l'industrie et de la sécurité

candidates.label: Defense Criminal Investigative Service
candidates.definition: The Defense Criminal Investigative Service is the criminal investigative arm of the Office of the Inspector General, U.S. Department of Defense.
synonyms: Service d'enquête criminelle de la défense
Servicio de Investigación Criminal de Defensa
Serviço de Investigação Criminal de Defesa

candidates.label: Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration
candidates.definition: The Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) is an agency in the United States Department of Transportation that regulates the trucking industry in the United States.

Administración Federal de Seguridad de Transporte de Mercancías

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) manufacturing complete automobiles, light duty motor vehicles, and heavy duty trucks (i.e., body and chassis or unibody) or (2) manufacturing motor vehicle chassis only.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) manufacturing complete automobiles, light duty motor vehicles, and heavy duty trucks (i.e., body and chassis or unibody) or (2) manufacturing motor vehicle chassis only.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Towing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in towing light or heavy motor vehicles, both local and long-distance. These establishments may provide incidental services, such as storage and emergency road repair services.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Towing
candidates.definition: See industry description for 488410.

candidates.label: Other Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing and/or rebuilding motor vehicle parts and accessories (except motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, motor vehicle steering and suspension components, motor vehicle brake systems, motor vehicle transmissions and power train parts, motor vehicle seating and interior trim, and motor vehicle stampings).

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Metal Stamping
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle stampings, such as fenders, tops, body parts, trim, and molding.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, motor vehicle steering and suspension components (except springs), motor vehicle brake systems, motor vehicle transmission and power train parts, motor vehicle seating and interior trim, motor vehicle metal stampings, and other motor vehicle parts and accessories. This industry group includes establishments that rebuild motor vehicle parts.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Body Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing truck and bus bodies and cabs and automobile bodies. The products made may be sold separately or may be assembled on purchased chassis and sold as complete vehicles.

hierarchy_path.label: Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing
Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Brake System Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing and/or rebuilding motor vehicle brake systems and related components.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle and Parts Dealers Industry
candidates.definition:
Industries in the Motor Vehicle and Parts Dealers subsector retail motor vehicles and parts.  Establishments in this subsector often operate from a showroom and/or an open lot where the vehicles are on display.  The display of vehicles and the related parts require little by way of display equipment.  The personnel generally include both the sales and sales support staff familiar with the requirements for registering and financing a vehicle as well as a staff of parts experts and mechanics trained to provide repair and maintenance services for the vehicles.  Specific industries included in this subsector identify the type of vehicle being retailed.

Sales of capital or durable nonconsumer goods, such as medium- and heavy-duty trucks, are always included in wholesale trade.
synonyms: Industria de Concesionarios de Vehículos y Piezas
Industria de Distribuidores de Vehículos y Partes
Industrie des concessionnaires de véhicules automobiles et de pièces détachées
Indústria de revendedores de veículos automotores e peças
