candidates.label: Karelian
candidates.definition: Karelian (Language) is a specific written or spoken structured system of communication that consists of grammar and vocabulary, and is a member of the Language top-level class.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing criminal actions.

candidates.label: Motor Neuron Disorder
candidates.definition: Motor neuron disorders are a group of progressive neurological diseases that affect the motor neurons, which are the nerve cells responsible for controlling voluntary muscles. These disorders lead to muscle weakness, atrophy, and eventual paralysis as the motor neurons degenerate and die.

candidates.label: Tic Disorders
candidates.definition: Tic Disorders involve the presence of repetitive, involuntary movements or vocalizations. They may include simple motor tics, such as blinking or head jerking, and complex tics, such as jumping or uttering words or phrases.

candidates.label: Musculoskeletal Disorders
candidates.definition: A Mobility Disability is a condition that impairs an individual's ability to move freely and perform physical activities, often due to issues with the musculoskeletal or nervous systems. This type of disability can range from partial to complete loss of movement in various parts of the body and may require the use of assistive devices such as wheelchairs, crutches, or prosthetics.

candidates.label: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
candidates.definition: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects motor neurons in the brain and spinal cord, leading to muscle weakness, atrophy, and eventually paralysis. This condition results in the loss of voluntary muscle control and can impact speaking, swallowing, and breathing.

candidates.label: Stroke
candidates.definition: A vascular insult to the brain, commonly referred to as a stroke or cerebrovascular accident, occurs when blood flow to a part of the brain is disrupted, leading to tissue damage and neurological deficits. This condition can result from either ischemia (lack of blood flow) or hemorrhage (bleeding) within the brain.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Neurological Disorders
candidates.definition: Neurological disorders encompass a broad range of conditions that affect the nervous system, including the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves. These disorders can result in a variety of symptoms, such as muscle weakness, coordination issues, seizures, and cognitive impairments, requiring tailored medical and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Neurodevelopmental Disorders
candidates.definition: Neurodevelopmental Disorders are characterized by onset during the developmental period, that is, during childhood or adolescence, although sometimes they are not diagnosed until adulthood. Symptoms and signs may include, but are not limited to, underlying abnormalities in cognitive processing (for example, deficits in learning and applying verbal or nonverbal information, visual perception, memory, or a combination of these); deficits in attention or impulse control; low frustration tolerance; excessive or poorly planned motor activity; difficulty with organizing (time, space, materials, or tasks); repeated accidental injury; and deficits in social skills. Symptoms and signs specific to tic disorders include sudden, rapid, recurrent, non-rhythmic, motor movement or vocalization.

candidates.label: Physical Disability
candidates.definition: Physical Disability pertains to impairments in bodily function or structure that limit an individual's mobility or physical capacity. These disabilities can be congenital, acquired through injury, or result from disease, impacting daily activities and often requiring adaptive tools or accommodations.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Negligence
candidates.definition: Motor Vehicle Negligence refers to a legal claim for damages resulting from the careless or reckless operation of a motor vehicle, which causes injury or harm to another person.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Theft
candidates.definition: The theft of a motor vehicle.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Dealership Liability
candidates.definition: Motor Vehicle Dealership Liability refers to the legal responsibility of a car dealership for any harm or damages caused to a customer or third party as a result of the dealership's actions or negligence in the sale or maintenance of a vehicle.
synonyms: Responsabilidad del Concesionario de Vehículos Motorizados
Responsabilidad del Distribuidor de Vehículos Motorizados
自動車ディーラーの責任

candidates.label: Theft from Motor Vehicle
candidates.definition: The theft of articles from a motor vehicle, whether locked or unlocked.
synonyms: Vol dans un véhicule à moteur

candidates.label: Theft of Motor Vehicle Parts/Accessories
candidates.definition: The theft of any part or accessory affixed to the interior or exterior of a motor vehicle in a manner which would make the item an attachment of the vehicle, or necessary for its operation
synonyms: Robo de partes/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
Robo de piezas/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
Roubo de peças/acessórios de veículos automotores
Vol de pièces/accessoires de véhicules à moteur

机动车零部件/配件盗窃
自動車部品/アクセサリーの盗難

candidates.label: Theft of Motor Vehicle Parts/Accessories
candidates.definition: Theft of Motor Vehicle Parts/Accessories: The act of taking or removing parts or accessories from a motor vehicle without the owner's consent, with the intention of permanently depriving the owner of their property.
synonyms: Robo de partes/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
Robo de piezas/accesorios de vehículos motorizados

candidates.label: Underinsured Motorist Claims
candidates.definition: A claim made by an insured individual against their own insurance policy when the at-fault driver in an accident does not have enough insurance coverage to fully compensate for the damages.
synonyms: Reclamaciones de automovilistas con seguro insuficiente
Reclamações de motoristas com seguro insuficiente
保険金不足のモーターリストクレーム
未投保或保险不足的机动车驾驶员索赔

candidates.label: Vehicular Manslaughter
candidates.definition: The unlawful killing of a human being caused by the operation of a motor vehicle by a person who acted with gross negligence or recklessness.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Personal Injury Claims are civil claims or tort claims seeking compensation for physical or emotional harm caused by the negligence or intentional actions of another party, including but not limited to slip-and-fall accidents, medical malpractice, and motor vehicle accidents.

candidates.label: Litigation Claims
candidates.definition: Demand or assertion seeking money, property, or enforcement of another right as provided by law.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Asset Analysis
candidates.definition: Asset Analysis is the process of evaluating and examining the value and condition of assets in the context of a regulatory, bankruptcy or financial restructuring matter, including asset valuation, due diligence, investigation, turnover, recovery, and financial advisory practices.

candidates.label: Asset Sales Practice
candidates.definition: Asset Sales Practice refers to the legal practice area that deals with the sale of assets, such as property or businesses, typically in the context of bankruptcy or restructuring proceedings.
synonyms: 資産売却プラクティス

candidates.label: Asset Valuation Practice
candidates.definition: Asset Valuation Practice refers to the specialized area of Asset Analysis that involves determining the monetary value of a particular asset or group of assets.
synonyms: 資産評価プラクティス

candidates.label: Criminal Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A criminal lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a criminal court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Driver
candidates.definition: A driver is an individual who operates a motor vehicle, controlling its direction and speed on public or private roads. This role requires adherence to traffic laws and regulations to ensure safety and order on thoroughfares.

candidates.label: Motorcyclist
candidates.definition: A motorcycle driver is an individual who operates a motorcycle, controlling its mechanisms to navigate roads and other environments. This role entails a distinct set of skills and adherence to specific traffic laws to ensure the safety of the driver and other road users.

candidates.label: Single Asset Real Estate Debtor
candidates.definition: A Single Asset Real Estate Debtor (SARE Debtor) refers to a specific classification in bankruptcy proceedings for debtors whose primary business involves a single real estate property. This classification impacts how bankruptcy cases are handled, particularly by expediting the process and applying unique rules concerning automatic stays and reorganization plans.

candidates.label: Governmental Criminal Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Criminal Law Division is a government entity responsible for prosecuting criminal cases on behalf of the public. It conducts investigations, files charges, represents the government in criminal trials, and ensures the enforcement of criminal laws.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Role in Matter
candidates.definition: An individual or entity's role, responsibility, or function in executing the legal matter.

candidates.label: Common Carrier
candidates.definition: A common carrier is an individual or business that advertises to the public to transport people, offer goods, or provide services to the public. Common carriers are legally obligated to provide services to any paying customer without discrimination.

candidates.label: Regulatory Applicant
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Applicant is an individual or entity that applies for authorization, licensing, or compliance approval from a regulatory authority. This role relates to sectors where operations are subject to legal and regulatory standards, ensuring adherence to established laws, guidelines, and norms.

candidates.label: Regulatory Petitioner
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Petitioner refers to an individual or entity formally requesting legal or official approval from a regulatory body, often through a structured application process. This encompasses a wide array of requests, from immigration status changes to the issuance of permits for business operations or environmental actions.

candidates.label: Governmental Regulatory Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Regulatory Law Division is a governmental entity responsible for creating, implementing, and enforcing regulations. It ensures compliance with laws and regulations, provides guidance to regulatory agencies, and represents the government in regulatory matters

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Law
candidates.definition: Law covering all the aspects of registering and titling motor vehicles, and licensing drivers.

candidates.label: Transportation Law
candidates.definition: Law involving transportation infrastructure and all forms of road, railway, water and air transport.

candidates.label: Asset Forfeiture Law
candidates.definition: Asset forfeiture or asset seizure is a form of confiscation of assets by the state.
synonyms: Gesetz über die Einziehung von Vermögenswerten
Loi sur la confiscation des avoirs

candidates.label: Criminal Law
candidates.definition: Law concerned with the punishment of individuals who commit crimes.

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Mass Torts Law
candidates.definition: Mass torts law is an area of law that deals with legal actions involving numerous plaintiffs against one or a few defendants, where the plaintiffs allege harm caused by common actions or products. These cases often involve complex negotiations and litigation due to the large number of claimants, extensive evidence, and significant legal and financial stakes.

candidates.label: Fraud and Economic Torts Law
candidates.definition: The wrong done by another person or business that occurs in, or affects, commerce.
synonyms: Droit de la fraude et des délits économiques
Lei de Fraude e Delitos Econômicos
Ley de Fraude y Agravios Económicos
Ley de Fraude y Delitos Económicos
詐欺および経済侵害法

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Asset
candidates.definition: A Motor Vehicle is a self-propelled vehicle that runs on the surface and not on rails, primarily used for the transportation of people or goods. Types include cars, trucks, motorcycles, and buses, and they are usually powered by internal combustion engines or electric motors. This item is relevant to several areas of law (e.g., Bankruptcy).

candidates.label: Van (Motor Vehicle)
candidates.definition: A van is a motor vehicle typically larger than a car, designed to transport passengers or cargo. Vans come in various sizes and configurations and are commonly used for transporting goods, people, or both, depending on their purpose and design.

candidates.label: Recreational Vehicle
candidates.definition: A recreational vehicle (RV) is a motorized or towable vehicle equipped with living accommodations, typically including sleeping, cooking, and bathroom facilities. RVs are used for travel, camping, and recreation, providing a mobile home away from home for individuals and families.

candidates.label: Autonomous Vehicle
candidates.definition: An autonomous or connected vehicle, also known as a self-driving or driverless car, is a vehicle equipped with technology that allows it to navigate and operate with varying levels of human intervention (Level 1 through Level 4), or no human intervention (Level 5). Such vehicles rely on sensors, cameras, and artificial intelligence to perceive their environment and make decisions based on pre-programmed algorithms and real-time data.

candidates.label: Motorcycle
candidates.definition: A motorcycle is a two-wheeled motorized vehicle typically powered by an internal combustion engine. Motorcycles are designed for personal transportation and come in various styles, including sport bikes, cruisers, touring bikes, and off-road motorcycles.

candidates.label: Investment Funds
candidates.definition: Investment Funds are pooled investment vehicles that collect capital from multiple investors to invest in a diversified portfolio of assets. These funds include various types such as mutual funds, hedge funds, and real estate investment trusts (REITs), each with distinct investment strategies and regulatory structures.

candidates.label: Sport Utility Vehicle
candidates.definition: A Sport Utility Vehicle (SUV) is a type of motor vehicle designed to combine the capabilities of a passenger car with features typically found in off-road vehicles. SUVs are characterized by their larger size, higher ground clearance, and often, four-wheel-drive or all-wheel-drive systems.

candidates.label: Off-Road Vehicle
candidates.definition: An off-road vehicle is a type of motorized vehicle specifically designed for use on rough and unpaved terrain. Examples of off-road vehicles include All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs), commonly known as four-wheelers, and dune buggies.

candidates.label: Car
candidates.definition: A car is a motorized vehicle designed for the transportation of passengers or cargo. It comes in various body styles, including sedan, coupe, convertible, and hatchback, each with distinct features and configurations.

candidates.label: Goods
candidates.definition: Goods: Tangible or intangible items that are capable of being owned or possessed and that have value or usefulness to their owner.

candidates.label: Driver's License
candidates.definition: A driver's license is a legal document issued by governmental authorities that authorizes an individual to operate a motor vehicle on public roads. The license is typically granted after the individual has passed both written and practical driving tests, and it may come with restrictions based on age, type of vehicle, or other factors.

candidates.label: Automobile Registration
candidates.definition: "Automobile Registration" refers to the official process of recording essential information about an automobile, such as its make, model, and ownership, with a government authority. This registration, often represented by a certificate or license plate, is required for the vehicle to be legally driven on public roads.

candidates.label: Transportation Agreement
candidates.definition: A Transportation Agreement is a legally binding contract between two or more parties that outlines the terms and conditions for the transportation of goods or people from one place to another.

candidates.label: Licenses and Permits
candidates.definition: A license is a legal authorization granted by a governing body or entity, permitting an individual or organization to engage in certain activities, such as operating a business or practicing a profession. Licenses are generally subject to regulations and can be revoked if the licensee fails to comply with specified conditions.

candidates.label: Equipment Lease
candidates.definition: An equipment lease is a contractual agreement in which one party (the lessor) grants another party (the lessee) the right to use and possess specific equipment for a predetermined period in exchange for periodic rental payments. These agreements often include terms related to maintenance, insurance, and the lessee's responsibilities during the lease term.

candidates.label: Equipment Agreements
candidates.definition: An Equipment Related Agreement is a legally binding contract that governs the use, rental, purchase, or maintenance of specific equipment or machinery. These agreements detail terms such as ownership, responsibilities for maintenance and repair, payment arrangements, and conditions for returning or replacing the equipment.

candidates.label: Equipment Purchase Agreement
candidates.definition: An Equipment Purchase Agreement is a legally binding contract between a buyer and a seller outlining the terms and conditions of the sale of specific equipment or machinery. It typically includes details such as the purchase price, payment terms, warranties, delivery arrangements, and any conditions or representations related to the equipment being sold.

candidates.label: Motion to Avoid Lien on Household Goods
candidates.definition: A Motion to Avoid Lien on Household Goods is a legal request to remove a lien on personal property that is essential for daily living, such as furniture and appliances.
synonyms: Antrag auf Vermeidung einer Pfandrechtsbelastung von Haushaltsgegenständen
Moción para evitar un gravamen sobre bienes domésticos
Motion pour éviter une hypothèque sur les biens ménagers
Motion to Avoid Lien on Household Goods
Petição para evitar ônus sobre bens domésticos
בקשה להימנע ממעטפה על רכוש ביתי
घरेलू सामान पर लिएन से बचने के लिए याचिका
家庭用品に対する抵当権回避申立
避免对家庭用品设定留置权动议

hierarchy_path.label: Motion to Avoid Lien on Household Goods

candidates.label: Criminal Record
candidates.definition: A criminal record is an official compilation of an individual's past arrests, charges, convictions, and other interactions with the criminal justice system. This record can have implications for employment, housing, and other opportunities, as many entities conduct background checks. In some jurisdictions, certain convictions can be expunged or sealed, removing them from public view.

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Commercial Motor Vehicle Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N code identifying whether the incident occurred while the driver was operating a commercial vehicle.
synonyms: 商用車インジケーター

candidates.label: Criminal Matter Identifiers
candidates.definition: Criminal Matter Identifiers are unique codes or numbers used to track and identify criminal cases, including information such as law enforcement arrest tracking numbers, fatality indicators, commercial motor vehicle indicators, law enforcement incident tracking numbers, and traffic accident indicators.

candidates.label: Regulatory Statement
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Statement is a type of Invoice Data that contains information related to legal regulations and requirements.

candidates.label: Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment refers to user taxes levied on motor vehicles owned by households. These taxes are typically paid at regular intervals and may include vehicle registration fees, road use taxes, and other related charges that households must pay to legally operate their motor vehicles.
synonyms: Taxes paid by households in respect of motor vehicles

hierarchy_path.label: Recurrent Use and Permission Tax Payment

candidates.label: Non-Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Non-Household Motor Vehicle Tax Payment refers to user taxes levied on motor vehicles owned by entities other than households, such as businesses, organizations, and government agencies. These taxes are typically paid at regular intervals and may include vehicle registration fees, road use taxes, and other related charges required for the legal operation of motor vehicles by non-household entities.
synonyms: Taxes paid by others in respect of motor vehicles

hierarchy_path.label: Recurrent Use and Permission Tax Payment

candidates.label: Capital Goods Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Capital Goods Tax Payment, also known as Taxes on Investment Goods, refers to taxes levied on investment goods such as machinery. These taxes can be imposed for an extended period or temporarily for counter-cyclical purposes. Unlike taxes on industrial inputs that are also levied on consumers, capital goods taxes specifically target goods used for investment purposes in industries and businesses.

hierarchy_path.label: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment

candidates.label: Goods and Services Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Goods and Services Tax Payment encompasses all taxes and duties levied on the production, extraction, sale, transfer, leasing, or delivery of goods, as well as the rendering of services. This also includes taxes on the use of goods or permissions to use goods or perform activities, ensuring comprehensive taxation of economic activities related to goods and services.
synonyms: Payment of Tax on Goods and Services

candidates.label: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment includes excises, profits generated and transferred from fiscal monopolies, customs and import duties, as well as taxes on exports, foreign exchange transactions, investment goods, betting stakes, and special taxes on services. These taxes do not form part of a general consumption tax but are instead targeted at specific goods and services.
synonyms: Taxes on specific goods and services

hierarchy_path.label: Specific Goods and Services Tax Payment

candidates.label: Asset Sale
candidates.definition: The concept of Asset Sale refers to the transfer of ownership of assets for a regulatory purpose (e.g., selling bankrupt or financially distressed company's assets to another party in order to raise funds and pay off creditors; e.g., to increase public benefits).

candidates.label: Criminal Lineup
candidates.definition: A criminal lineup is a procedure used by law enforcement in which a witness or victim of a crime is presented with a series of individuals, one of whom is the suspect, to determine if the witness can identify the perpetrator. The lineup can be live, with individuals standing in person, or photo-based, using pictures of potential suspects. Proper protocols are essential to ensure fairness and avoid unintentional suggestion or bias.

candidates.label: Criminal Proceedings
candidates.definition: Criminal Proceedings refer to the legal process of prosecuting and punishing individuals who have been accused of committing a crime, including all related proceedings such as indictment, grand jury, sentencing, and parole.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Asset Appreciation
candidates.definition: Asset Appreciation is the increase in the value of an asset over time. This growth in worth can occur due to various factors such as market demand, scarcity, improvements, or economic conditions, and represents a gain in the asset's potential sale price or intrinsic value.

candidates.label: Asset Depreciation
candidates.definition: Asset Depreciation is the systematic decrease in the recorded value of an asset over time, typically due to wear and tear, obsolescence, or the passage of time. This accounting method allocates the cost of tangible assets over their useful life, reflecting the reduction in the asset's economic value.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: New York Criminal Court
candidates.definition: The New York Criminal Court is a court within the New York State Courts system that handles criminal cases.

candidates.label: New York City - Criminal Court
candidates.definition: N.Y. City Crim. Ct.

candidates.definition: Tex. Crim. App.

candidates.definition: Okla. Crim. App.

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Claims
candidates.definition: Ct. Cl.

candidates.label: U.S. Transportation Command
candidates.definition: The United States Transportation Command (USTRANSCOM) is one of ten unified commands of the United States Department of Defense.
synonyms: Comando de Transporte de EE. UU.
Comando de Transporte de los Estados Unidos
アメリカ合衆国輸送軍団

candidates.label: Surface Transportation Board
candidates.definition: The Surface Transportation Board (STB) of the United States is a federal, bipartisan, independent adjudicatory board. The STB was established in 1996 to assume some of the regulatory functions that had been administered by the Interstate Commerce Commission when the ICC was abolished.

candidates.label: U.S. Dept. of Transportation
candidates.definition: The United States Department of Transportation (USDOT or DOT) is a federal Cabinet department of the U.S. government concerned with transportation.
synonyms: Departamento de Transporte de EE. UU.
Departamento de Transporte de los Estados Unidos
アメリカ合衆国運輸省

candidates.label: Bureau of Transportation Statistics
candidates.definition: The Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS), as part of the United States Department of Transportation, compiles, analyzes, and makes accessible information on the nation's transportation systems.
synonyms: Bureau de la statistique des transports

candidates.label: National Transportation Safety Board
candidates.definition: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) is an independent U.S. government investigative agency responsible for civil transportation accident investigation.
synonyms: Conseil national de la sécurité des transports
Conselho Nacional de Segurança nos Transportes
Junta Nacional de Seguridad del Transporte
Junta Nacional de Seguridad en el Transporte

candidates.label: Transportation Security Administration
candidates.definition: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) is an agency of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security that has authority over the security of the traveling public in the United States.
synonyms: Administración de Seguridad en el Transporte
Administration de la sécurité des transports

candidates.label: Bureau of Industry and Security
candidates.definition: The Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) is an agency of the United States Department of Commerce that deals with issues involving national security and high technology.
synonyms: Bureau de l'industrie et de la sécurité

candidates.label: Defense Criminal Investigative Service
candidates.definition: The Defense Criminal Investigative Service is the criminal investigative arm of the Office of the Inspector General, U.S. Department of Defense.
synonyms: Service d'enquête criminelle de la défense
Servicio de Investigación Criminal de Defensa
Serviço de Investigação Criminal de Defesa

candidates.label: Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration
candidates.definition: The Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) is an agency in the United States Department of Transportation that regulates the trucking industry in the United States.
synonyms: Administración Federal de Seguridad de Autotransporte de Mercancías
Administración Federal de Seguridad de Transporte de Mercancías
Administration fédérale de la sécurité des transporteurs routiers
Administração Federal de Segurança de Transportadores Rodoviários
Bundesbehörde für die Sicherheit im Straßenverkehr
联邦机动车运输安全管理局
連邦自動車運送安全局

candidates.label: U.S. Postal Regulatory Commission
candidates.definition: The United States Postal Regulatory Commission (or PRC), formerly called the Postal Rate Commission, is an independent regulatory agency created by the Postal Reorganization Act of 1970.
synonyms: Comisión Reguladora Postal de EE. UU.
Commission de réglementation postale des États-Unis

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) manufacturing complete automobiles, light duty motor vehicles, and heavy duty trucks (i.e., body and chassis or unibody) or (2) manufacturing motor vehicle chassis only.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) manufacturing complete automobiles, light duty motor vehicles, and heavy duty trucks (i.e., body and chassis or unibody) or (2) manufacturing motor vehicle chassis only.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Towing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in towing light or heavy motor vehicles, both local and long-distance. These establishments may provide incidental services, such as storage and emergency road repair services.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Towing
candidates.definition: See industry description for 488410.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Metal Stamping
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle stampings, such as fenders, tops, body parts, trim, and molding.

candidates.label: Other Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing and/or rebuilding motor vehicle parts and accessories (except motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, motor vehicle steering and suspension components, motor vehicle brake systems, motor vehicle transmissions and power train parts, motor vehicle seating and interior trim, and motor vehicle stampings).

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, motor vehicle steering and suspension components (except springs), motor vehicle brake systems, motor vehicle transmission and power train parts, motor vehicle seating and interior trim, motor vehicle metal stampings, and other motor vehicle parts and accessories. This industry group includes establishments that rebuild motor vehicle parts.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Body Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing truck and bus bodies and cabs and automobile bodies. The products made may be sold separately or may be assembled on purchased chassis and sold as complete vehicles.

hierarchy_path.label: Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing
Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Brake System Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing and/or rebuilding motor vehicle brake systems and related components.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) manufacturing motor vehicle bodies and cabs or (2) manufacturing truck, automobile and utility trailers, truck trailer chassis, detachable trailer bodies, and detachable trailer chassis.  The products made may be sold separately or may be assembled on purchased chassis and sold as complete vehicles.  Motor homes are units where the motor and the living quarters are contained in the same integrated unit, while travel trailers are designed to be towed by a motor unit, such as an automobile or a light truck.

hierarchy_path.label: Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing
Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing

candidates.label: Karelian
candidates.definition: Karelian (Language) is a specific written or spoken structured system of communication that consists of grammar and vocabulary, and is a member of the Language top-level class.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing criminal actions.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Tic Disorders
candidates.definition: Tic Disorders involve the presence of repetitive, involuntary movements or vocalizations. They may include simple motor tics, such as blinking or head jerking, and complex tics, such as jumping or uttering words or phrases.

candidates.label: Musculoskeletal Disorders
candidates.definition: A Mobility Disability is a condition that impairs an individual's ability to move freely and perform physical activities, often due to issues with the musculoskeletal or nervous systems. This type of disability can range from partial to complete loss of movement in various parts of the body and may require the use of assistive devices such as wheelchairs, crutches, or prosthetics.

candidates.label: Motor Neuron Disorder
candidates.definition: Motor neuron disorders are a group of progressive neurological diseases that affect the motor neurons, which are the nerve cells responsible for controlling voluntary muscles. These disorders lead to muscle weakness, atrophy, and eventual paralysis as the motor neurons degenerate and die.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Neurodevelopmental Disorders
candidates.definition: Neurodevelopmental Disorders are characterized by onset during the developmental period, that is, during childhood or adolescence, although sometimes they are not diagnosed until adulthood. Symptoms and signs may include, but are not limited to, underlying abnormalities in cognitive processing (for example, deficits in learning and applying verbal or nonverbal information, visual perception, memory, or a combination of these); deficits in attention or impulse control; low frustration tolerance; excessive or poorly planned motor activity; difficulty with organizing (time, space, materials, or tasks); repeated accidental injury; and deficits in social skills. Symptoms and signs specific to tic disorders include sudden, rapid, recurrent, non-rhythmic, motor movement or vocalization.

candidates.label: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis
candidates.definition: Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that affects motor neurons in the brain and spinal cord, leading to muscle weakness, atrophy, and eventually paralysis. This condition results in the loss of voluntary muscle control and can impact speaking, swallowing, and breathing.

candidates.label: Physical Disability
candidates.definition: Physical Disability pertains to impairments in bodily function or structure that limit an individual's mobility or physical capacity. These disabilities can be congenital, acquired through injury, or result from disease, impacting daily activities and often requiring adaptive tools or accommodations.

candidates.label: Conversion Disorder
candidates.definition: Conversion Disorder involves the presence of one or more symptoms of altered motor or sensory function that are inconsistent with or cannot be fully explained by medical or neurological conditions. The symptoms cause significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.

candidates.label: Neurological Disorders
candidates.definition: Neurological disorders encompass a broad range of conditions that affect the nervous system, including the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves. These disorders can result in a variety of symptoms, such as muscle weakness, coordination issues, seizures, and cognitive impairments, requiring tailored medical and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Regulatory Filing Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Filing Management refers to the process of managing and submitting required documents and information to regulatory authorities in order to comply with legal and regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Regulatory Research Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Research Management refers to the process of conducting legal research specifically related to regulations and compliance, including tracking and managing changes to regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Document Management Add-On
candidates.definition: A Document Management Add-On is a specialized tool or feature that enhances the functionality of a Document Management system. It is a software component that can be added to an existing Document Management system to provide additional capabilities or improve existing ones.

candidates.label: Document Management Implementation Services
candidates.definition: Document Management Implementation Services refers to the provision of professional assistance and guidance in the planning, execution, and maintenance of systems and processes for organizing, storing, and retrieving electronic documents within an organization.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Theft
candidates.definition: The theft of a motor vehicle.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Negligence
candidates.definition: Motor Vehicle Negligence refers to a legal claim for damages resulting from the careless or reckless operation of a motor vehicle, which causes injury or harm to another person.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Dealership Liability
candidates.definition: Motor Vehicle Dealership Liability refers to the legal responsibility of a car dealership for any harm or damages caused to a customer or third party as a result of the dealership's actions or negligence in the sale or maintenance of a vehicle.
synonyms: Responsabilidad del Concesionario de Vehículos Motorizados
Responsabilidad del Distribuidor de Vehículos Motorizados
自動車ディーラーの責任

candidates.label: Theft from Motor Vehicle
candidates.definition: The theft of articles from a motor vehicle, whether locked or unlocked.
synonyms: Vol dans un véhicule à moteur

candidates.label: Theft of Motor Vehicle Parts/Accessories
candidates.definition: The theft of any part or accessory affixed to the interior or exterior of a motor vehicle in a manner which would make the item an attachment of the vehicle, or necessary for its operation
synonyms: Robo de partes/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
Robo de piezas/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
Roubo de peças/acessórios de veículos automotores
Vol de pièces/accessoires de véhicules à moteur

机动车零部件/配件盗窃
自動車部品/アクセサリーの盗難

candidates.label: Theft of Motor Vehicle Parts/Accessories
candidates.definition: Theft of Motor Vehicle Parts/Accessories: The act of taking or removing parts or accessories from a motor vehicle without the owner's consent, with the intention of permanently depriving the owner of their property.
synonyms: Robo de partes/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
Robo de piezas/accesorios de vehículos motorizados
