candidates.label: Transportation Company Negligence
candidates.definition: Transportation Company Negligence refers to the failure of a common carrier to exercise reasonable care in the transportation of goods or passengers, resulting in harm or injury to those goods or passengers.
synonyms: Negligencia de la Compañía de Transporte

candidates.label: Transportation Accessibility Claim
candidates.definition: A claim of this type arises when transportation services, whether public or private, are not accessible to individuals with disabilities. This can range from buses not having accessible ramps to airports not providing appropriate assistance for passengers with mobility challenges.

candidates.label: Vehicular Manslaughter
candidates.definition: The unlawful killing of a human being caused by the operation of a motor vehicle by a person who acted with gross negligence or recklessness.

candidates.label: Criminal Theft
candidates.definition: The unlawful taking, carrying, leading, or riding away of property from the possession, or constructive possession, of another person.

candidates.label: Asset Analysis
candidates.definition: Asset Analysis is the process of evaluating and examining the value and condition of assets in the context of a regulatory, bankruptcy or financial restructuring matter, including asset valuation, due diligence, investigation, turnover, recovery, and financial advisory practices.

candidates.label: Asset Sales Practice
candidates.definition: Asset Sales Practice refers to the legal practice area that deals with the sale of assets, such as property or businesses, typically in the context of bankruptcy or restructuring proceedings.
synonyms: 資産売却プラクティス

candidates.label: Asset Valuation Practice
candidates.definition: Asset Valuation Practice refers to the specialized area of Asset Analysis that involves determining the monetary value of a particular asset or group of assets.
synonyms: 資産評価プラクティス

candidates.label: Criminal Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A criminal lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a criminal court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Regulatory Services (Non-Dispute)
candidates.definition: Any process that results in filing something with governmental entity.

candidates.label: Regulatory Compliance
candidates.definition: The process by which an entity (i.e., organization or person) ensures that it observes and complies with the external statutory laws and regulations.
synonyms: 規制コンプライアンス

candidates.label: Regulatory Enforcement
candidates.definition: The process of enforcement by a regulatory agency through an administrative procedure outside of any court system.

candidates.label: Regulatory Investigation
candidates.definition: A regulatory investigation is a formal inquiry conducted by a governmental or regulatory agency to examine whether laws, regulations, or industry standards have been violated. It aims to ensure compliance, enforce rules, and protect public interests in various sectors such as finance, healthcare, and environmental protection.

candidates.label: Document ID
candidates.definition: Identifier (e.g., IRIs, UUIDs, GUIDs) of the particular document stored in the systems of stakeholders (e.g., vendors, sellers, buyers, courts).

candidates.label: Patient
candidates.definition: A Patient is an individual who receives medical care, treatment, or other health services from a healthcare provider or institution. This person is under professional care due to illness, injury, or for preventive health measures, and is the recipient of medical attention, diagnosis, and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Legal Services Provider - Person
candidates.definition: A Legal Services Provider - Person is an individual who provides legal services and is associated with specific invoice data, including their provider level, timekeeper ID, first name, and last name.
synonyms: 法律サービスプロバイダー - 個人
法律服务提供者 - 个人

candidates.label: Alternative Legal Services Provider
candidates.definition: An Alternative Legal Services Provider (ALSP) is an entity that delivers legal or legal-related services outside the conventional law firm model.

candidates.label: Regulatory Applicant
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Applicant is an individual or entity that applies for authorization, licensing, or compliance approval from a regulatory authority. This role relates to sectors where operations are subject to legal and regulatory standards, ensuring adherence to established laws, guidelines, and norms.

candidates.label: Regulatory Petitioner
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Petitioner refers to an individual or entity formally requesting legal or official approval from a regulatory body, often through a structured application process. This encompasses a wide array of requests, from immigration status changes to the issuance of permits for business operations or environmental actions.

candidates.label: Governmental Regulatory Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Regulatory Law Division is a governmental entity responsible for creating, implementing, and enforcing regulations. It ensures compliance with laws and regulations, provides guidance to regulatory agencies, and represents the government in regulatory matters

candidates.label: Decedent
candidates.definition: A decedent is a legal term used to refer to a deceased person, particularly in the context of estate planning and probate law. The decedent's estate encompasses all assets, liabilities, rights, and obligations left behind, which are subject to administration and distribution according to the decedent's will or state intestacy laws.

candidates.label: Employment Title
candidates.definition: Employment Title refers to the formal designation given to a specific job position within an organization, such as "Software Engineer," "Marketing Manager," or "Legal Counsel." The title is often used to signify the roles and responsibilities of the position, and may also indicate the employee's rank or level within the organizational hierarchy

candidates.label: Roving Employment
candidates.definition: Roving Employment refers to a work arrangement where an employee does not have a fixed work location but instead moves between different sites, offices, or geographic areas as part of their job responsibilities. This setup may require extensive travel and adaptability, and is often seen in roles like sales, consulting, and field services.

candidates.label: Employment Location Type
candidates.definition: Employment Location Type describes where an employee performs their job duties and can include categories such as onsite, offsite, remote, roving, or third-party placement. These classifications help define the employee's working environment and may impact job responsibilities, oversight, and eligibility for certain benefits or allowances

candidates.label: Unemployment Benefits Law
candidates.definition: Unemployment benefits refer to those benefits given by the government to unemployed persons.
synonyms: Loi sur les allocations de chômage
חוק זכויות למען עזרה בביטוח אבטלה

candidates.label: Employee Pension and Benefits Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to retirement and welfare benefit plans, with particular emphasis on benefits arrangements.
synonyms: Droit des pensions et des avantages sociaux

candidates.label: Health Law
candidates.definition: Law, rules, regulations and other jurisprudence among providers, payers and vendors to the health care industry and its patients; and (2) delivery of health care services; all with an emphasis on operations, regulatory and transactional legal issues.

candidates.label: Workers Compensation Law
candidates.definition: Law addressing wage replacement and medical benefits to employees injured in the course of employment.
synonyms: Droit de la compensation des travailleurs

candidates.label: Labor and Employment Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to the relationship between trade individuals, unions, employers and the government.
synonyms: Droit du travail et de l'emploi

candidates.label: Employment Immigration Law
candidates.definition: National statutes, and regulations governing employment-based immigration into and deportation from a country.

candidates.label: Employment Discrimination Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to protected class employment discrimination including retaliation and harassment
synonyms: Loi sur la discrimination en matière d'emploi

candidates.label: Employment Health and Safety Law
candidates.definition: Law governing the health and safety of employees.
synonyms: Droit de la santé et de la sécurité au travail
Lei de Saúde e Segurança no Trabalho
Ley de Salud y Seguridad en el Empleo

candidates.label: Labor Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to the relationship between trade unions, employers, and the government.

candidates.label: Construction and Development Law
candidates.definition: Law dealing with matters relating to building construction, engineering, and related fields.
synonyms: Derecho de la Construcción y Desarrollo
Droit de la construction et de l'aménagement

candidates.label: Employee Benefits Motion
candidates.definition: A request made by a debtor in a bankruptcy case seeking authorization to continue providing employee benefits, such as health insurance and retirement plans, to its employees.
synonyms: Demande d'avantages sociaux pour les employés

candidates.label: Motion to Reinstate Retiree Benefits
candidates.definition: A Motion to Reinstate Retiree Benefits is a legal request made in a bankruptcy case to restore previously terminated benefits for retired individuals.
synonyms: Moción para restablecer los beneficios de los jubilados
Motion pour rétablir les avantages des retraités
Pedido de Reintegração de Benefícios de Aposentados
सेवानिवृत्त लाभों को पुनः स्थापित करने के लिए चलान
退職者の福利厚生復活請求

candidates.label: Medical Form
candidates.definition: A Medical Form is a standardized document used within healthcare settings for collecting, storing, or conveying information related to a patient's medical history, condition, or treatment. Examples include consent forms, referral forms, and records release forms.

candidates.label: Medical Record
candidates.definition: A medical record is a comprehensive and confidential document that contains a patient's medical history, including information about their medical conditions, diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, and interactions with healthcare providers. These records are typically maintained by healthcare institutions and practitioners to track and manage a patient's health and treatment over time, and they are essential for providing continuity of care and ensuring accurate medical documentation.

candidates.label: Medical Test
candidates.definition: A Medical Test is a clinical procedure performed to detect, diagnose, or monitor diseases, disease processes, susceptibility, or to determine a course of treatment. Examples include blood tests, DNA tests, drug tests, and paternity tests.

candidates.label: Severance Agreement
candidates.definition: An Employment Separation Agreement, also known as a Severance Agreement, is a legally binding document that outlines the terms and conditions under which an employment relationship is terminated. It typically includes details about severance pay, benefits continuation, non-disparagement clauses, non-compete agreements, and other post-employment obligations and rights. This agreement aims to provide clarity and protection for both the employer and the departing employee during the transition out of the company.

candidates.label: Compensation Agreement
candidates.definition: A Compensation Related Agreement is a legally binding contract that specifically focuses on the structure and details of an employee's compensation, including elements like base salary, bonuses, stock options, and other financial incentives. These agreements serve to outline how an employee's pay is determined, disbursed, and adjusted over time, ensuring clarity and fairness in compensation arrangements.

candidates.label: Application for Compensation
candidates.definition: An Application for Compensation is a formal request made by a professional, such as a bankruptcy trustee or attorney, to be reimbursed for their services and expenses related to a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Statement of Executive Compensation
candidates.definition: A Statement of Executive Compensation is a disclosure detailing the remuneration packages of a company's key executives and directors. It is usually included in annual proxy statements and is required by securities regulators.

candidates.label: Director Compensation and Governance Policy
candidates.definition: The Director Compensation and Governance Policy outlines the rules and guidelines for how board members are compensated and how governance activities are conducted within an organization. This policy ensures alignment with organizational goals and compliance with relevant laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Traffic Accident Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N - True if an incident involved a traffic accident; false otherwise.

candidates.label: Legal Services Related Attributes
candidates.definition: Legal Services Related Attributes refer to the characteristics and classifications of tasks performed by a legal service provider that determine whether the activities are billable or non-billable to the client. These attributes help identify the financial impact and efficiency of the services provided, influencing billing practices and revenue generation for the firm.

candidates.label: Regulatory Statement
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Statement is a type of Invoice Data that contains information related to legal regulations and requirements.

candidates.label: Dispute Events
candidates.definition: Dispute Events refer to the various stages and actions that occur during a legal disagreement between parties, including but not limited to negotiations, mediations, and arbitrations.

candidates.label: Alternative Dispute Resolution Events
candidates.definition: Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) Events refer to the events or phases involved in resolving legal disputes outside of traditional litigation, including arbitration and mediation.
synonyms: Eventos de Resolución Alternativa de Controversias
Eventos de Resolución Alternativa de Disputas
Eventos de resolução alternativa de disputas
Événements de règlement alternatif des litiges
代替紛争解決イベント

candidates.label: Regulatory Events
candidates.definition: Events related to legal services surrounding regulations.

candidates.label: Regulatory Filing Event
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Filing Event refers to a specific action or submission made to comply with regulations or requirements set forth by a governing authority. In the context of immigration, it could involve submitting applications, documents, or forms that adhere to the regulations and procedures established by immigration authorities for various immigration-related matters.

candidates.label: Employment Offer
candidates.definition: A job offer is a formal proposal made by an employer to a candidate selected for a position, detailing the terms and conditions of employment. This includes information about the role, salary, benefits, start date, and other pertinent details.

candidates.label: Employment Events
candidates.definition: Employment Events refer to specific milestones in an employee's work history with an organization, such as the date of hiring, date of termination, and date of re-hiring. These events are important for both administrative record-keeping and compliance with employment laws and regulations

candidates.label: Employment Written Warning
candidates.definition: An Employment Written Warning is a formal document issued by an employer to an employee, outlining specific instances of misconduct or performance issues. It serves as an official notice that continued violations may result in further disciplinary action, including termination.

candidates.label: Employment Mandatory Training
candidates.definition: Employment Mandatory Training is a compulsory educational program required by an employer to address specific skills, knowledge gaps, or compliance issues among employees. This training aims to improve performance, ensure adherence to company policies, or meet legal and regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Voluntary Employment Termination Event
candidates.definition: Voluntary employment termination event refers to the act of an employee willingly leaving their job, either through resignation or retirement. This is distinct from involuntary termination, where the employer initiates the separation due to reasons like downsizing, performance issues, or misconduct.

candidates.label: Employment Benefits
candidates.definition: Employment Benefits are additional compensations or advantages provided to employees over and above their regular salary, often as part of an employment contract. These benefits may include healthcare, retirement plans, paid time off, and other perks designed to attract and retain employees.

candidates.label: Educational Benefits
candidates.definition: Educational benefits are perks provided to employees to support their educational goals and aspirations, including offerings such as courses, seminars, tuition reimbursement, and professional development opportunities.

candidates.label: Dental Insurance Benefits
candidates.definition: Dental insurance benefits are a specialized form of health insurance offered to employees that covers part or all of the costs associated with dental care, such as routine check-ups, cleanings, and certain dental procedures.

candidates.label: Health Insurance Benefits
candidates.definition: Health insurance benefits refer to any medical coverage provided to employees as part of their compensation package, which pays for medical and surgical expenses incurred by the insured, often including preventive care and prescription medication.

candidates.label: Time-Off Benefits
candidates.definition: Time-off benefits encompass policies or perks that allow employees to take time away from work for various reasons, such as vacation, illness, or personal needs, often with pay.

candidates.label: Compensation Metrics
candidates.definition: Compensation Metrics are quantitative measures used to assess the financial rewards provided to employees. Examples include salaries, bonuses, and commissions, which help evaluate and compare the monetary value individuals receive for their work contributions.

candidates.label: Compensation Bonus
candidates.definition: A bonus is a supplemental payment given to employees in addition to their regular salary or wages, typically as a reward for exceptional performance, meeting certain targets, or achieving specific goals. Bonuses are intended to incentivize and recognize outstanding contributions to an organization.

candidates.label: Cost Metrics
candidates.definition: Metrics that specifically focus on the expenses and costs incurred in the process of delivering services or goods, used for budgeting, financial planning, and pricing strategies.

candidates.label: Revenue Metrics
candidates.definition: Metrics that provide a quantitative measure of the total income generated through various forms of engagement, offering valuable insights into financial health and growth opportunities.

candidates.label: Delaware Industrial Accident Board
candidates.definition: The Delaware Industrial Accident Board is a specialized court within the Delaware State Courts system that handles cases related to workplace injuries and accidents.

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Claims
candidates.definition: Ct. Cl.

candidates.label: Workers' Compensation Court
candidates.definition: A Workers' Compensation Court is a specific type of administrative proceeding forum that handles disputes related to workers' compensation claims.

candidates.label: Montana Workers Compensation Court
candidates.definition: The Montana Workers Compensation Court is a specialized court within the Montana State Courts system that handles cases related to workers' compensation disputes.

candidates.label: Connecticut Compensation Review Board
candidates.definition: Conn. Comp. Rev. Bd.

candidates.label: Oregon Workers' Compensation Division
candidates.definition: The Oregon Workers' Compensation Division is a state agency responsible for administering the workers' compensation system in Oregon.

candidates.label: Arkansas Workers' Compensation Commission
candidates.definition: Ark. Workers' Comp.

candidates.label: Mississippi Workers Compensation Opinions
candidates.definition: Mississippi Workers Compensation Opinions are legal decisions made by the Mississippi State Courts regarding workers' compensation cases in Mississippi.

candidates.label: New Jersey Department of Labor - Workmen's Compensation Bureau
candidates.definition: The New Jersey Department of Labor - Workmen's Compensation Bureau is a government entity responsible for administering proceedings related to workmen's compensation claims in the state of New Jersey.

hierarchy_path.label: New Jersey Department of Labor - Workmen's Compensation Bureau

candidates.label: Louisiana Department of Labor - Office of Workers' Compensation
candidates.definition: The Louisiana Department of Labor - Office of Workers' Compensation is a government agency responsible for administering workers' compensation proceedings in Louisiana State Courts.

hierarchy_path.label: Louisiana Department of Labor - Office of Workers' Compensation

candidates.label: Employee Benefits Security Administration
candidates.definition: The Employee Benefits Security Administration (EBSA) is an agency of the United States Department of Labor responsible for administering, regulating and enforcing the provisions of Title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA).
synonyms: Administración de Seguridad de Beneficios para Empleados
Administration de la sécurité des avantages sociaux des employés
Administração de Segurança de Benefícios aos Empregados

candidates.label: Veterans Benefits Administration
candidates.definition: The Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA) is an agency of the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs. It is responsible for administering the Department's programs that provide financial and other forms of assistance to veterans, their dependents, and survivors.
synonyms: Administration des avantages des anciens combattants

candidates.label: DOL Benefits Review Board
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor's Benefits Review Board was created in 1972, by the United States Congress, to review and issue decisions on appeals of workers' compensation claims arising under the Longshore and Harbor Workers' Compensation Act and the Black Lung Benefits amendments to the Federal Coal Mine Health and Safety Act of 1969.
synonyms: Conseil d'examen des avantages du DOL
Conselho de Revisão de Benefícios do DOL
Junta de Revisión de Beneficios del DOL

candidates.label: U.S. General Services Administration
candidates.definition: The General Services Administration (GSA), an independent agency of the United States government that helps to manage and support the basic functioning of federal agencies.
synonyms: Administración de Servicios Generales de EE. UU.
Administration des services généraux des États-Unis
Administração de Serviços Gerais dos EUA
המנהלת הכללית לשירותים ציבוריים של ארצות הברית
アメリカ合衆国一般サービス管理局

candidates.label: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services
candidates.definition: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) is an agency of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) that administers the country's naturalization and immigration systems.
synonyms: Services de citoyenneté et d'immigration des États-Unis
Servicio de Ciudadanía e Inmigración de los Estados Unidos
Servicios de Ciudadanía e Inmigración de Estados Unidos
Serviços de Cidadania e Imigração dos Estados Unidos
שירותי אזרחות והגירה של ארצות הברית
美国公民和移民服务局

candidates.label: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
candidates.definition: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS), previously known as the Health Care Financing Administration (HCFA), is a federal agency within the United States Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) that administers the Medicare program and works in partnership with state governments to administer Medicaid, the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), and health insurance portability standards.
synonyms: Centres for Medicare and Medicaid Services
Centres pour les services Medicare et Medicaid
Centros de Servicios de Medicare y Medicaid
Centros de Serviços de Medicare e Medicaid
Zentren für Medicare und Medicaid Services
メディケアおよびメディケイドサービスセンター
美国医疗保险和医疗补助服务中心

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services
Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services

candidates.label: Health Resources and Services Administration
candidates.definition: The Health Resources and Services Administration (HRSA) is an agency of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services focused on improving access to health care services for people who are uninsured, isolated or medically vulnerable.
synonyms: Administración de Recursos y Servicios de Salud
Administration des ressources et des services de santé
Administração de Recursos e Serviços de Saúde
保健資源・サービス管理局
卫生资源和服务管理局

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Postal Regulatory Commission
candidates.definition: The United States Postal Regulatory Commission (or PRC), formerly called the Postal Rate Commission, is an independent regulatory agency created by the Postal Reorganization Act of 1970.
synonyms: Comisión Reguladora Postal de EE. UU.
Commission de réglementation postale des États-Unis

candidates.label: U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission
candidates.definition: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is an independent agency of the United States government tasked with protecting public health and safety related to nuclear energy.
synonyms: Comisión Reguladora Nuclear de los Estados Unidos
Commission de réglementation nucléaire des États-Unis
הוועדה לרישוי גרעיני של ארצות הברית
米国原子力規制委員会

candidates.label: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission
candidates.definition: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) is the United States federal agency that regulates the transmission and wholesale sale of electricity and natural gas in interstate commerce and regulates the transportation of oil by pipeline in interstate commerce.
synonyms: Comisión Federal de Regulación de Energía
Comissão Federal de Regulação de Energia
Commission fédérale de régulation de l'énergie
連邦エネルギー規制委員会

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: NULL

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing care and early learning opportunities for infants and children. These establishments generally care for children from birth through school age and may also offer pre-kindergarten, kindergarten, and/or before- or after-school educational programs. The care and early learning provided by these establishments may include opportunities for development in health, social and emotional learning, and family engagement.

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: See industry description for 624410.

candidates.label: Engineering Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in applying physical laws and principles of engineering in the design, development, and utilization of machines, materials, instruments, structures, processes, and systems. The assignments undertaken by these establishments may involve any of the following activities: provision of advice, preparation of feasibility studies, preparation of preliminary and final plans and designs, provision of technical services during the construction or installation phase, inspection and evaluation of engineering projects, and related services.

hierarchy_path.label: Architectural, Engineering, and Related Services Industry

candidates.label: Engineering Services
candidates.definition: See industry description for 541330.

hierarchy_path.label: Architectural, Engineering, and Related Services Industry

candidates.label: Architectural Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in planning and designing residential, institutional, leisure, commercial, and industrial buildings and structures by applying knowledge of design, construction procedures, zoning regulations, building codes, and building materials.

hierarchy_path.label: Architectural, Engineering, and Related Services Industry

candidates.label: Architectural Services
candidates.definition: See industry description for 541310.

hierarchy_path.label: Architectural, Engineering, and Related Services Industry

candidates.label: Graphic Design Services
candidates.definition: See industry description for 541430.

candidates.label: Graphic Design Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in planning, designing, and managing the production of visual communication in order to convey specific messages or concepts, clarify complex information, or project visual identities. These services can include the design of printed materials, packaging, advertising, signage systems, and corporate identification (logos). This industry also includes commercial artists engaged exclusively in generating drawings and illustrations requiring technical accuracy or interpretative skills.

candidates.label: Interior Design Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in planning, designing, and administering projects in interior spaces to meet the physical and aesthetic needs of people using them, taking into consideration building codes, health and safety regulations, traffic patterns and floor planning, mechanical and electrical needs, and interior fittings and furniture. Interior designers and interior design consultants work in areas, such as hospitality design, health care design, institutional design, commercial and corporate design, and residential design. This industry also includes interior decorating consultants engaged exclusively in providing aesthetic services associated with interior spaces.

candidates.label: AAA International Dispute Resolution Procedures
candidates.definition: AAA International Dispute Resolution Procedures refer to the specific rules and procedures established by the American Arbitration Association for resolving international disputes through arbitration.
synonyms: Procedimentos de Resolução de Disputas Internacionais da AAA
Procédures de règlement des différends internationaux de l'AAA

candidates.label: AAA Employment Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedures
candidates.definition: The AAA Employment Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedures are a set of guidelines and procedures established by the American Arbitration Association specifically for resolving employment-related disputes through arbitration and mediation.
synonyms: AAA就业仲裁规则和调解程序
AAA雇用仲裁規則および調停手続き
Regras de Arbitragem de Emprego e Procedimentos de Mediação da AAA
Règles d'arbitrage et de médiation en matière d'emploi de l'AAA

hierarchy_path.label: AAA Employment Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedures

candidates.label: AAA Labor Arbitration Rules
candidates.definition: The AAA Labor Arbitration Rules are a set of rules created by the American Arbitration Association specifically for labor disputes.
synonyms: Regras de Arbitragem Trabalhista da AAA
Règles d'arbitrage du travail de l'AAA

candidates.label: Stroke
candidates.definition: A vascular insult to the brain, commonly referred to as a stroke or cerebrovascular accident, occurs when blood flow to a part of the brain is disrupted, leading to tissue damage and neurological deficits. This condition can result from either ischemia (lack of blood flow) or hemorrhage (bleeding) within the brain.

candidates.label: B Corporation
candidates.definition: A B Corporation is a type of corporation that is legally required to consider the impact of its decisions not only on its shareholders, but also on its stakeholders, such as employees, customers, and the environment.

candidates.label: Benefit Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A Benefit Limited Liability Company (BLLC) is a type of limited liability company that combines the liability protection of a traditional LLC with a commitment to pursuing public benefits alongside profit. These companies are structured to focus on social and environmental goals while still operating as for-profit entities.

candidates.label: Labor Union
candidates.definition: A trade union (or a labor union in the U.S.) is an association of workers forming a legal unit or legal personhood, usually called a "bargaining unit", which acts as bargaining agent and legal representative for a unit of employees in all matters of law or right arising from or in the administration of a collective agreement.

candidates.label: Neurological Disorders
candidates.definition: Neurological disorders encompass a broad range of conditions that affect the nervous system, including the brain, spinal cord, and peripheral nerves. These disorders can result in a variety of symptoms, such as muscle weakness, coordination issues, seizures, and cognitive impairments, requiring tailored medical and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Digestive Disorders
candidates.definition: Digestive disorders encompass a broad range of conditions affecting the digestive system, including the esophagus, stomach, intestines, liver, and pancreas. These disorders can result in significant functional limitations, impacting nutrient absorption, digestion, and overall health, and may require ongoing medical management or surgical intervention.

candidates.label: Tic Disorders
candidates.definition: Tic Disorders involve the presence of repetitive, involuntary movements or vocalizations. They may include simple motor tics, such as blinking or head jerking, and complex tics, such as jumping or uttering words or phrases.

candidates.label: Skin Disorders
candidates.definition: Skin disorders encompass a range of conditions affecting the skin's structure and function, including inflammatory, infectious, and neoplastic diseases. These disorders can cause symptoms such as rashes, lesions, and systemic effects that impair the skin's ability to protect the body.

candidates.label: Blood Disorders
candidates.definition: Hematological Disorders encompass a range of conditions affecting the blood and its components, including red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets, and plasma. These disorders can impact blood production, function, and composition, leading to symptoms such as anemia, bleeding, or increased risk of infection.

candidates.label: Eating Disorders
candidates.definition: Eating Disorders are characterized by disturbances in eating behavior and preoccupation with, and excessive self-evaluation of, body weight and shape. Symptoms and signs may include, but are not limited to, restriction of energy consumption when compared with individual requirements; recurrent episodes of binge eating or behavior intended to prevent weight gain, such as self-induced vomiting, excessive exercise, or misuse of laxatives; mood disturbances, social withdrawal, or irritability; amenorrhea; dental problems; abnormal laboratory findings; and cardiac abnormalities.

candidates.label: Alternative Dispute Resolution Management
candidates.definition: Alternative Dispute Resolution Management refers to the process of resolving legal disputes outside of the traditional court system, such as through mediation or arbitration, and involves managing the various stages of the ADR process.

candidates.label: Managed Services
candidates.definition: Managed Services: A type of alternative legal service that involves the outsourcing of legal work to a third-party provider who manages and performs the work on behalf of the client.

candidates.label: Process Improvement Services
candidates.definition: Process Improvement Services refers to professional services that aim to enhance and optimize business processes, including but not limited to consulting and management, in order to increase efficiency and effectiveness.

candidates.label: Change Management and Adoption Services
candidates.definition: Change Management and Adoption Services refers to the professional services that assist organizations in managing and implementing changes to their business processes, technology, or organizational structure, with a focus on ensuring successful adoption and integration of the changes.

candidates.label: Document Management Implementation Services
candidates.definition: Document Management Implementation Services refers to the provision of professional assistance and guidance in the planning, execution, and maintenance of systems and processes for organizing, storing, and retrieving electronic documents within an organization.

candidates.label: Regulatory Filing Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Filing Management refers to the process of managing and submitting required documents and information to regulatory authorities in order to comply with legal and regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Regulatory Research Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Research Management refers to the process of conducting legal research specifically related to regulations and compliance, including tracking and managing changes to regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Pension and Benefits Claims
candidates.definition: Pension and benefits claims are legal actions initiated by an employee against their employer for not complying with applicable laws or the terms of an employment agreement regarding retirement and other benefits. These claims can involve issues such as the mismanagement or mishandling of employee pension or retirement accounts, denial or reduction of employee benefits, or failure to provide required benefits such as health insurance or disability coverage. Employees may also bring pension and benefits claims if their employer retaliates against them for asserting their rights to these benefits.
synonyms: Réclamations de pensions et d'avantages sociaux

candidates.label: Public Benefits Objectives
candidates.definition: Public benefits objectives refer to legal services related to the accurate and efficient distribution of public assistance to eligible recipients. For example, underpayment objectives focus on identifying and rectifying instances where beneficiaries receive less than they are entitled to, often due to administrative errors or oversight. Overpayment objectives aim to detect and recover funds that have been erroneously disbursed in excess of eligibility, to maintain the integrity and sustainability of the benefit program.

candidates.label: Requirements for Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Requirements for public benefits refer to the conditions or activities that an individual or family must fulfill to qualify for or continue receiving financial assistance or services from a government program. These can include mandates for job searches, participation in educational or vocational training programs, or completion of specific plans aimed at improving self-sufficiency. Failure to meet these requirements may result in reduction, suspension, or termination of benefits.

candidates.label: Welfare Fraud
candidates.definition: The use of deceitful statements, practices, or devices to unlawfully obtain welfare benefits.

candidates.label: Hit and Run
candidates.definition: The concept of Hit and Run refers to a criminal offense where a person involved in an accident fails to stop and provide information, such as their name and contact details, to the other party involved.
synonyms: Failure to Stop and Give Information
Fuga de la escena del accidente

candidates.label: Health Rights
candidates.definition: Health rights refer to the legal and ethical entitlements aimed at ensuring access to a minimum standard of healthcare and well-being for individuals. These rights can include access to medical treatment, preventative care, and essential medications, and are often supported by both international treaties and national healthcare policies.

candidates.label: Victim Compensation
candidates.definition: Victim compensation refers to the financial restitution provided to victims of crimes to cover their losses and alleviate the adverse impacts of the crime. This compensation can cover expenses such as medical bills, therapy costs, lost wages, and other related damages, and is typically funded by government programs or court orders against the offender.

candidates.label: Compensation Clause
candidates.definition: A Compensation Clause, also known as a Payment Clause, is a provision in a contract that outlines the terms and conditions for payment in the event of a breach or termination of the agreement.

candidates.label: No Compensation Clause
candidates.definition: A No Compensation Clause is a provision in a contract that prohibits one party from receiving any form of payment or reimbursement for their services or actions related to the contract.

candidates.label: Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Public benefits are government programs designed to provide financial or material aid to individuals or families who meet certain eligibility criteria, usually based on need or income. These benefits can include healthcare, food assistance, housing subsidies, and unemployment compensation, among others, and are intended to improve the well-being and stability of a society

candidates.label: Analysis of Employee Benefits/Pensions
candidates.definition: The Analysis of Employee Benefits/Pensions is the examination and evaluation of the benefits and pension plans provided to employees by an employer, typically in the context of legal obligations.
synonyms: Analyse des avantages sociaux/retraites des employés
従業員の福利厚生/年金の分析

candidates.label: Provider of Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Provider of public benefits refers to the governmental entity responsible for administering and disbursing public assistance programs. These can be national, state, or local agencies, each offering different types of benefits tailored to the needs and eligibility criteria of their jurisdiction.

candidates.label: Unemployment Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Unemployment public benefits refer to government programs that provide financial assistance to individuals who have lost their jobs and are actively seeking new employment. These benefits are typically time-limited and contingent on meeting certain eligibility criteria like work history and reason for unemployment.

candidates.label: Analysis of Benefits Plans
candidates.definition: The concept of "Analysis of Benefits Plans" refers to the examination and evaluation of employee benefits plans, such as health insurance and retirement plans, to determine their effectiveness and compliance with legal requirements.

candidates.label: M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits Practice
candidates.definition: A legal practice area that focuses on employment, labor, and employee benefits issues related to mergers and acquisitions, including executive compensation matters.
synonyms: M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits
M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits Service

M&A F3 就业、劳动和员工福利实践

Práctica de empleo, trabajo y beneficios para empleados de M&A F3
Prática de Emprego, Trabalho e Benefícios aos Empregados M&A F3
