hierarchy_path.label: M&A F Regulatory and Specialty Matters Practice
M&A F3 Employment, Labor and Employee Benefits Practice

candidates.label: Military and Veteran Benefits
candidates.definition: Military and Veteran Benefits refer to a range of government programs designed to provide financial, healthcare, educational, and other types of assistance to active-duty military members, veterans, and their families. These benefits are intended to support those who have served in the armed forces and may vary based on service length, deployment history, and other factors. In the United States, these benefits can include medical assistance, disability, educational benefits, mortgage benefits, old age benefits, and other programs.

candidates.label: Assessment of Eligibility for Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Assessment of eligibility for public benefits by a lawyer or other legal service provider involves a comprehensive review and analysis of an individual's or family's financial, social, and legal circumstances to determine their qualification for specific government assistance programs. This pre-application assessment typically includes gathering documentation, evaluating against program criteria, and providing legal advice on how best to present the case to maximize chances of approval

hierarchy_path.label: Assessment of Eligibility for Public Benefits

candidates.label: Old Age Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Old Age Public Benefits refer to government programs designed to provide financial assistance, healthcare, and other forms of support to individuals who have reached retirement age. These programs aim to ensure a reasonable standard of living and access to necessary services for elderly citizens.

candidates.label: Executive Compensation Practice
candidates.definition: A specialized area of legal practice that deals with the compensation of executives in the context of mergers and acquisitions.
synonyms: M&A F3.1 Pratique de la rémunération des cadres
M&A F3.1 Prática de Remuneração Executiva
M&A F3.1 経営陣報酬実務
M&A F3.1 高管薪酬实践

candidates.label: Proceeding Transferred Into This Dispute Forum
candidates.definition: The concept of "Case Transferred Into This Dispute Forum" refers to a legal matter that has been moved from another forum to the current forum for resolution, and is currently open or pending.
synonyms: Case Transferred Into This Dispute Forum

hierarchy_path.label: Proceeding Transferred Into This Dispute Forum

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Insurance Adjuster
candidates.definition: An insurance adjuster is a professional who works for an insurance company and investigates and evaluates insurance claims made by policyholders.

candidates.label: Insurer
candidates.definition: One that insures, usually an insurance underwriter.

candidates.label: Lawful Permanent Resident
synonyms: A Lawful Permanent Resident (LPR) is an individual who has been legally granted the right to live and work indefinitely within the destination country (e.g., United States). LPR status provides many of the same benefits as citizenship, such as the ability to work and travel freely, but unlike citizens, LPRs may lose their status under certain conditions like committing serious crimes or abandoning their destination-country residence (e.g., U.S. residence).

candidates.label: Role in Document
candidates.definition: A "Role in Document" is a specific position or responsibility held by an individual, such as a Filer, Editor, or Author, in relation to a legal document or matter.

candidates.label: Legal Services Provider - Person
candidates.definition: A Legal Services Provider - Person is an individual who provides legal services and is associated with specific invoice data, including their provider level, timekeeper ID, first name, and last name.
synonyms: 法律サービスプロバイダー - 個人
法律服务提供者 - 个人

candidates.label: Alternative Legal Services Provider
candidates.definition: An Alternative Legal Services Provider (ALSP) is an entity that delivers legal or legal-related services outside the conventional law firm model.

candidates.label: Regulatory Applicant
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Applicant is an individual or entity that applies for authorization, licensing, or compliance approval from a regulatory authority. This role relates to sectors where operations are subject to legal and regulatory standards, ensuring adherence to established laws, guidelines, and norms.

candidates.label: Regulatory Petitioner
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Petitioner refers to an individual or entity formally requesting legal or official approval from a regulatory body, often through a structured application process. This encompasses a wide array of requests, from immigration status changes to the issuance of permits for business operations or environmental actions.

candidates.label: Governmental Regulatory Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Regulatory Law Division is a governmental entity responsible for creating, implementing, and enforcing regulations. It ensures compliance with laws and regulations, provides guidance to regulatory agencies, and represents the government in regulatory matters

candidates.label: Insurance Law
candidates.definition: Law surrounding insurance, including insurance policies and claims.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Public Policy and Government Affairs Law
candidates.definition: Rules surrounding lobbying and political advocacy.
synonyms: Derecho de Política Pública y Asuntos Gubernamentales
Direito de Políticas Públicas e Assuntos Governamentais
Droit des politiques publiques et des affaires gouvernementales
Public Policy and Government Affairs Law
Recht der öffentlichen Politik und Regierungsangelegenheiten
सार्वजनिक नीति और सरकारी कानून विषयक
公共政策および政府関連法
公共政策和政府事务法

hierarchy_path.label: Public Policy and Government Affairs Law

candidates.label: Bankruptcy, Insolvency, and Restructuring Law
candidates.definition: Laws relating to insolvent individuals and companies.
synonyms: Droit de la faillite et de la restructuration

candidates.label: Lender Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law which holds lender liable for a borrower's financial losses that are directly or indirectly related to the lender's actions.

candidates.label: Product Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to liability for injuries caused by defective or unsafe products.
synonyms: Droit de la responsabilité du produit

candidates.label: Corporate Law
candidates.definition: Law that governs the formation and operation of corporations.

candidates.label: Corporate Governance Law
candidates.definition: Mechanisms, processes, and relations by which corporations are controlled and operated.

candidates.label: Corporate Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to insolvency or reorganization of corporations.

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Catastrophe Bonds
candidates.definition: Catastrophe bonds, commonly known as cat bonds, are high-yield debt instruments designed to raise money for insurance companies in the event of a natural disaster. These bonds provide insurers with an alternative source of capital to cover significant losses from catastrophes, transferring some of the financial risk to investors who receive regular coupon payments unless a specified catastrophe event occurs.

candidates.label: Mobile Home
candidates.definition: A Mobile Home is a prefabricated residential structure built in a factory and transported to its site, designed for permanent or semi-permanent habitation. These homes are typically placed in mobile home parks or on private land and can be moved to different locations as needed.

candidates.label: Whole Life Insurance
candidates.definition: Whole life insurance is a type of permanent life insurance that provides coverage for the insured's entire lifetime. The policy includes a death benefit and typically a cash value component that grows over time, and the premiums are generally fixed and paid for the life of the policy.

candidates.label: Universal Life Insurance
candidates.definition: Universal life insurance is a type of permanent life insurance that offers both a death benefit and a cash value component, similar to whole life insurance. However, it differs in its flexibility, allowing policyholders to adjust the premiums and death benefit within certain limits, and it often has an investment element where the cash value can be invested for potential growth.

candidates.label: Term Life Insurance
candidates.definition: Term life insurance is a policy that provides coverage for a specified period, or "term," usually ranging from 10 to 30 years. If the insured individual dies within this term, the death benefit is paid out to the beneficiaries; however, if the term expires and the policyholder is still alive, there is generally no payout.

candidates.label: Statement of Good Faith
candidates.definition: A Statement of Good Faith is a written declaration made in a Bankruptcy and Restructuring Document, expressing the honest intention of the declarant to act in good faith towards the other parties involved in the proceedings.

candidates.label: Insurance Claims Form
candidates.definition: An Insurance Claims Form is a standardized document used by a policyholder to request reimbursement or coverage for a loss or event that is insured under their policy. The form usually includes fields for providing details about the claim such as the incident, damages, and other relevant documentation.

candidates.label: Home Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: A home insurance policy is a contract between a homeowner and an insurance provider that offers financial protection against damages to the home, its contents, or liability claims arising from injuries on the property. The policy outlines the covered perils, such as fire, theft, or natural disasters, and requires the payment of premiums for the coverage to remain active.

candidates.label: Homeowner's Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: A homeowner's insurance policy is a contract between a homeowner and an insurance provider that offers financial protection against damages to the home, its contents, and liability claims arising from injuries on the property. The policy typically covers a range of perils such as fire, theft, and natural disasters. Premiums are usually required for the coverage to remain active.

candidates.label: Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: An insurance policy is a contract between an insurance provider and a policyholder that outlines the terms and conditions under which the insurer will provide coverage and financial compensation for specific risks, events, or damages. The policy specifies the premiums to be paid by the policyholder, the coverage limits, and the obligations of each party in the event of a claim.

candidates.label: Insurance Form
candidates.definition: An Insurance Form is a standardized document used by an insurance provider for capturing relevant information to evaluate, administer, or process an insurance policy. This could be for applications, claims, renewals, or any other insurance-related activities.

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Insured Matter
candidates.definition: An insured matter refers to the specific risk or subject covered under an insurance policy, including the circumstances, conditions, and objects that the insurance agreement protects against.

candidates.label: Insurance Deductible Amount
candidates.definition: An insurance deductible amount, in the context of legal matters, is the portion of a covered loss that the insured party is responsible for paying before the insurance company's coverage begins.

candidates.label: Arbitrators/Mediators Expense
candidates.definition: The Arbitrators/Mediators Expense concept refers to the costs incurred by parties in a legal matter for the services of an impartial third party who facilitates negotiations and resolution of disputes outside of court.

candidates.label: Legal Services Related Attributes
candidates.definition: Legal Services Related Attributes refer to the characteristics and classifications of tasks performed by a legal service provider that determine whether the activities are billable or non-billable to the client. These attributes help identify the financial impact and efficiency of the services provided, influencing billing practices and revenue generation for the firm.

candidates.label: Regulatory Statement
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Statement is a type of Invoice Data that contains information related to legal regulations and requirements.

candidates.label: Engagement Fee Model
candidates.definition: The model (e.g., hourly, fixed fee, contingency fee) by which legal services are obtained.
synonyms: エンゲージメント料金モデル

candidates.label: Vacancy
candidates.definition: Vacancy refers to the state of a property or space being unoccupied or not in use. This can occur in both residential and commercial real estate, indicating that no tenant or occupant currently resides or operates in the property.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy and Restructuring Events
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy and Restructuring Events: The various legal proceedings and actions that occur during the process of bankruptcy and restructuring, including confirmation of a bankruptcy plan, asset sales, installment payments, joinder, auctions, creditor voting, initial meetings, disbursements, automatic stays, and financial management courses.
synonyms: Événements de faillite et de restructuration
破産および再編イベント

candidates.label: Dispute Events
candidates.definition: Dispute Events refer to the various stages and actions that occur during a legal disagreement between parties, including but not limited to negotiations, mediations, and arbitrations.

candidates.label: Alternative Dispute Resolution Events
candidates.definition: Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) Events refer to the events or phases involved in resolving legal disputes outside of traditional litigation, including arbitration and mediation.
synonyms: Eventos de Resolución Alternativa de Controversias
Eventos de Resolución Alternativa de Disputas
Eventos de resolução alternativa de disputas
Événements de règlement alternatif des litiges
代替紛争解決イベント

candidates.label: Regulatory Events
candidates.definition: Events related to legal services surrounding regulations.

candidates.label: Regulatory Filing Event
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Filing Event refers to a specific action or submission made to comply with regulations or requirements set forth by a governing authority. In the context of immigration, it could involve submitting applications, documents, or forms that adhere to the regulations and procedures established by immigration authorities for various immigration-related matters.

candidates.label: Corporate Representative Deposition
candidates.definition: A Corporate Representative Deposition is a legal proceeding where a company designates a representative to testify on its behalf in response to a subpoena or other legal request for information.

candidates.label: Military Discharge
candidates.definition: Military Discharge is the formal release of a service member from their obligation to continue service in the armed forces. The discharge can occur for various reasons, such as the completion of a service term, medical reasons, or disciplinary actions, and may be classified as honorable, general, or other types, affecting future veteran benefits.

candidates.label: Dental Insurance Benefits
candidates.definition: Dental insurance benefits are a specialized form of health insurance offered to employees that covers part or all of the costs associated with dental care, such as routine check-ups, cleanings, and certain dental procedures.

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Claims
candidates.definition: Ct. Cl.

candidates.label: Copyright Claims Board
candidates.definition: A Copyright Claims Board (CCB) is a specialized administrative body within the United States Copyright Office that is responsible for resolving disputes related to copyright claims.
synonyms: Conseil des revendications de droits d'auteur
Conselho de Reivindicações de Direitos Autorais
Junta de Reclamaciones de Derechos de Autor
Junta de Resolución de Reclamaciones de Derechos de Autor
著作権請求審査委員会

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Federal Claims
candidates.definition: Fed. Cl.

candidates.label: U.S. Bankruptcy Court - S.D. Indiana
candidates.definition: Bankr. S.D. Ind.

candidates.label: Stock Exchanges
candidates.definition: Stock Exchanges are organized markets where securities such as stocks, bonds, and other financial instruments are bought and sold. These exchanges provide a regulated and transparent environment for trading, ensuring fair pricing and facilitating liquidity for investors.

candidates.label: Texas Supreme Court
candidates.definition: Tex.

candidates.label: Michigan State Courts
candidates.definition: Michigan State Courts are the judicial system of the state of Michigan, consisting of the Michigan Supreme Court, Michigan Circuit Court, Michigan District Court, and Michigan Court of Appeals.

candidates.label: U.S. Bankruptcy Court - D. Utah
candidates.definition: Bankr. D. Utah

candidates.label: U.S. Bankruptcy Court - D. Idaho
candidates.definition: Bankr. D. Idaho

candidates.label: U.S. Bankruptcy Court - S.D. Ohio
candidates.definition: Bankr. S.D. Ohio

candidates.label: Office of Tax Policy
candidates.definition: The Office of Tax Policy is an agency of the United States Department of the Treasury headed by the Assistant Secretary of the Treasury for Tax Policy.

candidates.label: Domestic Policy Council
candidates.definition: The Domestic Policy Council (DPC) of the United States is the principal forum used by the President of the United States for considering domestic policy matters, excluding economic matters, which are the domain of the National Economic Council.

candidates.label: Office of Federal Procurement Policy
candidates.definition: The Office of Federal Procurement Policy (OFPP) is a component of the United States Office of Management and Budget (OMB), which is part of the Executive Office of the President of the United States (EOP).
synonyms: Bureau de la politique fédérale d'approvisionnement
Escritório de Política Federal de Aquisições
Oficina de Política de Adquisiciones Federales

candidates.label: Office of National Drug Control Policy
candidates.definition: The Office of National Drug Control Policy is a component of the Executive Office of the President of the United States.
synonyms: Bureau de la politique nationale de contrôle des drogues
Escritório de Política Nacional de Controle de Drogas
Office of National Drug Control Policy
Oficina de Política Nacional de Control de Drogas
राष्ट्रीय नशीली दवा नियंत्रण नीति कार्यालय

hierarchy_path.label: Office of National Drug Control Policy

candidates.label: Office of Disability Employment Policy
candidates.definition: The Office of Disability Employment Policy (ODEP) is the only non-regulatory federal agency that promotes policies and coordinates with employers and all levels of government to increase workplace success for people with disabilities.
synonyms: Bureau de la politique de l'emploi des personnes handicapées
Escritório de Política de Emprego para Pessoas com Deficiência
Oficina de Política de Empleo para Personas con Discapacidad
משרד מדיניות התעסוקה לאנשים עם מוגבלויות

candidates.label: Office of Science and Technology Policy
candidates.definition: The Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) is a department of the United States government, part of the Executive Office of the President (EOP), established with a broad mandate to advise the President on the effects of science and technology on domestic and international affairs.
synonyms: Bureau de la politique scientifique et technologique
Escritório de Política de Ciência e Tecnologia
Office of Science and Technology Policy
Oficina de Política de Ciencia y Tecnología

hierarchy_path.label: Office of Science and Technology Policy

candidates.label: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
candidates.definition: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) is a United States government corporation providing deposit insurance to depositors in U.S. commercial banks and savings institutions.
synonyms: Corporación Federal de Seguros de Depósito
Corporação Federal de Seguro de Depósito

candidates.label: Center for Consumer Information and Insurance Oversight
candidates.definition: The Center for Consumer Information and Insurance Oversight (CCIIO) is charged with helping implement many reforms of the Affordable Care Act, the historic health reform bill that was signed into law March 23, 2010. CCIIO oversees the implementation of the provisions related to private health insurance. In particular, CCIIO is working with states to establish new Health Insurance Marketplaces.
synonyms: Centre for Consumer Information and Insurance Oversight
Centre pour l'information des consommateurs et la surveillance des assurances
Centro de Información y Supervisión de Seguros para el Consumidor
Centro de Informação ao Consumidor e Supervisão de Seguros
מרכז למידע ופיקוח על ביטוח לצרכנים
उपभोक्ता सूचना और बीमा निगरानी केंद्र
消費者情報及び保険監督センター
消费者信息和保险监管中心

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services
Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
Center for Consumer Information and Insurance Oversight

candidates.label: Bad River Band of the Lake Superior Tribe

hierarchy_path.label: Bad River Band of the Lake Superior Tribe

candidates.label: U.S. General Services Administration
candidates.definition: The General Services Administration (GSA), an independent agency of the United States government that helps to manage and support the basic functioning of federal agencies.
synonyms: Administración de Servicios Generales de EE. UU.
Administration des services généraux des États-Unis
Administração de Serviços Gerais dos EUA
המנהלת הכללית לשירותים ציבוריים של ארצות הברית
アメリカ合衆国一般サービス管理局

candidates.label: Religious Market Segment
candidates.definition: The religious market segment refers to a division of the market that is specifically targeted towards consumers based on their religious beliefs and practices. This segment encompasses products, services, and marketing strategies that are designed to cater to the needs, preferences, and interests of individuals who follow specific religious faiths, taking into account factors such as religious observances, dietary restrictions, and spiritual needs.

candidates.label: Finance and Insurance Services Industry
candidates.definition: The Finance and Insurance industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in financial transactions (transactions involving the creation, liquidation, or change in ownership of financial assets) and/or in facilitating financial transactions. Three principal types of activities include: raising funds by taking deposits and/or issuing securities and, in the process, incurring liabilities; and pooling of risk by underwriting insurance and annuities; and providing specialized services facilitating or supporting financial intermediation, insurance, and employee benefit programs.
synonyms: Industria de Servicios Financieros y de Seguros
Industrie des services financiers et d'assurance
Indústria de Serviços Financeiros e de Seguros
金融・保険サービス業

candidates.label: Other Insurance Related Activities
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing services related to insurance (except insurance agencies and brokerages).

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Agencies, Brokerages, and Other Insurance Related Activities

candidates.label: Insurance Carriers
candidates.definition:
This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in underwriting (assuming the risk, assigning premiums, and so forth) annuities and insurance policies and investing premiums to build up a portfolio of financial assets to be used against future claims. Direct insurance carriers are establishments that are primarily engaged in initially underwriting and assuming the risk of annuities and insurance policies. Reinsurance carriers are establishments that are primarily engaged in assuming all or part of the risk associated with an existing insurance policy (or set of policies) originally underwritten by another insurance carrier.

Industries are defined in terms of the type of risk being insured against, such as death, loss of employment because of age or disability, and/or property damage. Contributions and premiums are set on the basis of actuarial calculations of probable payouts based on risk factors from experience tables and expected investment returns on reserves.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry

candidates.label: Insurance Agencies and Brokerages
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in acting as agents (i.e., brokers) in selling annuities and insurance policies.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Agencies, Brokerages, and Other Insurance Related Activities

candidates.label: Insurance Agencies and Brokerages
candidates.definition: See industry description for 524210.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Agencies, Brokerages, and Other Insurance Related Activities

candidates.label: Direct Insurance (except Life, Health, and Medical) Carriers
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting (i.e., assuming the risk and assigning premiums) various types of insurance policies (except life, disability income, accidental death and dismemberment, and health and medical insurance policies).

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Direct Insurance (except Life, Health, and Medical) Carriers

candidates.label: Insurance and Employee Benefit Funds
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises legal entities (i.e., funds, plans, and/or programs) organized to provide insurance and employee benefits exclusively for the sponsor, firm, or its employees or members.

hierarchy_path.label: Funds, Trusts, and Other Financial Vehicles Industry

candidates.label: Direct Life Insurance Carriers
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting (i.e., assuming the risk and assigning premiums) annuities and life insurance policies, disability income insurance policies, and accidental death and dismemberment insurance policies.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers

candidates.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
candidates.definition: Industries in the Insurance Carriers and Related Activities subsector group establishments that are primarily engaged in one of the following: (1) underwriting (assuming the risk, assigning premiums, and so forth) annuities and insurance policies or (2) facilitating such underwriting by selling insurance policies and by providing other insurance and employee benefit related services.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry

candidates.label: Non-current Law
candidates.definition: The previously effective law currently appears to have no legal efficacy, having been displaced (e.g., overruled, reversed, vacated, superseded).

candidates.label: Data Protection Regulation
candidates.definition: Data Protection Regulation refers to laws and policies designed to safeguard personal and sensitive information from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, alteration, and destruction. These regulations ensure that organizations (e.g., companies, nonprofits, governments) implement appropriate measures to protect data privacy and security, complying with legal standards such as the GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA.

candidates.label: AAA International Dispute Resolution Procedures
candidates.definition: AAA International Dispute Resolution Procedures refer to the specific rules and procedures established by the American Arbitration Association for resolving international disputes through arbitration.
synonyms: Procedimentos de Resolução de Disputas Internacionais da AAA
Procédures de règlement des différends internationaux de l'AAA

candidates.label: Civil Law System
candidates.definition: Civil Law is a legal system that is primarily based on written codes and statutes, rather than case law. This system emphasizes comprehensive sets of laws that are codified and accessible, guiding judges in applying the law to individual cases.

candidates.label: Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: A court's body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Trust (Emotion)
candidates.definition: Trust is an emotion based on the belief in the reliability, truth, or ability of someone or something, often associated with a sense of security and confidence.

candidates.label: Delusional Disorder
candidates.definition: Delusional Disorder is a mental health condition characterized by the presence of one or more persistent delusions that are not attributable to other mental health disorders or medical conditions. Individuals with delusional disorder experience significant distress or impairment due to the belief in false, fixed, and unshakable convictions despite evidence to the contrary.

candidates.label: Partnership
candidates.definition: A partnership is a type of legal entity where two or more individuals or entities come together to carry out a business venture, sharing profits and losses.

candidates.label: Limited Partnership
candidates.definition: A Limited Partnership is a business entity that consists of one or more General Partners and one or more Limited Partners. The General Partner may be an individual or an entity, such as a corporation. Typically, the General Partners are liable for any and all of the company's financial obligations, while the Limited Partners possess no liability for the company's debts, obligations or actions. General Partners usually possess all the rights and responsibilities of managing the business entity, including all of its activities and financial matters. They also hold general liability for the debts, obligations, and activities of the Limited Partnership.

candidates.label: General Partnership
candidates.definition: A general partnership, the basic form of partnership under common law, is in most countries an association of persons or an unincorporated company with the following major features: must be created by agreement, proof of existence and estoppel; formed by two or more persons; the owners are jointly and severally liable for any legal actions and debts the company may face, unless otherwise provided by law or in the agreement. It is a partnership in which partners share equally in both responsibility and liability.

candidates.label: Master Limited Partnership
candidates.definition: A Master Limited Partnership (MLP) is a publicly traded partnership primarily involved in the natural resources, real estate, and financial services sectors. MLPs combine the tax benefits of a partnership with the liquidity of publicly traded securities.

candidates.label: Exempted Limited Partnership
candidates.definition: An exempted limited partnership (ELP) is a Cayman partnership that is registered under the Exempted Limited Partnership Law. The ELP Law specifically preserves relevant provisions of the Partnership Law and provides that the principles of common law and equity applicable to partnerships shall apply to ELPs.
synonyms: 免責有限責任事業組合

candidates.label: Limited Liability Partnership
candidates.definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A limited liability company (LLC) is a corporate structure in the United States whereby the owners are not personally liable for the company's debts or liabilities. Limited liability companies are hybrid entities that combine the characteristics of a corporation with those of a partnership or sole proprietorship.

candidates.label: Benefit Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A Benefit Limited Liability Company (BLLC) is a type of limited liability company that combines the liability protection of a traditional LLC with a commitment to pursuing public benefits alongside profit. These companies are structured to focus on social and environmental goals while still operating as for-profit entities.

candidates.label: Document Management Add-On
candidates.definition: A Document Management Add-On is a specialized tool or feature that enhances the functionality of a Document Management system. It is a software component that can be added to an existing Document Management system to provide additional capabilities or improve existing ones.

candidates.label: Document Management Implementation Services
candidates.definition: Document Management Implementation Services refers to the provision of professional assistance and guidance in the planning, execution, and maintenance of systems and processes for organizing, storing, and retrieving electronic documents within an organization.

candidates.label: Alternative Dispute Resolution Management
candidates.definition: Alternative Dispute Resolution Management refers to the process of resolving legal disputes outside of the traditional court system, such as through mediation or arbitration, and involves managing the various stages of the ADR process.

candidates.label: Managed Services
candidates.definition: Managed Services: A type of alternative legal service that involves the outsourcing of legal work to a third-party provider who manages and performs the work on behalf of the client.

candidates.label: Process Improvement Services
candidates.definition: Process Improvement Services refers to professional services that aim to enhance and optimize business processes, including but not limited to consulting and management, in order to increase efficiency and effectiveness.

candidates.label: Change Management and Adoption Services
candidates.definition: Change Management and Adoption Services refers to the professional services that assist organizations in managing and implementing changes to their business processes, technology, or organizational structure, with a focus on ensuring successful adoption and integration of the changes.

candidates.label: Regulatory Filing Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Filing Management refers to the process of managing and submitting required documents and information to regulatory authorities in order to comply with legal and regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Regulatory Research Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Research Management refers to the process of conducting legal research specifically related to regulations and compliance, including tracking and managing changes to regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Corporate Meeting Management
candidates.definition: Corporate Meeting Management refers to the process of organizing and managing meetings for corporations, including scheduling, logistics, and documentation.

candidates.label: Insurance Carrier Bad Faith
candidates.definition: The concept of Insurance Carrier Bad Faith refers to the intentional or reckless failure of an insurance company to fulfill its contractual obligations to its policyholders, such as denying a valid claim or failing to investigate a claim in a timely manner.

candidates.label: Good Faith Clause
candidates.definition: A Good Faith Clause is a provision in a contract that requires the parties to act honestly and fairly towards each other in their dealings, and to refrain from taking advantage of one another.

candidates.label: Insurance Policy Claim
candidates.definition: A claim made by an individual or entity to an insurance company for compensation or reimbursement for a loss or damage covered by an insurance policy.

candidates.label: Insurance Claims
candidates.definition: Insurance Claims: A legal demand made by an insured party to an insurance company for compensation or coverage for a loss or damage that is covered by the insurance policy.

candidates.label: Principal’s/Promoter’s Bad Faith Liability for Real Estate Broker’s Commission
candidates.definition: The liability of a principal or promoter for a real estate broker's commission due to bad faith, where the principal or promoter acted with dishonesty or deceit in the transaction, resulting in the broker not receiving the commission they were entitled to.
synonyms: Haftung des Auftraggebers / Förderers aus bösem Glauben für die Maklerprovision im Immobilienbereich
Principal’s/Promoter’s Bad Faith Liability for Real Estate Broker’s Commission
Responsabilidad de mala fe del promotor/principal por la comisión del corredor de bienes raíces
Responsabilidade de má-fé do mandante / promotor pela comissão do corretor imobiliário
Responsabilité de mauvaise foi du mandant / promoteur pour la commission de courtier immobilier
אחריות רע בשל הפקדת עמלת תיווך נכסים של המתווך על המפיץ/המקדם
वास्तु संपदा दलाल की आयोजक/प्रधान की बुरी नीयत की जिम्मेदारी के लिए
不動産ブローカーの手数料に対する委託者/プロモーターの悪意に基づく責任
房地产经纪人佣金的委托人/推广者恶意责任
