candidates.definition: A professional limited liability company ('PLLC') is a U.S. business entity designed for licensed professionals, such as lawyers, doctors, architects, engineers, accountants, and chiropractors. While many businesses choose to form a limited liability company ('LLC') because of the tax, limited liability, and other benefits, some states don't allow LLCs to be owned by professionals whose occupation requires a license. In these states, licensed professionals who want the benefits of an LLC must form a PLLC instead.
synonyms: Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung für Freiberufler
プロフェッショナル有限責任会社

candidates.label: Evidence Management
candidates.definition: Evidence Management refers to the process of collecting, organizing, and preserving evidence in a legal case, with the aim of ensuring its admissibility and reliability in court.

candidates.label: Strict Liability
candidates.definition: Strict liability refers to a legal doctrine where a party is held responsible for their actions or the consequences of those actions, regardless of intent or negligence. This means that the person or entity is liable for damages even if they didn't intend to cause harm or weren't negligent in their actions.

candidates.label: City Liability
candidates.definition: City Liability refers to the legal responsibility of a municipality for any harm or damage caused to individuals or property due to the actions or negligence of the city or its employees.

candidates.label: Agent Liability
candidates.definition: Agent Liability refers to the legal responsibility of an individual or entity acting as an agent for another party, for any harm caused to a third party due to their negligence or breach of fiduciary duty.

candidates.label: Family Liability
candidates.definition: Family Liability refers to the legal responsibility of family members for the actions or debts of other family members, including spousal and parent-child liability, as well as agency and vicarious liability.

candidates.label: Spousal Liability
candidates.definition: Spousal Liability refers to the legal responsibility of one spouse for the debts incurred by the other spouse.

candidates.label: Elevator Liability
candidates.definition: A legal claim for damages resulting from injuries or harm suffered by individuals while using elevators or escalators.

candidates.label: Education Liability
candidates.definition: Education Liability refers to the legal responsibility and accountability of educational institutions and their staff for any harm or injury caused to students or their families, including civil claims related to privacy of school records, corporal punishment, compulsory education, and prayer in public school.

candidates.label: Corrections Liability
candidates.definition: The legal responsibility for damages or harm caused by a correctional facility or its employees, including but not limited to negligence, abuse, or mistreatment of inmates.

candidates.label: Social Host Liability
candidates.definition: The legal concept of Social Host Liability refers to the responsibility of a person who provides alcohol to others, particularly minors, and can be held liable for any harm or damages caused by the intoxicated individuals.

candidates.label: Physician
candidates.definition: A physician or doctor is a person who is licensed to practice medicine and provide medical treatment to patients.

candidates.label: Medical Expert
candidates.definition: A person who holds a health care professional degree from a university or college, who has engaged in the practice of that profession, and who has knowledge or skill about the subject upon which he is called to testify or provide an opinion. See Fla. Stat. § 766.202(5); Fort Walton Beach Medical Center, Inc. v. Dingler, 697 So.2d 575 (Fla. App. 1997).

candidates.label: Patient
candidates.definition: A Patient is an individual who receives medical care, treatment, or other health services from a healthcare provider or institution. This person is under professional care due to illness, injury, or for preventive health measures, and is the recipient of medical attention, diagnosis, and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Treating Physician
candidates.definition: Physician who has treated a patient.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Governmental Civil Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Civil Law Division is a governmental entity that handles civil legal matters involving the government. It provides legal advice to government agencies, represents the government in civil litigation, and ensures compliance with civil laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Mental Health Expert
candidates.definition: An expert in the field of mental health.
synonyms: メンタルヘルス専門家

candidates.label: Health Care Ombudsman
candidates.definition: When a health care business files for bankruptcy, the ombudsman appointed to monitor the quality of patient care, representing the interests of the patients. Bankruptcy Code §333(a)(1)

candidates.label: Provider Level
candidates.definition: Provider Level refers to the classification of a person who provides legal services, which can be further categorized as either a timekeeper or non-timekeeper level.

candidates.label: Provider Last Name
candidates.definition: The concept of "Provider Last Name" refers to the surname or family name of an individual who provides legal services as a person, within the broader category of Legal Services Provider.

candidates.label: Negligence and Malpractice Law
candidates.definition: Failure to exercise the care that a reasonably prudent person or professional, in the case of professional negligence, would exercise in like circumstances and the harm caused by those actions.
synonyms: Derecho de negligencia y mala praxis
Derecho de negligencia y mala práctica
Droit de la négligence et de la faute professionnelle

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Constitutional and Civil Rights Law
candidates.definition: Rights that protect individuals' freedom from infringement by governments, social organizations, and private individuals.
synonyms: Derecho Constitucional y de Derechos Civiles
Direito Constitucional e dos Direitos Civis
Droit constitutionnel et des droits civils

candidates.label: Public Health and Welfare Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to the authority of the government at various jurisdictional levels to improve the health of the general population within societal limits and norms.
synonyms: Direito da Saúde e Bem-Estar Público
Droit de la santé et de la protection sociale
Ley de Salud Pública y Bienestar
Ley de Salud Pública y Bienestar Social

candidates.label: Employment Health and Safety Law
candidates.definition: Law governing the health and safety of employees.
synonyms: Droit de la santé et de la sécurité au travail
Lei de Saúde e Segurança no Trabalho
Ley de Salud y Seguridad en el Empleo

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Medical - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate property buildings, offices, and campuses leased to members or organizations within the healthcare community.

candidates.label: Hospitality - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate used in the hospitality industry including hotels, travel centers, water parks, amusement facilities, golf courses, etc.
synonyms: ホスピタリティ - 不動産資産
酒店业 - 房地产资产

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Personal Care Products
candidates.definition: In the context of bankruptcies and monthly spending budgets, Personal Care Products refer to non-medical goods purchased for hygiene or beautification, such as toiletries, cosmetics, and grooming supplies. These are generally considered non-discretionary expenses, but their allocation may be scrutinized during a bankruptcy process to determine reasonable living expenses for the debtor

candidates.label: DOC
candidates.definition: A Microsoft Word document file from Microsoft Office 2003 or earlier.

candidates.label: Medical Test
candidates.definition: A Medical Test is a clinical procedure performed to detect, diagnose, or monitor diseases, disease processes, susceptibility, or to determine a course of treatment. Examples include blood tests, DNA tests, drug tests, and paternity tests.

candidates.label: Medical Form
candidates.definition: A Medical Form is a standardized document used within healthcare settings for collecting, storing, or conveying information related to a patient's medical history, condition, or treatment. Examples include consent forms, referral forms, and records release forms.

candidates.label: Medical Record
candidates.definition: A medical record is a comprehensive and confidential document that contains a patient's medical history, including information about their medical conditions, diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, and interactions with healthcare providers. These records are typically maintained by healthcare institutions and practitioners to track and manage a patient's health and treatment over time, and they are essential for providing continuity of care and ensuring accurate medical documentation.

candidates.label: Emergency Medical Assistance Coverage
candidates.definition: This refers to immediate healthcare services provided in urgent or life-threatening situations. Coverage for emergency medical assistance is often a component of general health insurance policies and is also commonly included in travel insurance plans.

hierarchy_path.label: Subsidized Healthcare Coverage Policy for Low-Income Groups

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: Civil Cover Sheet
candidates.definition: A Civil Cover Sheet is a document used in trial court practice to provide basic information about a civil case, such as the parties involved and the nature of the case.
synonyms: 民事訴訟カバーシート

candidates.label: Health Care Directive
candidates.definition: A Health Care Directive is a legal document that outlines an individual's wishes regarding medical treatment and end-of-life care in the event they become unable to make decisions for themselves.

hierarchy_path.label: Document Collections by Areas of Law

candidates.label: Health Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: A health insurance policy is an agreement between an insurer and a policyholder wherein the insurer covers part or all of the policyholder's healthcare costs in exchange for premium payments. The policy specifies what medical services are covered, the network of healthcare providers, and any co-payments, deductibles, or other financial responsibilities for the insured.

candidates.label: M.D. (Doctor of Medicine)
candidates.definition: The suffix "M.D." stands for "Doctor of Medicine" and is used after a person's name to indicate that they have completed the requisite training and education to practice medicine. This suffix signifies the individual's medical credentials and is commonly used in healthcare settings, academic environments, and formal documents.

candidates.label: Litigation Identifiers
candidates.definition: "Information that denotes a litigation matter."

candidates.label: Litigation Support Vendors Expense
candidates.definition: Expenses incurred by vendors who provide support services to parties involved in a legal dispute, such as document management, e-discovery, and trial presentation services.
synonyms: Despesas de fornecedores de suporte à litigação
Frais de prestataires de soutien à la litige
Gastos de proveedores de apoyo a litigios
訴訟サポートベンダー費用

candidates.label: Insured Matter
candidates.definition: An insured matter refers to the specific risk or subject covered under an insurance policy, including the circumstances, conditions, and objects that the insurance agreement protects against.

candidates.label: Matter Net Fees
candidates.definition: Matter Net Fees are the fees charged for legal services provided in a specific matter, after deducting any expenses or disbursements incurred by the lawyer.

candidates.label: Matter Total Due
candidates.definition: The Matter Total Due is a specific detail within the broader category of Engagement Fee Details, referring to the total amount owed for legal services provided in a particular matter.

candidates.label: Matter Final Bill
candidates.definition: A "Matter Final Bill" is a detailed record of the final charges for legal services provided in a specific matter, and is a subcategory of the broader concept of "Engagement Fee Detail".

candidates.label: Matter Description
candidates.definition: The textual description of the work performed in the billing period.

candidates.label: Matter Tax on Fees
candidates.definition: A Matter Tax on Fees is a type of Tax Detail and Engagement Fee Detail that refers to a tax imposed on the fees charged for legal services provided in a specific matter or case.
synonyms: Impuesto sobre la materia gravable de las tarifas
Taxe sur les frais de dossier

candidates.label: Matter Net Expenses
candidates.definition: Matter Net Expenses refers to the costs incurred by a law firm in providing legal services to a client, which are then passed on to the client as part of the engagement fee.

candidates.label: Medical Discharge
candidates.definition: A Military Medical Discharge refers to the release of a service member from active duty due to a physical or mental condition that impairs their ability to perform their military duties. The condition may be due to an illness, injury, or a mental health issue. Depending on the severity and duration of the condition, the service member might receive benefits and compensation based on their level of disability.

candidates.label: Clinical Trial
candidates.definition: A Clinical Trial is a research study conducted with human participants to evaluate the safety, efficacy, and side effects of new medical treatments, drugs, or interventions. These trials are essential for determining whether new therapies should be approved for use in the general population.

candidates.label: Civil Arrest
candidates.definition: The concept of Civil Arrest refers to the act of detaining a person by legal authority in order to enforce a civil judgment against them.

candidates.label: Litigation Events
candidates.definition: Events occurring during the course of disputes.

candidates.label: Administrative Discharge
candidates.definition: An Administrative Discharge refers to the release of a service member from their military obligations without the involvement of a court-martial. It is based on administrative reasons rather than punitive ones. This type of discharge can be classified into various categories, such as honorable, other than honorable, or general, based on the circumstances and reasons for the separation. Administrative discharges can result from factors like medical conditions, patterns of misconduct, or other non-judicial reasons.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Litigation Hold Event
candidates.definition: A Litigation Hold Event is a specific event within the context of a legal dispute where a party is required to preserve relevant evidence.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Claims
candidates.definition: Ct. Cl.

candidates.label: Copyright Claims Board
candidates.definition: A Copyright Claims Board (CCB) is a specialized administrative body within the United States Copyright Office that is responsible for resolving disputes related to copyright claims.
synonyms: Conseil des revendications de droits d'auteur
Conselho de Reivindicações de Direitos Autorais
Junta de Reclamaciones de Derechos de Autor
Junta de Resolución de Reclamaciones de Derechos de Autor
著作権請求審査委員会

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Federal Claims
candidates.definition: Fed. Cl.

candidates.label: Civil Court
candidates.definition: A Civil Court is a type of court forum that has jurisdiction over non-criminal cases involving private disputes between individuals or organizations.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court
candidates.definition: A court in New York State that handles civil legal matters.

candidates.label: New York City - Civil Court
candidates.definition: N.Y. City Civ. Ct.

candidates.label: Texas Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: The Texas Court of Civil Appeals is a specific court within the Texas State Courts system that hears appeals in civil cases.

candidates.label: Alabama Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Ala. Civ. App.

candidates.label: Oklahoma Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Okla. Civ. App.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County
candidates.definition: A court located in Bronx County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County

candidates.label: Civil Rights Center
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor Civil Rights Center (CRC) develops, administers, and enforces Departmental policies, practices, and procedures pursuant to Title VI and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Section 188 of the Workforce Investment Act (WIA), as amended; the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended; the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, as amended; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; the Equal Pay Act of 1963; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, as amended; Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, as amended; the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978; the Civil Rights Act of 1991; the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation (No FEAR) Act; and related statutes and Executive Orders.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Division
candidates.definition: The Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice, created in 1957 by the enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1957, works to uphold the civil and constitutional rights of all Americans, particularly some of the most vulnerable members of our society. The Division enforces federal statutes prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, disability, religion, familial status and national origin.

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
candidates.definition: The Office for Civil Rights (OCR) is a sub-agency of the U.S. Department of Education that is primarily focused on enforcing civil rights laws prohibiting schools from engaging in discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or membership in patriotic youth organizations.
synonyms: Bureau des droits civils - Ministère de l'Éducation
Escritório de Direitos Civis - Departamento de Educação
Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
Oficina de Derechos Civiles - Departamento de Educación
市民権事務局 - 教育省
民权办公室 - 教育部

hierarchy_path.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - HHS
candidates.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) enforces federal civil rights laws, conscience and religious freedom laws, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules, and the Patient Safety Act and Rule, which together protect fundamental rights of nondiscrimination, conscience, religious freedom, and health information privacy.

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
candidates.definition: The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is a bipartisan, independent commission of the United States federal government, created in 1957, that is charged with the responsibility for investigating, reporting on, and making recommendations concerning civil rights issues in the United States.
synonyms: Comisión de Derechos Civiles de los Estados Unidos
Comissão de Direitos Civis dos EUA
アメリカ合衆国市民権委員会

candidates.label: Defense Health Agency
candidates.definition: The Defense Health Agency (DHA) is a joint, integrated Combat Support Agency that enables the U.S. Army, U.S. Navy, and U.S. Air Force medical services to provide a medically ready force and ready medical force to Combatant Commands in both peacetime and wartime.
synonyms: Agence de santé de la défense

candidates.label: Indian Health Service
candidates.definition: The Indian Health Service (IHS) is an operating division (OPDIV) within the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Public Health Service
candidates.definition: The mission of the U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps is to protect, promote, and advance the health and safety of the United States.
synonyms: Service de santé publique des États-Unis
Servicio de Salud Pública de EE. UU.
Servicio de Salud Pública de los EE. UU.
Serviço de Saúde Pública dos EUA
השירות לבריאות הציבור של ארצות הברית

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: UK National Health Service
candidates.definition: The UK National Health Service (NHS) is a publicly funded healthcare system in the United Kingdom, providing comprehensive medical services to residents. Established to offer free at the point of use healthcare, the NHS administers services including hospitals, general practitioners, dental care, and specialized treatments, ensuring accessible health care across the country.

candidates.label: Veterans Health Administration
candidates.definition: The Veterans Health Administration (VHA) is the component of the United States Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) led by the Under Secretary of Veterans Affairs for Health that implements the healthcare program of the VA through the administration and operation of numerous VA Medical Centers (VAMC), Outpatient Clinics (OPC), Community Based Outpatient Clinics (CBOC), and VA Community Living Centers (VA Nursing Home) Programs.
synonyms: 退伍军人医疗保健管理局

candidates.label: Medical Laboratories
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments known as medical laboratories primarily engaged in providing analytic or diagnostic services, including body fluid analysis, generally to the medical profession or to the patient on referral from a health practitioner.

candidates.label: Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting (i.e., assuming the risk and assigning premiums) health and medical insurance policies. Group hospitalization plans and HMO establishments that provide health and medical insurance policies without providing health care services are included in this industry.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers
Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers

candidates.label: Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting (i.e., assuming the risk and assigning premiums) annuities and life insurance policies, disability income insurance policies, accidental death and dismemberment insurance policies, and health and medical insurance policies.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers

candidates.label: Home Health Equipment Rental
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in renting home-type health and invalid equipment, such as wheelchairs, hospital beds, oxygen tanks, walkers, and crutches.

hierarchy_path.label: Real Estate, Rental and Leasing Industry

candidates.label: Voluntary Health Organizations
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in raising funds for health related research, such as disease (e.g., heart, cancer, diabetes) prevention, health education, and patient services.

hierarchy_path.label: Religious, Grantmaking, Civic, Professional, and Similar Organizations

candidates.label: Health-Conscious Market Segment
candidates.definition: The health-conscious segment refers to a portion of the market that is characterized by consumers who prioritize health and wellness in their purchasing decisions. This segment includes products and services that are designed to promote physical, mental, and emotional well-being, catering to individuals who seek a healthy lifestyle.

candidates.label: Food (Health) Supplement Retailers
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing food supplement products, such as vitamins, nutrition supplements, and body enhancing supplements.

hierarchy_path.label: Health and Personal Care Retailers Industry
Other Health and Personal Care Retailers

candidates.label: Other Ambulatory Health Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing ambulatory health care services (except offices of physicians, dentists, and other health practitioners; outpatient care centers; medical laboratories and diagnostic imaging centers; and home health care providers).

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: NULL

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing care and early learning opportunities for infants and children. These establishments generally care for children from birth through school age and may also offer pre-kindergarten, kindergarten, and/or before- or after-school educational programs. The care and early learning provided by these establishments may include opportunities for development in health, social and emotional learning, and family engagement.

candidates.label: Civil Law System
candidates.definition: Civil Law is a legal system that is primarily based on written codes and statutes, rather than case law. This system emphasizes comprehensive sets of laws that are codified and accessible, guiding judges in applying the law to individual cases.

candidates.label: Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: A court's body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing Multidistrict Litigation (MDLs).
synonyms: Regeln des Justizausschusses für multidisziplinäre Rechtsstreitigkeiten
Regras do Painel Judicial de Litígios Multidistritais
Règles du Panel judiciaire sur les litiges multidistricts
多区域诉讼司法小组规则
多地区訴訟司法パネルの規則

hierarchy_path.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation

candidates.label: Civil Union
candidates.definition: A civil union is a legally recognized arrangement similar to marriage, which provides some of the legal and social protections afforded to married couples. However, it may lack some of the federal benefits and recognition that come with marriage.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Partnership
candidates.definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

candidates.label: Tort Claimants Committee
candidates.definition: A Tort Claimants Committee is typically established within bankruptcy proceedings or large-scale litigation contexts to represent the interests of individuals who have tort claims against the debtor or defendant. This committee works to ensure that the rights and recovery opportunities of tort claimants are protected and maximized during the legal process.

candidates.label: Benefit Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A Benefit Limited Liability Company (BLLC) is a type of limited liability company that combines the liability protection of a traditional LLC with a commitment to pursuing public benefits alongside profit. These companies are structured to focus on social and environmental goals while still operating as for-profit entities.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Sole Proprietorship
candidates.definition: A sole proprietorship, also known as the sole trader, individual entrepreneurship or proprietorship, is a type of enterprise that is owned and run by one person and in which there is no legal distinction between the owner and the business entity. The sole trader receives all profits (subject to taxation specific to the business) and has unlimited responsibility for all losses and debts. Every asset of the business is owned by the proprietor and all debts of the business are the proprietor's. It is a "sole" proprietorship in contrast with partnerships (which have at least two owners).

candidates.label: Partnership
candidates.definition: A partnership is a type of legal entity where two or more individuals or entities come together to carry out a business venture, sharing profits and losses.

candidates.label: Litigation Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Management involves the management of various aspects of a legal case, including but not limited to, retention and litigation hold management, redaction management, expert witness management, brief analysis management, jury selection management, citation check management, remote trial management, eDiscovery services, exhibit management, and costs and settlement management.

candidates.label: Litigation Finance Management
candidates.definition: Litigation finance management refers to the process of managing the financial aspects of a legal case, including funding, budgeting, and accounting, in order to maximize the potential return on investment for the parties involved.

candidates.label: Litigation Analytics Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Analytics Management is the systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and utilizing data and insights from legal disputes to inform and improve decision-making, strategy, and performance in legal practice, often through the use of specialized software and tools.

candidates.label: Litigation Collaboration Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Collaboration Management refers to the process of managing and coordinating the efforts of multiple parties involved in a legal dispute, with the goal of achieving a successful outcome.

candidates.label: Retention and Litigation Hold Management
candidates.definition: Retention and Litigation Hold Management refers to the process of preserving and managing electronic data that may be relevant to a legal matter, in order to comply with legal obligations and avoid spoliation sanctions.

candidates.label: Matter Management
candidates.definition: Matter Management refers to the process of organizing and overseeing legal cases and related tasks, such as document management and budgeting, within a legal operations framework.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A textual narrative of a matter's factual and legal details.

candidates.label: Matter Complexity
candidates.definition: Classification for a given' matter's complexity, either textually (e.g., low, medium, high) or numerically (e.g., 1 through 10).

candidates.label: Public Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A public version of a matter narrative.

candidates.label: Generic Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A generic version of a matter narrative. Usually used to further describe a category of matters.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Audience
candidates.definition: A Matter Narrative Audience is a group or individual for whom the factual and legal details of a matter are presented in a textual narrative.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Description
candidates.definition: A textual narrative description, detailing a matter's factual and legal details.

candidates.label: Confidential Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A confidential version of a matter narrative.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Format
candidates.definition: The file format containing the narrative description of a matter.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice: Negligence or breach of duty by a healthcare professional that results in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Malpractice Claims refer to civil claims of negligence or malfeasance brought against a professional or tradesperson, such as a doctor, lawyer, accountant, or architect, for failing to meet the standard of care expected in their field.

candidates.label: Legal Malpractice
candidates.definition: Legal Malpractice is the failure of an attorney to provide competent and professional legal services to a client, resulting in harm or damages to the client.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Claims refer to legal claims made against medical professionals or institutions for negligence or failure to provide adequate care resulting in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Defenses
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Defenses are legal arguments or strategies used by defendants in a medical malpractice case to defend against the plaintiff's claims of negligence or wrongdoing.
synonyms: Defesas em casos de negligência médica
Défenses en cas de faute médicale

candidates.label: Medical Device Malpractice
candidates.definition: Medical Device Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that arises from the use of a faulty or defective medical device during a medical procedure.
synonyms: Faute professionnelle liée aux dispositifs médicaux

candidates.label: Pathology Malpractice
candidates.definition: Pathology Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that arises from errors or negligence in the examination and diagnosis of tissue samples and bodily fluids by pathologists.

candidates.label: Pediatrics Malpractice
candidates.definition: Pediatrics Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that involves the negligent or wrongful actions of healthcare providers in the treatment of children.

candidates.label: Psychiatric Malpractice
candidates.definition: Psychiatric Malpractice refers to the negligence or misconduct of a mental health professional, such as a psychiatrist, which results in harm or injury to a patient, and falls under the broader category of Medical Malpractice Claims.

candidates.label: Urogynecology Malpractice
candidates.definition: Urogynecology Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that involves injuries or harm caused by the negligence or misconduct of a urogynecologist, who specializes in the treatment of pelvic floor disorders in women.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Civil Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Family Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Family Health Public Benefit refers to government programs specifically designed to provide healthcare coverage or financial assistance for medical needs to families. These programs aim to ensure that both adults and children within a family unit have access to necessary healthcare services

candidates.label: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit refers to specialized government programs aimed at providing healthcare services or financial assistance specifically for mothers and their children. These may cover prenatal care, childbirth, postnatal care, and pediatric services to ensure the health and well-being of both mother and child. In the United States, one such program is the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

hierarchy_path.label: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit

candidates.label: Provider of Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Provider of public benefits refers to the governmental entity responsible for administering and disbursing public assistance programs. These can be national, state, or local agencies, each offering different types of benefits tailored to the needs and eligibility criteria of their jurisdiction.

candidates.label: Litigation Practice
candidates.definition: Matters that include official proceedings (e.g., court case, arbitration, mediation)

candidates.label: Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Health public benefit refers to government programs designed to provide medical services or financial assistance for healthcare needs to eligible individuals. This can include programs like Medicaid in the United States, which offers healthcare coverage to low-income families and individuals.

candidates.label: Dispute Service
candidates.definition: A process that describes a conflict/adversarial event potentially or actually involving a second party. This can include government entity or internal investigation

candidates.label: Mediation Practice
candidates.definition: Mediation is a process wherein the parties meet with a mutually selected impartial and neutral person who assists them in the negotiation of their differences. Mediation leaves the decision power totally and strictly with the parties.

candidates.label: Provider Matter ID
candidates.definition: The provider's matter ID number.

candidates.label: Provider Identifier
candidates.definition: Identifier (e.g., IRIs, UUIDs, GUIDs) of the Provider (e.g., law firm, ALSP) within the systems of stakeholders (e.g., vendors, sellers, buyers, courts).

candidates.label: Litigation Docket Number
candidates.definition: A Litigation Docket Number is a unique identifier assigned to a legal case that is used to track and manage the case within the court system.

candidates.label: Physician
candidates.definition: A physician or doctor is a person who is licensed to practice medicine and provide medical treatment to patients.

candidates.label: Medical Expert
candidates.definition: A person who holds a health care professional degree from a university or college, who has engaged in the practice of that profession, and who has knowledge or skill about the subject upon which he is called to testify or provide an opinion. See Fla. Stat. § 766.202(5); Fort Walton Beach Medical Center, Inc. v. Dingler, 697 So.2d 575 (Fla. App. 1997).

candidates.label: Patient
candidates.definition: A Patient is an individual who receives medical care, treatment, or other health services from a healthcare provider or institution. This person is under professional care due to illness, injury, or for preventive health measures, and is the recipient of medical attention, diagnosis, and therapeutic interventions.

candidates.label: Treating Physician
candidates.definition: Physician who has treated a patient.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Governmental Civil Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Civil Law Division is a governmental entity that handles civil legal matters involving the government. It provides legal advice to government agencies, represents the government in civil litigation, and ensures compliance with civil laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Mental Health Expert
candidates.definition: An expert in the field of mental health.
synonyms: メンタルヘルス専門家

candidates.label: Health Care Ombudsman
candidates.definition: When a health care business files for bankruptcy, the ombudsman appointed to monitor the quality of patient care, representing the interests of the patients. Bankruptcy Code §333(a)(1)

candidates.label: Provider Level
candidates.definition: Provider Level refers to the classification of a person who provides legal services, which can be further categorized as either a timekeeper or non-timekeeper level.

candidates.label: Provider Last Name
candidates.definition: The concept of "Provider Last Name" refers to the surname or family name of an individual who provides legal services as a person, within the broader category of Legal Services Provider.

candidates.label: Negligence and Malpractice Law
candidates.definition: Failure to exercise the care that a reasonably prudent person or professional, in the case of professional negligence, would exercise in like circumstances and the harm caused by those actions.
synonyms: Derecho de negligencia y mala praxis
Derecho de negligencia y mala práctica
Droit de la négligence et de la faute professionnelle

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Constitutional and Civil Rights Law
candidates.definition: Rights that protect individuals' freedom from infringement by governments, social organizations, and private individuals.
synonyms: Derecho Constitucional y de Derechos Civiles
Direito Constitucional e dos Direitos Civis
Droit constitutionnel et des droits civils

candidates.label: Public Health and Welfare Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to the authority of the government at various jurisdictional levels to improve the health of the general population within societal limits and norms.
synonyms: Direito da Saúde e Bem-Estar Público
Droit de la santé et de la protection sociale
Ley de Salud Pública y Bienestar
Ley de Salud Pública y Bienestar Social

candidates.label: Employment Health and Safety Law
candidates.definition: Law governing the health and safety of employees.
synonyms: Droit de la santé et de la sécurité au travail
Lei de Saúde e Segurança no Trabalho
Ley de Salud y Seguridad en el Empleo

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Medical - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate property buildings, offices, and campuses leased to members or organizations within the healthcare community.

candidates.label: Hospitality - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate used in the hospitality industry including hotels, travel centers, water parks, amusement facilities, golf courses, etc.
synonyms: ホスピタリティ - 不動産資産
酒店业 - 房地产资产

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Personal Care Products
candidates.definition: In the context of bankruptcies and monthly spending budgets, Personal Care Products refer to non-medical goods purchased for hygiene or beautification, such as toiletries, cosmetics, and grooming supplies. These are generally considered non-discretionary expenses, but their allocation may be scrutinized during a bankruptcy process to determine reasonable living expenses for the debtor

candidates.label: DOC
candidates.definition: A Microsoft Word document file from Microsoft Office 2003 or earlier.

candidates.label: Medical Test
candidates.definition: A Medical Test is a clinical procedure performed to detect, diagnose, or monitor diseases, disease processes, susceptibility, or to determine a course of treatment. Examples include blood tests, DNA tests, drug tests, and paternity tests.

candidates.label: Medical Form
candidates.definition: A Medical Form is a standardized document used within healthcare settings for collecting, storing, or conveying information related to a patient's medical history, condition, or treatment. Examples include consent forms, referral forms, and records release forms.

candidates.label: Medical Record
candidates.definition: A medical record is a comprehensive and confidential document that contains a patient's medical history, including information about their medical conditions, diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, and interactions with healthcare providers. These records are typically maintained by healthcare institutions and practitioners to track and manage a patient's health and treatment over time, and they are essential for providing continuity of care and ensuring accurate medical documentation.

candidates.label: Emergency Medical Assistance Coverage
candidates.definition: This refers to immediate healthcare services provided in urgent or life-threatening situations. Coverage for emergency medical assistance is often a component of general health insurance policies and is also commonly included in travel insurance plans.

hierarchy_path.label: Subsidized Healthcare Coverage Policy for Low-Income Groups

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: Civil Cover Sheet
candidates.definition: A Civil Cover Sheet is a document used in trial court practice to provide basic information about a civil case, such as the parties involved and the nature of the case.
synonyms: 民事訴訟カバーシート

candidates.label: Health Care Directive
candidates.definition: A Health Care Directive is a legal document that outlines an individual's wishes regarding medical treatment and end-of-life care in the event they become unable to make decisions for themselves.

hierarchy_path.label: Document Collections by Areas of Law

candidates.label: Health Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: A health insurance policy is an agreement between an insurer and a policyholder wherein the insurer covers part or all of the policyholder's healthcare costs in exchange for premium payments. The policy specifies what medical services are covered, the network of healthcare providers, and any co-payments, deductibles, or other financial responsibilities for the insured.

candidates.label: M.D. (Doctor of Medicine)
candidates.definition: The suffix "M.D." stands for "Doctor of Medicine" and is used after a person's name to indicate that they have completed the requisite training and education to practice medicine. This suffix signifies the individual's medical credentials and is commonly used in healthcare settings, academic environments, and formal documents.

candidates.label: Litigation Identifiers
candidates.definition: "Information that denotes a litigation matter."

candidates.label: Litigation Support Vendors Expense
candidates.definition: Expenses incurred by vendors who provide support services to parties involved in a legal dispute, such as document management, e-discovery, and trial presentation services.
synonyms: Despesas de fornecedores de suporte à litigação
Frais de prestataires de soutien à la litige
Gastos de proveedores de apoyo a litigios
訴訟サポートベンダー費用

candidates.label: Insured Matter
candidates.definition: An insured matter refers to the specific risk or subject covered under an insurance policy, including the circumstances, conditions, and objects that the insurance agreement protects against.

candidates.label: Matter Net Fees
candidates.definition: Matter Net Fees are the fees charged for legal services provided in a specific matter, after deducting any expenses or disbursements incurred by the lawyer.

candidates.label: Matter Total Due
candidates.definition: The Matter Total Due is a specific detail within the broader category of Engagement Fee Details, referring to the total amount owed for legal services provided in a particular matter.

candidates.label: Matter Final Bill
candidates.definition: A "Matter Final Bill" is a detailed record of the final charges for legal services provided in a specific matter, and is a subcategory of the broader concept of "Engagement Fee Detail".

candidates.label: Matter Description
candidates.definition: The textual description of the work performed in the billing period.

candidates.label: Matter Tax on Fees
candidates.definition: A Matter Tax on Fees is a type of Tax Detail and Engagement Fee Detail that refers to a tax imposed on the fees charged for legal services provided in a specific matter or case.
synonyms: Impuesto sobre la materia gravable de las tarifas
Taxe sur les frais de dossier

candidates.label: Matter Net Expenses
candidates.definition: Matter Net Expenses refers to the costs incurred by a law firm in providing legal services to a client, which are then passed on to the client as part of the engagement fee.

candidates.label: Medical Discharge
candidates.definition: A Military Medical Discharge refers to the release of a service member from active duty due to a physical or mental condition that impairs their ability to perform their military duties. The condition may be due to an illness, injury, or a mental health issue. Depending on the severity and duration of the condition, the service member might receive benefits and compensation based on their level of disability.

candidates.label: Clinical Trial
candidates.definition: A Clinical Trial is a research study conducted with human participants to evaluate the safety, efficacy, and side effects of new medical treatments, drugs, or interventions. These trials are essential for determining whether new therapies should be approved for use in the general population.

candidates.label: Civil Arrest
candidates.definition: The concept of Civil Arrest refers to the act of detaining a person by legal authority in order to enforce a civil judgment against them.

candidates.label: Litigation Events
candidates.definition: Events occurring during the course of disputes.

candidates.label: Administrative Discharge
candidates.definition: An Administrative Discharge refers to the release of a service member from their military obligations without the involvement of a court-martial. It is based on administrative reasons rather than punitive ones. This type of discharge can be classified into various categories, such as honorable, other than honorable, or general, based on the circumstances and reasons for the separation. Administrative discharges can result from factors like medical conditions, patterns of misconduct, or other non-judicial reasons.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Litigation Hold Event
candidates.definition: A Litigation Hold Event is a specific event within the context of a legal dispute where a party is required to preserve relevant evidence.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Claims
candidates.definition: Ct. Cl.

candidates.label: Copyright Claims Board
candidates.definition: A Copyright Claims Board (CCB) is a specialized administrative body within the United States Copyright Office that is responsible for resolving disputes related to copyright claims.
synonyms: Conseil des revendications de droits d'auteur
Conselho de Reivindicações de Direitos Autorais
Junta de Reclamaciones de Derechos de Autor
Junta de Resolución de Reclamaciones de Derechos de Autor
著作権請求審査委員会

candidates.label: U.S. Court of Federal Claims
candidates.definition: Fed. Cl.

candidates.label: Civil Court
candidates.definition: A Civil Court is a type of court forum that has jurisdiction over non-criminal cases involving private disputes between individuals or organizations.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court
candidates.definition: A court in New York State that handles civil legal matters.

candidates.label: New York City - Civil Court
candidates.definition: N.Y. City Civ. Ct.

candidates.label: Texas Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: The Texas Court of Civil Appeals is a specific court within the Texas State Courts system that hears appeals in civil cases.

candidates.label: Alabama Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Ala. Civ. App.

candidates.label: Oklahoma Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Okla. Civ. App.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County
candidates.definition: A court located in Bronx County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County

candidates.label: Civil Rights Center
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor Civil Rights Center (CRC) develops, administers, and enforces Departmental policies, practices, and procedures pursuant to Title VI and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Section 188 of the Workforce Investment Act (WIA), as amended; the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended; the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, as amended; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; the Equal Pay Act of 1963; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, as amended; Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, as amended; the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978; the Civil Rights Act of 1991; the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation (No FEAR) Act; and related statutes and Executive Orders.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Division
candidates.definition: The Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice, created in 1957 by the enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1957, works to uphold the civil and constitutional rights of all Americans, particularly some of the most vulnerable members of our society. The Division enforces federal statutes prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, disability, religion, familial status and national origin.

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
candidates.definition: The Office for Civil Rights (OCR) is a sub-agency of the U.S. Department of Education that is primarily focused on enforcing civil rights laws prohibiting schools from engaging in discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or membership in patriotic youth organizations.
synonyms: Bureau des droits civils - Ministère de l'Éducation
Escritório de Direitos Civis - Departamento de Educação
Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
Oficina de Derechos Civiles - Departamento de Educación
市民権事務局 - 教育省
民权办公室 - 教育部

hierarchy_path.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - HHS
candidates.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) enforces federal civil rights laws, conscience and religious freedom laws, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules, and the Patient Safety Act and Rule, which together protect fundamental rights of nondiscrimination, conscience, religious freedom, and health information privacy.

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
candidates.definition: The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is a bipartisan, independent commission of the United States federal government, created in 1957, that is charged with the responsibility for investigating, reporting on, and making recommendations concerning civil rights issues in the United States.
synonyms: Comisión de Derechos Civiles de los Estados Unidos
Comissão de Direitos Civis dos EUA
アメリカ合衆国市民権委員会

candidates.label: Defense Health Agency
candidates.definition: The Defense Health Agency (DHA) is a joint, integrated Combat Support Agency that enables the U.S. Army, U.S. Navy, and U.S. Air Force medical services to provide a medically ready force and ready medical force to Combatant Commands in both peacetime and wartime.
synonyms: Agence de santé de la défense

candidates.label: Indian Health Service
candidates.definition: The Indian Health Service (IHS) is an operating division (OPDIV) within the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS).

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Public Health Service
candidates.definition: The mission of the U.S. Public Health Service Commissioned Corps is to protect, promote, and advance the health and safety of the United States.
synonyms: Service de santé publique des États-Unis
Servicio de Salud Pública de EE. UU.
Servicio de Salud Pública de los EE. UU.
Serviço de Saúde Pública dos EUA
השירות לבריאות הציבור של ארצות הברית

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: UK National Health Service
candidates.definition: The UK National Health Service (NHS) is a publicly funded healthcare system in the United Kingdom, providing comprehensive medical services to residents. Established to offer free at the point of use healthcare, the NHS administers services including hospitals, general practitioners, dental care, and specialized treatments, ensuring accessible health care across the country.

candidates.label: Veterans Health Administration
candidates.definition: The Veterans Health Administration (VHA) is the component of the United States Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) led by the Under Secretary of Veterans Affairs for Health that implements the healthcare program of the VA through the administration and operation of numerous VA Medical Centers (VAMC), Outpatient Clinics (OPC), Community Based Outpatient Clinics (CBOC), and VA Community Living Centers (VA Nursing Home) Programs.
synonyms: 退伍军人医疗保健管理局

candidates.label: Medical Laboratories
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments known as medical laboratories primarily engaged in providing analytic or diagnostic services, including body fluid analysis, generally to the medical profession or to the patient on referral from a health practitioner.

candidates.label: Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting (i.e., assuming the risk and assigning premiums) health and medical insurance policies. Group hospitalization plans and HMO establishments that provide health and medical insurance policies without providing health care services are included in this industry.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers
Direct Health and Medical Insurance Carriers

candidates.label: Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in initially underwriting (i.e., assuming the risk and assigning premiums) annuities and life insurance policies, disability income insurance policies, accidental death and dismemberment insurance policies, and health and medical insurance policies.

hierarchy_path.label: Insurance Carriers and Related Activities Industry
Direct Life, Health, and Medical Insurance Carriers

candidates.label: Home Health Equipment Rental
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in renting home-type health and invalid equipment, such as wheelchairs, hospital beds, oxygen tanks, walkers, and crutches.

hierarchy_path.label: Real Estate, Rental and Leasing Industry

candidates.label: Voluntary Health Organizations
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in raising funds for health related research, such as disease (e.g., heart, cancer, diabetes) prevention, health education, and patient services.

hierarchy_path.label: Religious, Grantmaking, Civic, Professional, and Similar Organizations

candidates.label: Health-Conscious Market Segment
candidates.definition: The health-conscious segment refers to a portion of the market that is characterized by consumers who prioritize health and wellness in their purchasing decisions. This segment includes products and services that are designed to promote physical, mental, and emotional well-being, catering to individuals who seek a healthy lifestyle.

candidates.label: Food (Health) Supplement Retailers
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing food supplement products, such as vitamins, nutrition supplements, and body enhancing supplements.

hierarchy_path.label: Health and Personal Care Retailers Industry
Other Health and Personal Care Retailers

candidates.label: Other Ambulatory Health Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing ambulatory health care services (except offices of physicians, dentists, and other health practitioners; outpatient care centers; medical laboratories and diagnostic imaging centers; and home health care providers).

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: NULL

candidates.label: Child Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing care and early learning opportunities for infants and children. These establishments generally care for children from birth through school age and may also offer pre-kindergarten, kindergarten, and/or before- or after-school educational programs. The care and early learning provided by these establishments may include opportunities for development in health, social and emotional learning, and family engagement.

candidates.label: Civil Law System
candidates.definition: Civil Law is a legal system that is primarily based on written codes and statutes, rather than case law. This system emphasizes comprehensive sets of laws that are codified and accessible, guiding judges in applying the law to individual cases.

candidates.label: Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: A court's body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing Multidistrict Litigation (MDLs).
synonyms: Regeln des Justizausschusses für multidisziplinäre Rechtsstreitigkeiten
Regras do Painel Judicial de Litígios Multidistritais
Règles du Panel judiciaire sur les litiges multidistricts
多区域诉讼司法小组规则
多地区訴訟司法パネルの規則

hierarchy_path.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation

candidates.label: Civil Union
candidates.definition: A civil union is a legally recognized arrangement similar to marriage, which provides some of the legal and social protections afforded to married couples. However, it may lack some of the federal benefits and recognition that come with marriage.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Partnership
candidates.definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

candidates.label: Tort Claimants Committee
candidates.definition: A Tort Claimants Committee is typically established within bankruptcy proceedings or large-scale litigation contexts to represent the interests of individuals who have tort claims against the debtor or defendant. This committee works to ensure that the rights and recovery opportunities of tort claimants are protected and maximized during the legal process.

candidates.label: Benefit Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A Benefit Limited Liability Company (BLLC) is a type of limited liability company that combines the liability protection of a traditional LLC with a commitment to pursuing public benefits alongside profit. These companies are structured to focus on social and environmental goals while still operating as for-profit entities.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Sole Proprietorship
candidates.definition: A sole proprietorship, also known as the sole trader, individual entrepreneurship or proprietorship, is a type of enterprise that is owned and run by one person and in which there is no legal distinction between the owner and the business entity. The sole trader receives all profits (subject to taxation specific to the business) and has unlimited responsibility for all losses and debts. Every asset of the business is owned by the proprietor and all debts of the business are the proprietor's. It is a "sole" proprietorship in contrast with partnerships (which have at least two owners).

candidates.label: Partnership
candidates.definition: A partnership is a type of legal entity where two or more individuals or entities come together to carry out a business venture, sharing profits and losses.

candidates.label: Litigation Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Management involves the management of various aspects of a legal case, including but not limited to, retention and litigation hold management, redaction management, expert witness management, brief analysis management, jury selection management, citation check management, remote trial management, eDiscovery services, exhibit management, and costs and settlement management.

candidates.label: Litigation Finance Management
candidates.definition: Litigation finance management refers to the process of managing the financial aspects of a legal case, including funding, budgeting, and accounting, in order to maximize the potential return on investment for the parties involved.

candidates.label: Litigation Analytics Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Analytics Management is the systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and utilizing data and insights from legal disputes to inform and improve decision-making, strategy, and performance in legal practice, often through the use of specialized software and tools.

candidates.label: Litigation Collaboration Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Collaboration Management refers to the process of managing and coordinating the efforts of multiple parties involved in a legal dispute, with the goal of achieving a successful outcome.

candidates.label: Retention and Litigation Hold Management
candidates.definition: Retention and Litigation Hold Management refers to the process of preserving and managing electronic data that may be relevant to a legal matter, in order to comply with legal obligations and avoid spoliation sanctions.

candidates.label: Matter Management
candidates.definition: Matter Management refers to the process of organizing and overseeing legal cases and related tasks, such as document management and budgeting, within a legal operations framework.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A textual narrative of a matter's factual and legal details.

candidates.label: Matter Complexity
candidates.definition: Classification for a given' matter's complexity, either textually (e.g., low, medium, high) or numerically (e.g., 1 through 10).

candidates.label: Public Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A public version of a matter narrative.

candidates.label: Generic Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A generic version of a matter narrative. Usually used to further describe a category of matters.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Audience
candidates.definition: A Matter Narrative Audience is a group or individual for whom the factual and legal details of a matter are presented in a textual narrative.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Description
candidates.definition: A textual narrative description, detailing a matter's factual and legal details.

candidates.label: Confidential Matter Narrative
candidates.definition: A confidential version of a matter narrative.

candidates.label: Matter Narrative Format
candidates.definition: The file format containing the narrative description of a matter.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice: Negligence or breach of duty by a healthcare professional that results in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Malpractice Claims refer to civil claims of negligence or malfeasance brought against a professional or tradesperson, such as a doctor, lawyer, accountant, or architect, for failing to meet the standard of care expected in their field.

candidates.label: Legal Malpractice
candidates.definition: Legal Malpractice is the failure of an attorney to provide competent and professional legal services to a client, resulting in harm or damages to the client.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Claims
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Claims refer to legal claims made against medical professionals or institutions for negligence or failure to provide adequate care resulting in harm or injury to a patient.

candidates.label: Medical Malpractice Defenses
candidates.definition: Medical Malpractice Defenses are legal arguments or strategies used by defendants in a medical malpractice case to defend against the plaintiff's claims of negligence or wrongdoing.
synonyms: Defesas em casos de negligência médica
Défenses en cas de faute médicale

candidates.label: Medical Device Malpractice
candidates.definition: Medical Device Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that arises from the use of a faulty or defective medical device during a medical procedure.
synonyms: Faute professionnelle liée aux dispositifs médicaux

candidates.label: Pathology Malpractice
candidates.definition: Pathology Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that arises from errors or negligence in the examination and diagnosis of tissue samples and bodily fluids by pathologists.

candidates.label: Pediatrics Malpractice
candidates.definition: Pediatrics Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that involves the negligent or wrongful actions of healthcare providers in the treatment of children.

candidates.label: Psychiatric Malpractice
candidates.definition: Psychiatric Malpractice refers to the negligence or misconduct of a mental health professional, such as a psychiatrist, which results in harm or injury to a patient, and falls under the broader category of Medical Malpractice Claims.

candidates.label: Urogynecology Malpractice
