candidates.definition: Urogynecology Malpractice refers to a type of medical malpractice claim that involves injuries or harm caused by the negligence or misconduct of a urogynecologist, who specializes in the treatment of pelvic floor disorders in women.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Civil Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Family Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Family Health Public Benefit refers to government programs specifically designed to provide healthcare coverage or financial assistance for medical needs to families. These programs aim to ensure that both adults and children within a family unit have access to necessary healthcare services

candidates.label: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit refers to specialized government programs aimed at providing healthcare services or financial assistance specifically for mothers and their children. These may cover prenatal care, childbirth, postnatal care, and pediatric services to ensure the health and well-being of both mother and child. In the United States, one such program is the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

hierarchy_path.label: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit

candidates.label: Provider of Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Provider of public benefits refers to the governmental entity responsible for administering and disbursing public assistance programs. These can be national, state, or local agencies, each offering different types of benefits tailored to the needs and eligibility criteria of their jurisdiction.

candidates.label: Litigation Practice
candidates.definition: Matters that include official proceedings (e.g., court case, arbitration, mediation)

candidates.label: Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Health public benefit refers to government programs designed to provide medical services or financial assistance for healthcare needs to eligible individuals. This can include programs like Medicaid in the United States, which offers healthcare coverage to low-income families and individuals.

candidates.label: Dispute Service
candidates.definition: A process that describes a conflict/adversarial event potentially or actually involving a second party. This can include government entity or internal investigation

candidates.label: Mediation Practice
candidates.definition: Mediation is a process wherein the parties meet with a mutually selected impartial and neutral person who assists them in the negotiation of their differences. Mediation leaves the decision power totally and strictly with the parties.

candidates.label: Provider Matter ID
candidates.definition: The provider's matter ID number.

candidates.label: Provider Identifier
candidates.definition: Identifier (e.g., IRIs, UUIDs, GUIDs) of the Provider (e.g., law firm, ALSP) within the systems of stakeholders (e.g., vendors, sellers, buyers, courts).

candidates.label: Litigation Docket Number
candidates.definition: A Litigation Docket Number is a unique identifier assigned to a legal case that is used to track and manage the case within the court system.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Claimant
candidates.definition: A person who asserts a claim, particularly the right or title to property (e.g., a claimant to an estate)

candidates.label: Creditor
candidates.definition: A creditor is a person or company to whom money is owed. Also, a party to which a bankruptcy debtor owes money.

candidates.label: Subordinated Creditor
candidates.definition: A Subordinated Creditor holds a claim or loan against a debtor that ranks below other debts in terms of repayment priority, often arising when creditors agree to lesser priority in exchange for potential higher returns. In bankruptcy, these creditors are paid only after senior debts have been settled, making their recovery prospects less certain.

candidates.label: Secured Creditor
candidates.definition: A secured creditor is any creditor or lender associated with an investment in or issuance of a credit product backed by collateral. Secured creditors have a first-order claim on the payouts of a distressed credit investment.

candidates.label: Unsecured Creditor
candidates.definition: An unsecured creditor is an individual or institution that lends money without obtaining specified assets as collateral. If a borrower fails to make payment on debt that is unsecured, the creditor cannot take any of the borrower's assets without winning a lawsuit first.

candidates.label: Petitioning Creditor
candidates.definition: From U.S. Bankruptcy Court PACER

candidates.label: Undersecured Creditor
candidates.definition: An Undersecured Creditor is a lender whose collateral value securing a loan is less than the outstanding debt owed by the borrower. This situation often arises in bankruptcy contexts, affecting the creditor’s ability to fully recover the loan amount through collateral liquidation.

candidates.label: Creditor Among 20 Largest Unsecured Creditors
candidates.definition: A creditor among the 20 largest unsecured creditors is a person or entity who is owed a debt by a debtor and is one of the top 20 creditors who do not have a security interest in the debtor's property.
synonyms: 20 सबसे बड़े अवधारणहीन उधारदाताओं में से एक उधारदाता
20大无担保债权人中的债权人
Acreedor entre los 20 mayores acreedores no garantizados
Credor entre os 20 maiores credores não garantidos
Créancier parmi les 20 plus grands créanciers non garantis
Gläubiger unter den 20 größten ungesicherten Gläubigern
נושה בין 20 הנושים הלא מבוטחים הגדולים ביותר

candidates.label: Commercial Publisher
candidates.definition: A commercial publisher operates to produce and distribute various types of publications, such as books, magazines, or digital content, with the primary goal of generating profit. Unlike academic publishers, commercial publishers often focus on broader audiences and market-driven topics. In the legal sector, commercial publishers might produce legal texts, practice guides, or public-facing materials that appeal to professionals, students, or the general public, with revenue generated through sales, subscriptions, or advertising.

candidates.label: Banking Law
candidates.definition: Law that governs how banks and other financial institutions conduct business.

candidates.label: Banking Operations Law
candidates.definition: Law governing routine banking operations including bank capital requirements, risk management, etc.

candidates.label: Commercial Finance Law
candidates.definition: Law supporting the function of providing loans or other financing to businesses.

candidates.label: Commercial and Trade Law
candidates.definition: Law that applies to the rights, relations, and conduct of persons and businesses engaged in commerce, merchandising, trade, and sales.

candidates.label: Commercial Transactions Law
candidates.definition: Law related to business contracts and agreements.

candidates.label: Negligence and Malpractice Law
candidates.definition: Failure to exercise the care that a reasonably prudent person or professional, in the case of professional negligence, would exercise in like circumstances and the harm caused by those actions.
synonyms: Derecho de negligencia y mala praxis
Derecho de negligencia y mala práctica
Droit de la négligence et de la faute professionnelle

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Payable-on-Death Account
candidates.definition: A Payable-on-Death (POD) Account is a financial account, such as a bank account or a certificate of deposit, that allows the account holder to designate a specific beneficiary who will receive the funds in the account upon the account holder's death. This designation operates outside of the probate process, making it a straightforward way to transfer assets to a chosen beneficiary without the need for a will or going through the probate court.

candidates.label: Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities
candidates.definition: Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities (CMBS) are a type of asset-backed security backed by a pool of commercial real estate loans, such as those for office buildings, hotels, shopping centers, and other income-generating properties. Investors in CMBS receive payments from the principal and interest collected on the underlying commercial mortgages, offering exposure to the commercial real estate market.

candidates.label: Commercial Paper
candidates.definition: Commercial paper is an unsecured, short-term debt instrument issued by a corporation, typically for the financing of accounts payable and inventories and meeting short-term liabilities.

candidates.label: Asset-Backed Commercial Paper
candidates.definition: ABCP is a type of short-term debt instrument that is backed by a pool of financial assets, such as trade receivables, auto loans, credit card debt, residential mortgages, or other asset-backed securities (ABS). The maturity of ABCP typically ranges from 1 to 270 days, with an average of around 30 days.

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Personal Care Products
candidates.definition: In the context of bankruptcies and monthly spending budgets, Personal Care Products refer to non-medical goods purchased for hygiene or beautification, such as toiletries, cosmetics, and grooming supplies. These are generally considered non-discretionary expenses, but their allocation may be scrutinized during a bankruptcy process to determine reasonable living expenses for the debtor

candidates.label: Cash and Cash Equivalents
candidates.definition: Cash is legal tender – currency or coins – that can be used to exchange goods, debt, or services. Sometimes it also includes the value of assets that can be easily converted into cash immediately, as reported by a company. Cash equivalents are investment

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: Death Certificate
candidates.definition: A Death Certificate is an official document issued by a government authority that records the details of a person's death. It typically includes information such as the deceased individual's name, date and place of death, cause of death, and the attending physician's or coroner's signature. Death certificates are important for legal and administrative purposes, including the settlement of the deceased person's estate and handling matters related to inheritance, insurance, and funeral arrangements.

candidates.label: Claim of Exemption
candidates.definition: A claim of exemption is a legal document filed in bankruptcy cases that allows a debtor to protect certain property from being seized by creditors.

candidates.label: Claim Construction Brief
candidates.definition: A Claim Construction (Markman) Brief is a written document submitted to a court that provides an interpretation of the language used in patent claims, specifically for the purpose of determining the scope of the patent.

candidates.label: Intervenor Claim
candidates.definition: An Intervenor Claim is a legal pleading filed by a third party who seeks to join an ongoing lawsuit because they have a direct interest in the outcome of the case.

candidates.label: Answer to Intervenor Claim
candidates.definition: An Answer to Intervenor Claim is a legal document filed by a party who seeks to intervene in a lawsuit, responding to the claims made by the intervenor.
synonyms: Antwort auf den Einwand des Intervenienten
Respuesta a la Demanda del Interventor
Respuesta a la Demanda del Interviniente
介入者請求に対する回答

candidates.label: Insurance Claims Form
candidates.definition: An Insurance Claims Form is a standardized document used by a policyholder to request reimbursement or coverage for a loss or event that is insured under their policy. The form usually includes fields for providing details about the claim such as the incident, damages, and other relevant documentation.

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Banking Law Documents
candidates.definition: "Banking Law Documents" refer to the collection of legal texts, forms, contracts, and regulatory materials that govern the operations, transactions, and compliance of financial institutions. These documents encompass a wide range of topics, including but not limited to, customer interactions, financial transactions, regulatory compliance, and risk management.

hierarchy_path.label: Document Collections by Areas of Law

candidates.label: Commercial Motor Vehicle Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N code identifying whether the incident occurred while the driver was operating a commercial vehicle.
synonyms: 商用車インジケーター

candidates.label: Litigation Identifiers
candidates.definition: "Information that denotes a litigation matter."

candidates.label: Litigation Support Vendors Expense
candidates.definition: Expenses incurred by vendors who provide support services to parties involved in a legal dispute, such as document management, e-discovery, and trial presentation services.
synonyms: Despesas de fornecedores de suporte à litigação
Frais de prestataires de soutien à la litige
Gastos de proveedores de apoyo a litigios
訴訟サポートベンダー費用

candidates.label: Matter Funds Applied
candidates.definition: The concept of "Matter Funds Applied" refers to the portion of an engagement fee that is specifically allocated to cover expenses related to a particular legal matter, as opposed to the fee being applied generally to the overall engagement.

candidates.label: Regulatory Statement
candidates.definition: A Regulatory Statement is a type of Invoice Data that contains information related to legal regulations and requirements.

candidates.label: Death
candidates.definition: Death, in a legal context, marks the end of a person's life, as well as the termination of an individual's legal rights and duties. It initiates the probate process, where a deceased's estate is settled, debts are paid, and assets are distributed to heirs or beneficiaries according to a will or intestate laws.

candidates.label: Funeral Practices
candidates.definition: Funeral Practices are the various rituals, ceremonies, and customs performed to honor and remember a deceased person. These practices vary greatly across different cultures, religions, and legal systems, often reflecting the beliefs and traditions of the community.

candidates.label: Grand Jury Proceeding
candidates.definition: A Grand Jury Proceeding is a legal process where a group of citizens is convened to determine whether there is enough evidence to bring criminal charges against a person or entity.
synonyms: הליך חשיפת חשדות על ידי חבורת המשותפים

candidates.label: Penalty Proceeding
candidates.definition: A penalty proceeding is a legal process that deals specifically with the determination and imposition of punishment for a criminal offense.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Litigation Events
candidates.definition: Events occurring during the course of disputes.

candidates.label: Litigation Hold Event
candidates.definition: A Litigation Hold Event is a specific event within the context of a legal dispute where a party is required to preserve relevant evidence.

candidates.label: Taken Into Custody
candidates.definition: Taken Into Custody: The act of a law enforcement officer taking a person into custody, typically for the purpose of charging them with a crime.

candidates.label: Employment Offer
candidates.definition: A job offer is a formal proposal made by an employer to a candidate selected for a position, detailing the terms and conditions of employment. This includes information about the role, salary, benefits, start date, and other pertinent details.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: Employment Benefits
candidates.definition: Employment Benefits are additional compensations or advantages provided to employees over and above their regular salary, often as part of an employment contract. These benefits may include healthcare, retirement plans, paid time off, and other perks designed to attract and retain employees.

candidates.label: Copyright Claims Board
candidates.definition: A Copyright Claims Board (CCB) is a specialized administrative body within the United States Copyright Office that is responsible for resolving disputes related to copyright claims.

candidates.definition: Delaware State Courts are the state-level judicial system in Delaware, consisting of various courts with different jurisdictions and responsibilities.

candidates.label: Louisiana State Courts
candidates.definition: Louisiana State Courts are the judicial system of the state of Louisiana, including the Louisiana Supreme Court and the Louisiana Court of Appeal.

candidates.label: Indiana State Courts
candidates.definition: The Indiana State Courts refer to the judicial system of the state of Indiana, which includes various courts such as the Indiana Tax Court, Indiana Supreme Court, Indiana Superior Court, Indiana City Court, Indiana Town Court, Indiana Circuit Court, and Indiana Court of Appeals.

candidates.label: Drug Enforcement Administration
candidates.definition: The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) is a United States federal law enforcement agency under the United States Department of Justice, tasked with combating drug smuggling and use within the United States.

Administration pour le contrôle des drogues

candidates.definition: The Office of Child Support Enforcement (OCSE) is a United States government office responsible for overseeing the U.S. child support program.

candidates.label: Governmental Body
candidates.definition: Administrative entities of government or state agency or appointed commission, as a permanent or semi-permanent governmental organization that oversees or administers specific governmental functions.

candidates.label: Governmental Branch
candidates.definition: The branches of government typically refer to the three main divisions of a democratic system: the legislative, which makes laws; the executive, which enforces and administers laws; and the judicial, which interprets laws. These branches are designed to balance power and ensure that no single entity has unchecked authority.

candidates.label: State Governmental Body
candidates.definition: A regional governmental body, such as a state or provincial government, operates at a level below the national government but above local governments like cities or municipalities. These bodies have jurisdiction over a specific geographic area and possess their own set of powers and responsibilities, often delineated by a constitution or charter. They typically consist of executive, legislative, and judicial branches, and they manage issues more localized than those of the national government but broader than those of city or town governments. Examples include U.S. state governments, Canadian provincial governments, or Indian state governments.

candidates.label: Local Governmental Body
candidates.definition: A local governmental body, such as a city or municipality, is responsible for governance at the community or city level, addressing issues and services that directly affect residents within a specific locality. These bodies typically have authority over matters like zoning, local law enforcement, public works, sanitation, and local ordinances. Their structure can include a mayor or city manager, a city council or board, and various departments or agencies dedicated to specific community services. The specific powers and responsibilities of local governments can vary widely based on regional laws and traditions.

candidates.label: County Governmental Body
candidates.definition: A county governmental body oversees governance at the county level, which is typically a subdivision of a state or province, but above local city or town governments. County governments handle administrative and regulatory responsibilities that span multiple municipalities within their boundaries. These responsibilities can include law enforcement (through county sheriff's departments), maintaining county roads and infrastructure, overseeing public health and welfare programs, managing property records, and conducting elections.

candidates.label: National Governmental Body
candidates.definition: A national governmental body refers to an organization or institution at the highest level of governance in a country, responsible for making, executing, or interpreting laws and regulations. These bodies often include the executive branch (headed by presidents or prime ministers), legislative branches (such as parliaments or congresses), and judicial branches (like supreme or constitutional courts). Their functions and structures vary depending on the specific form and constitution of each nation's government.

candidates.label: Quasi-Public Governmental Body
candidates.definition: A Quasi-Public Governmental Body is an organization that has governmental functions but operates with some degree of independence from direct government control. These bodies often provide public services and may have regulatory authority, yet they typically function more like private enterprises.

candidates.label: Multi-National Governmental Body
candidates.definition: A Multi-National Governmental Body refers to an organization formed by a coalition of multiple nations, primarily to address collective concerns, facilitate diplomatic relations, or coordinate actions in various fields. These entities operate based on treaties or agreements and often have a governing structure, legal personality, and set jurisdictions.

candidates.label: Death Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in preparing the dead for burial or interment, conducting funerals, operating sites or structures reserved for the interment of human or animals remains, and/or cremating the dead.

candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in investigating, appraising, and settling insurance claims.

candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in accepting demand and other deposits and making commercial, industrial, and consumer loans. Commercial banks and branches of foreign banks are included in this industry.

candidates.label: Investment Banking and Securities Intermediation
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in underwriting, originating, and/or maintaining markets for issues of securities, or acting as agents (i.e., brokers) between buyers and sellers in buying or selling securities on a commission or transaction fee basis. Investment bankers act as principals (i.e., investors who buy or sell on their own account) in firm commitment transactions or act as agents in best effort and standby commitments. This industry also includes establishments acting as principals in buying or selling securities generally on a spread basis, such as securities dealers or stock option dealers.

candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing fresh and frozen bread and bread-type rolls and other fresh bakery (except cookies and crackers) products.

candidates.label: Commercial Photography
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing commercial photography services, generally for advertising agencies, publishers, and other business and industrial users.

hierarchy_path.label: Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services Industry
Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services

candidates.label: Commercial Screen Printing
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in screen printing without publishing (except books, fabric grey goods, and manifold business forms). This industry includes establishments engaged in screen printing on purchased stock materials, such as stationery, invitations, labels, and similar items, on a job-order basis. Establishments primarily engaged in printing on apparel and textile products, such as T-shirts, caps, jackets, towels, and napkins, are included in this industry.

hierarchy_path.label: Printing and Related Support Activities Industry

candidates.label: Commercial Printing (except Screen and Books)
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in commercial printing (except screen printing, books printing) without publishing (except fabric grey goods printing). The printing processes used in this industry include, but are not limited to, lithographic, gravure, flexographic, letterpress, engraving, and various digital printing technologies. This industry includes establishments engaged in commercial printing on purchased stock materials, such as stationery, invitations, labels, and similar items, on a job-order basis. Establishments primarily engaged in traditional printing activities combined with document photocopying services (i.e., quick printers) or primarily engaged in printing graphical materials using digital printing equipment are included in this industry.

hierarchy_path.label: Printing and Related Support Activities Industry

candidates.label: AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedures
candidates.definition: The AAA Commercial Arbitration Rules and Mediation Procedures are a set of guidelines and procedures established by the American Arbitration Association for resolving commercial disputes through arbitration and mediation.

Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Child Support Guidelines
candidates.definition: Child Support Guidelines are legal regulations or guidelines established by a government authority to determine the amount of financial support that a non-custodial parent is required to provide for their child or children. These guidelines take into account factors such as the parents' incomes, the child's needs, and the cost of living. They provide a standardized method for calculating child support payments, ensuring fairness and consistency in support orders.

candidates.label: Brain Death
candidates.definition: Encephalic Death

candidates.label: Brain Stem Death
candidates.definition: Brain stem death is a medical condition where an individual has irreversibly lost all brain stem functions, which are responsible for consciousness and vital bodily functions, while other parts of the brain may still have activity. It is often considered a criterion for legal death in many jurisdictions, as the loss of brain stem function is critical for maintaining life.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A limited liability company (LLC) is a corporate structure in the United States whereby the owners are not personally liable for the company's debts or liabilities. Limited liability companies are hybrid entities that combine the characteristics of a corporation with those of a partnership or sole proprietorship.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Partnership
candidates.definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

candidates.label: Benefit Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A Benefit Limited Liability Company (BLLC) is a type of limited liability company that combines the liability protection of a traditional LLC with a commitment to pursuing public benefits alongside profit. These companies are structured to focus on social and environmental goals while still operating as for-profit entities.

candidates.label: Professional Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A professional limited liability company ('PLLC') is a U.S. business entity designed for licensed professionals, such as lawyers, doctors, architects, engineers, accountants, and chiropractors. While many businesses choose to form a limited liability company ('LLC') because of the tax, limited liability, and other benefits, some states don't allow LLCs to be owned by professionals whose occupation requires a license. In these states, licensed professionals who want the benefits of an LLC must form a PLLC instead.
synonyms: Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung für Freiberufler
プロフェッショナル有限責任会社

candidates.label: Tort Claimants Committee
candidates.definition: A Tort Claimants Committee is typically established within bankruptcy proceedings or large-scale litigation contexts to represent the interests of individuals who have tort claims against the debtor or defendant. This committee works to ensure that the rights and recovery opportunities of tort claimants are protected and maximized during the legal process.

candidates.label: Governmental Entity
candidates.definition: Any government or any governmental agency, semi-governmental or judicial entity or authority (including, without limitation, any stock exchange or any self-regulatory organization established under statute).

candidates.label: Litigation Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Management involves the management of various aspects of a legal case, including but not limited to, retention and litigation hold management, redaction management, expert witness management, brief analysis management, jury selection management, citation check management, remote trial management, eDiscovery services, exhibit management, and costs and settlement management.

candidates.label: Litigation Finance Management
candidates.definition: Litigation finance management refers to the process of managing the financial aspects of a legal case, including funding, budgeting, and accounting, in order to maximize the potential return on investment for the parties involved.

candidates.label: Litigation Analytics Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Analytics Management is the systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and utilizing data and insights from legal disputes to inform and improve decision-making, strategy, and performance in legal practice, often through the use of specialized software and tools.

candidates.label: Litigation Collaboration Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Collaboration Management refers to the process of managing and coordinating the efforts of multiple parties involved in a legal dispute, with the goal of achieving a successful outcome.

candidates.label: Retention and Litigation Hold Management
candidates.definition: Retention and Litigation Hold Management refers to the process of preserving and managing electronic data that may be relevant to a legal matter, in order to comply with legal obligations and avoid spoliation sanctions.

candidates.label: Regulatory Filing Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Filing Management refers to the process of managing and submitting required documents and information to regulatory authorities in order to comply with legal and regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Regulatory Research Management
candidates.definition: Regulatory Research Management refers to the process of conducting legal research specifically related to regulations and compliance, including tracking and managing changes to regulatory requirements.

candidates.label: Normandie
candidates.definition: Normandie is a region in France.

candidates.label: Wrongful Death Claim
candidates.definition: A wrongful death claim is a legal action brought by the survivors of a deceased person against the party responsible for the death, seeking compensation for damages resulting from the loss of the individual's life.

candidates.label: Wrongful Birth Claim
candidates.definition: A wrongful birth claim is a type of personal injury claim brought by parents who allege that they were not properly informed by a medical professional of the risk of having a child with a genetic or congenital abnormality, resulting in the birth of a child with such abnormality.
synonyms: Action en responsabilité pour naissance fautive
Anspruch auf Schadensersatz bei fehlerhafter Geburt

candidates.label: Wrongful Foreclosure Claim
candidates.definition: A wrongful foreclosure claim is a legal action brought by a borrower against a lender or creditor alleging that the foreclosure of their property was conducted improperly or unlawfully.
synonyms: תביעה על מימוש משכנתא בלתי חוקי
不当な差し押さえ請求

candidates.label: Death Claims
candidates.definition: A Death Claim is a legal action seeking compensation for the death of an individual caused by the negligence or intentional act of another party.

candidates.label: Child Abuse Claim
candidates.definition: A Child Abuse Claim is a legal action brought by a child or their representative alleging physical, emotional, or sexual abuse by a parent or caregiver.

candidates.label: Wills Claims
candidates.definition: A claim made in a legal matter regarding the validity or interpretation of a will.

candidates.label: Fraud Claims
candidates.definition: Fraud Claims refer to civil or tort claims that involve intentional misrepresentation or deceitful conduct by one party towards another, including fraudulent misrepresentation, legal fraud, fraudulent transfers, and other forms of fraudulent behavior.

candidates.label: Animal Claims
candidates.definition: Animal Claims refer to legal claims related to harm caused by or to animals, including liability for injury to pets, cruelty to animals, animal attacks, trespassing livestock causing harm, dangerous domesticated animals, injury caused by wild animals, and negligent handling of animals.

candidates.label: Responses to Claims
candidates.definition: Responses to Claims refer to the various legal actions taken by a party in response to a claim made against them, including denial, counterclaim, and admission.

candidates.label: Social Security Claims
candidates.definition: A claim made under the Social Security program, which provides benefits to eligible individuals based on their contributions to the program, or in some cases, based on their status as a dependent or survivor of a contributor.

candidates.label: Claim Construction Practice
candidates.definition: Claim Construction Practice refers to the process of analyzing and interpreting the language used in patent claims to determine the scope of the invention, and is a subset of Fact Investigation and Development.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Claims Review Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Review Practice (Bankruptcy) is a specialized legal practice that focuses on reviewing and evaluating claims made in bankruptcy cases.
synonyms: 請求審査プラクティス

candidates.label: Claims Objection Practice
candidates.definition: Claims Objection Practice (Bankruptcy) refers to the legal practice of objecting to claims made by creditors in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Creditor Meeting and Communication Practice
candidates.definition: A Creditor Meeting and Communication Practice is a specialized area of law within Bankruptcy and Financial Restructuring Practice that deals with the legal procedures and requirements for communication and meetings between creditors and debtors during bankruptcy proceedings.
synonyms: Pratique de réunion et de communication des créanciers
Práctica de reuniones y comunicación con acreedores

債権者会議およびコミュニケーションの実践

candidates.definition: Analysis of Vendors & Commercial Arrangement Considerations refers to the process of evaluating and assessing the legal obligations and risks associated with commercial relationships and agreements with third-party vendors.

Analyse von Anbietern und kommerziellen Vereinbarungen

candidates.label: Dispute Service
candidates.definition: A process that describes a conflict/adversarial event potentially or actually involving a second party. This can include government entity or internal investigation

candidates.label: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit
candidates.definition: Mother and Child Health Public Benefit refers to specialized government programs aimed at providing healthcare services or financial assistance specifically for mothers and their children. These may cover prenatal care, childbirth, postnatal care, and pediatric services to ensure the health and well-being of both mother and child. In the United States, one such program is the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP).

candidates.label: Litigation Practice
candidates.definition: Matters that include official proceedings (e.g., court case, arbitration, mediation)

candidates.label: Status - Denied
candidates.definition: Status of "Denied" indicates that an application, request, or action has been reviewed and declined by the relevant authority, signaling that the individual's request or application did not meet the necessary criteria or legal requirements, resulting in a denial of the intended action, status change, or benefit.

candidates.label: Litigation Docket Number
candidates.definition: A Litigation Docket Number is a unique identifier assigned to a legal case that is used to track and manage the case within the court system.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Governmental Civil Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Civil Law Division is a governmental entity that handles civil legal matters involving the government. It provides legal advice to government agencies, represents the government in civil litigation, and ensures compliance with civil laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Joint Debtor
candidates.definition: Two or more debtors who are jointly liable for a single debt (e.g., cosigners on a loan)

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Constitutional and Civil Rights Law
candidates.definition: Rights that protect individuals' freedom from infringement by governments, social organizations, and private individuals.
synonyms: Derecho Constitucional y de Derechos Civiles
Direito Constitucional e dos Direitos Civis
Droit constitutionnel et des droits civils

candidates.label: Construction and Development Law
candidates.definition: Law dealing with matters relating to building construction, engineering, and related fields.
synonyms: Derecho de la Construcción y Desarrollo
Droit de la construction et de l'aménagement

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Personal Care Products
candidates.definition: In the context of bankruptcies and monthly spending budgets, Personal Care Products refer to non-medical goods purchased for hygiene or beautification, such as toiletries, cosmetics, and grooming supplies. These are generally considered non-discretionary expenses, but their allocation may be scrutinized during a bankruptcy process to determine reasonable living expenses for the debtor

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: Personal Information
candidates.definition: Personal Information refers to any data that relates to an identified or identifiable individual. This includes both sensitive personal information, which requires greater protection due to its potential to cause harm if disclosed, and personally identifiable information (PII), which can be used to identify, contact, or locate a specific individual.

candidates.label: Sensitive Personal Information
candidates.definition: Sensitive Personal Information refers to personal data that requires greater protection due to its nature and the potential harm that could result from its unauthorized disclosure. This includes data such as health records, financial information, racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, and biometric data.

candidates.label: Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction
candidates.definition: A Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is a legal request made by a defendant to dismiss a case on the grounds that the court does not have the authority to hear the case due to the defendant not having sufficient contacts with the jurisdiction.
synonyms: Antrag auf Abweisung wegen fehlender persönlicher Zuständigkeit

candidates.definition: A Partnership Agreement is a contract between partners in a partnership that specifies their mutual obligations, responsibilities, profit-sharing ratios, and other operational terms.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Notice of Loss
candidates.definition: In disputes, the date where a claimant gives notice of the claimant's loss.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Civil Arrest
candidates.definition: The concept of Civil Arrest refers to the act of detaining a person by legal authority in order to enforce a civil judgment against them.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: Asset Depreciation
candidates.definition: Asset Depreciation is the systematic decrease in the recorded value of an asset over time, typically due to wear and tear, obsolescence, or the passage of time. This accounting method allocates the cost of tangible assets over their useful life, reflecting the reduction in the asset's economic value.

candidates.definition: A Copyright Claims Board (CCB) is a specialized administrative body within the United States Copyright Office that is responsible for resolving disputes related to copyright claims.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Center
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor Civil Rights Center (CRC) develops, administers, and enforces Departmental policies, practices, and procedures pursuant to Title VI and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Section 188 of the Workforce Investment Act (WIA), as amended; the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended; the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, as amended; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; the Equal Pay Act of 1963; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, as amended; Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, as amended; the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978; the Civil Rights Act of 1991; the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation (No FEAR) Act; and related statutes and Executive Orders.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Division
candidates.definition: The Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice, created in 1957 by the enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1957, works to uphold the civil and constitutional rights of all Americans, particularly some of the most vulnerable members of our society. The Division enforces federal statutes prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, disability, religion, familial status and national origin.

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
candidates.definition: The Office for Civil Rights (OCR) is a sub-agency of the U.S. Department of Education that is primarily focused on enforcing civil rights laws prohibiting schools from engaging in discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or membership in patriotic youth organizations.

candidates.label: U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
candidates.definition: The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is a bipartisan, independent commission of the United States federal government, created in 1957, that is charged with the responsibility for investigating, reporting on, and making recommendations concerning civil rights issues in the United States.

candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal care services (except hair, nail, facial, nonpermanent makeup, or non-medical diet and weight reducing services).

Establishments primarily engaged in the preparation of sites for new construction and establishments primarily engaged in subdividing land for sale as building sites also are included in this sector.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Civil Law System
candidates.definition: Civil Law is a legal system that is primarily based on written codes and statutes, rather than case law. This system emphasizes comprehensive sets of laws that are codified and accessible, guiding judges in applying the law to individual cases.

candidates.label: Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: A court's body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Aphonia
candidates.definition: Aphonia is the complete loss of voice resulting from the inability to produce vocal sounds through the vocal cords, resulting in the inability to produce speech that can be heard, understood, or sustained. This condition can be caused by various factors, including severe laryngitis, vocal cord paralysis, trauma to the larynx, or neurological disorders.

candidates.label: Paralysis
candidates.definition: Paralysis is the loss of muscle function in part or most of the body, often accompanied by sensory loss, due to nerve damage or disruption in the spinal cord or brain. It can be temporary or permanent and requires comprehensive medical treatment and rehabilitation to manage symptoms and improve quality of life.

candidates.label: Syndicate
candidates.definition: A Syndicate refers to a group of individuals or organizations that come together for a common purpose, often to pool resources, share risks, and achieve a collective goal in various industries. In a financial transactions, a syndicate refers to a group of financial institutions or investors that come together to collectively finance a large loan or underwrite a security issuance, spreading the risk among all participants. In the context of a syndicated loan, the syndicate includes multiple lenders who each contribute a portion of the loan amount, with one or more lead banks coordinating the transaction.

candidates.label: Civil Union
candidates.definition: A civil union is a legally recognized arrangement similar to marriage, which provides some of the legal and social protections afforded to married couples. However, it may lack some of the federal benefits and recognition that come with marriage.

candidates.label: Limited Partnership
candidates.definition: A Limited Partnership is a business entity that consists of one or more General Partners and one or more Limited Partners. The General Partner may be an individual or an entity, such as a corporation. Typically, the General Partners are liable for any and all of the company's financial obligations, while the Limited Partners possess no liability for the company's debts, obligations or actions. General Partners usually possess all the rights and responsibilities of managing the business entity, including all of its activities and financial matters. They also hold general liability for the debts, obligations, and activities of the Limited Partnership.

candidates.label: Visual Field Loss
candidates.definition: Visual Field Loss refers to a reduction or loss of the normal field of vision, affecting peripheral or central sight. This condition can result from various underlying causes such as glaucoma, stroke, or retinal damage, significantly impacting daily activities and the ability to navigate environments.

candidates.label: Central Vision Loss
candidates.definition: Central vision loss refers to the significant impairment or complete loss of vision in the central part of the visual field, while peripheral vision remains intact. This condition often results from diseases affecting the macula or the central retina, leading to difficulties with tasks that require detailed vision, such as reading or recognizing faces, despite maintaining peripheral awareness.

candidates.label: Partial Vision Loss
candidates.definition: Partial Vision Loss refers to a significant reduction in vision that cannot be fully corrected with conventional glasses, contact lenses, or surgery. This condition affects the ability to perform everyday tasks and may involve difficulties with visual acuity, contrast sensitivity, peripheral vision, or depth perception.

candidates.label: Loss Function
candidates.definition: A Loss Function, also known as a Cost Function, is a mathematical function that provides a single metric to evaluate the performance of an AI model by quantifying the difference between predicted and actual values. The primary goal of training an AI system is to minimize the total loss over a set of validation examples, thereby improving the model's accuracy and effectiveness.

candidates.label: Closing Management
candidates.definition: Closing Management refers to the process of managing the legal aspects of a transaction, specifically the final stages of completing the deal.

candidates.label: Los Rios
candidates.definition: Locations used: Mexico

candidates.label: Los Lagos
candidates.definition: Locations used: Malawi

candidates.label: Loss of Consortium
candidates.definition: The concept of Loss of Consortium refers to the damages suffered by a spouse or parent due to the injury or death of their loved one, which includes the loss of companionship, affection, and support.

candidates.label: Loss of Spousal Consortium
candidates.definition: The loss of the benefits and companionship of a spouse due to injury or death caused by the negligence or wrongful act of another.
synonyms: पति या पत्नी के साथ रहने का नुकसान
配偶者の損害賠償請求

candidates.label: Loss of Parental Consortium
candidates.definition: The loss of a parent's ability to provide love, affection, guidance, and support to their child due to injury or death caused by the negligence of another party.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Personal Injury Claims are civil claims or tort claims seeking compensation for physical or emotional harm caused by the negligence or intentional actions of another party, including but not limited to slip-and-fall accidents, medical malpractice, and motor vehicle accidents.

candidates.label: Litigation Claims
candidates.definition: Demand or assertion seeking money, property, or enforcement of another right as provided by law.

candidates.label: Venue Claims
candidates.definition: A venue claim is a type of procedural claim in which a party argues that a legal proceeding should take place in a specific geographic location, either based on a statute, a contractual agreement, or the doctrine of forum non conveniens.

candidates.label: Wills Claims
candidates.definition: A claim made in a legal matter regarding the validity or interpretation of a will.

candidates.label: Fraud Claims
candidates.definition: Fraud Claims refer to civil or tort claims that involve intentional misrepresentation or deceitful conduct by one party towards another, including fraudulent misrepresentation, legal fraud, fraudulent transfers, and other forms of fraudulent behavior.

candidates.label: Animal Claims
candidates.definition: Animal Claims refer to legal claims related to harm caused by or to animals, including liability for injury to pets, cruelty to animals, animal attacks, trespassing livestock causing harm, dangerous domesticated animals, injury caused by wild animals, and negligent handling of animals.

candidates.label: Divorce Claims
candidates.definition: Divorce Claims refer to legal actions taken by a party to end a marriage and obtain a legal dissolution of the marital relationship, including issues related to property division, spousal support, and child custody.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Civil Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Governmental Civil Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Civil Law Division is a governmental entity that handles civil legal matters involving the government. It provides legal advice to government agencies, represents the government in civil litigation, and ensures compliance with civil laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Real Estate Operator
candidates.definition: A Real Estate Operator is a professional or entity responsible for the day-to-day management, maintenance, and operations of real estate properties. This role includes overseeing property functions, ensuring tenant satisfaction, handling leasing activities, and maintaining the physical condition of the property.

candidates.label: Real Estate Developer
candidates.definition: A Real Estate Developer is a professional or company that acquires land, finances real estate deals, builds or renovates properties, and arranges for their sale or lease. This role involves various stages of development, including market research, site selection, obtaining permits, and managing construction projects.

candidates.label: Single Asset Real Estate Debtor
candidates.definition: A Single Asset Real Estate Debtor (SARE Debtor) refers to a specific classification in bankruptcy proceedings for debtors whose primary business involves a single real estate property. This classification impacts how bankruptcy cases are handled, particularly by expediting the process and applying unique rules concerning automatic stays and reorganization plans.

candidates.label: Buyer
candidates.definition: A buyer or purchaser generally means a person, company or entity contracting with a seller for services or merchandise to be provided or delivered for a named individual. In a strict legal sense, a buyer may also include someone who acquires title to something by deed, devise, execution, prescription, possession, occupancy, or escheat.

candidates.label: Agency Service Provider
candidates.definition: An Agency Service Provider in project finance transactions acts as an intermediary between the various parties involved, ensuring smooth communication and coordination. This entity manages administrative tasks, facilitates financial transactions, and ensures compliance with contractual obligations.

candidates.label: Litigation Finance Company
candidates.definition: A Litigation Finance Company is a specialized financial institution that provides funding to plaintiffs or law firms to cover legal costs in exchange for a share of any potential recovery or settlement from a lawsuit.

candidates.label: Litigation Applicant/Petitioner
candidates.definition: A Litigation Applicant/Petitioner is an individual or entity initiating a lawsuit or formal legal proceedings by filing a complaint or petition with a court. This party seeks judicial intervention to obtain relief, resolve disputes, or enforce legal rights, setting the judicial process in motion.

candidates.label: Product Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to liability for injuries caused by defective or unsafe products.
synonyms: Droit de la responsabilité du produit

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Constitutional and Civil Rights Law
candidates.definition: Rights that protect individuals' freedom from infringement by governments, social organizations, and private individuals.
synonyms: Derecho Constitucional y de Derechos Civiles
Direito Constitucional e dos Direitos Civis
Droit constitutionnel et des droits civils

candidates.label: Lender Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law which holds lender liable for a borrower's financial losses that are directly or indirectly related to the lender's actions.

candidates.label: Property Rights and Transactions Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to the acquisition and transfer of real property.
synonyms: Derecho de Propiedad y Ley de Transacciones
Direito de Propriedade e Lei de Transações
Droit des droits de propriété et des transactions

candidates.label: Real Property Law
candidates.definition: Law that governs the various forms of ownership and tenancy in real property.

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Intellectual Property Assets
candidates.definition: Intellectual property is a broad categorical description for the set of intangibles owned and legally protected by a company from outside use or implementation without consent. Intellectual property can consist of patents, trade secrets, copyrights, franchises, and trademarks or simply ideas.

candidates.label: Religious Property - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A Religious Property is a property and real estate asset used by a religious organization for worship and religious activities. This property can include the main worship building, additional structures, and the surrounding land.

candidates.label: Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate asset type

candidates.label: Office Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate office buildings.

candidates.label: Retail Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate retail properties including malls, stores, and restaurants.
synonyms: Activo inmobiliario de venta al por menor

candidates.label: Logging Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A logging real estate asset refers to a property that contains or is primarily used for the extraction of timber through logging activities. This asset is crucial in the context of forestry law, environmental law, and property law, as it involves the rights to harvest, manage, and sell timber resources.

candidates.label: Coastal Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A Coastal Real Estate Asset refers to property located along the coastlines of oceans or seas. These assets typically offer scenic views, access to water-based activities, and come with unique environmental and regulatory considerations related to coastal management and conservation.

candidates.label: Mining - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A mining real estate asset is a type of property that includes land and any minerals or resources that can be extracted from it, such as coal or gold.

candidates.label: Airport - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: An Airport - Real Estate Asset refers to the land, buildings, and infrastructure associated with an airport, including runways, terminals, and other facilities. This type of asset involves both operational and non-operational properties that contribute to the airport's functionality and revenue generation.

candidates.label: Personal Information
candidates.definition: Personal Information refers to any data that relates to an identified or identifiable individual. This includes both sensitive personal information, which requires greater protection due to its potential to cause harm if disclosed, and personally identifiable information (PII), which can be used to identify, contact, or locate a specific individual.

candidates.label: Sensitive Personal Information
candidates.definition: Sensitive Personal Information refers to personal data that requires greater protection due to its nature and the potential harm that could result from its unauthorized disclosure. This includes data such as health records, financial information, racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, and biometric data.

candidates.label: Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction
candidates.definition: A Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is a legal request made by a defendant to dismiss a case on the grounds that the court does not have the authority to hear the case due to the defendant not having sufficient contacts with the jurisdiction.

candidates.definition: A Catalogue is a type of directory that systematically lists, describes, and often categorizes items or services, typically for the purpose of identification, reference, or purchase. Unlike a simple directory, a catalogue often includes additional information about the listed items, such as specifications, prices, or images.

candidates.label: Civil Cover Sheet
candidates.definition: A Civil Cover Sheet is a document used in trial court practice to provide basic information about a civil case, such as the parties involved and the nature of the case.
synonyms: 民事訴訟カバーシート

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: General Business Liability Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: General Business Liability Insurance, also known simply as general liability insurance, provides coverage for common business risks, including bodily injury and property damage that occur at the business premises or as a result of business operations. It also often covers legal costs related to these risks. This type of insurance is foundational for most businesses.

candidates.label: Requests for Production
candidates.definition: Requests for Production are a type of Discovery Document that require a party to provide specific documents or items for inspection and copying by the opposing party.

candidates.label: Litigation Identifiers
candidates.definition: "Information that denotes a litigation matter."

candidates.label: Litigation Support Vendors Expense
candidates.definition: Expenses incurred by vendors who provide support services to parties involved in a legal dispute, such as document management, e-discovery, and trial presentation services.

Frais de prestataires de soutien à la litige
