candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Civil Arrest
candidates.definition: The concept of Civil Arrest refers to the act of detaining a person by legal authority in order to enforce a civil judgment against them.

candidates.label: Property Tax Payment
candidates.definition: A Property Tax Payment refers to the payment made by property owners to local government authorities, based on the assessed value of their property. This payment is used to fund various public services such as education, infrastructure, and emergency services within the community.

candidates.label: Recurrent Taxes on Property
candidates.definition: Recurrent Taxes on Property are regular, ongoing taxes imposed on specific types of property or goods, typically assessed annually. Although rare in OECD member countries, these taxes can include levies on cattle, jewelry, windows, and other visible signs of wealth.

candidates.label: Real Estate Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Payment of Recurrent Taxes on Immovable Property refers to the regular, often annual, tax payments made by property owners to local or national governments based on the assessed value of their real estate. These taxes fund public services such as education, infrastructure, and emergency services.

candidates.label: Litigation Events
candidates.definition: Events occurring during the course of disputes.

candidates.label: Litigation Hold Event
candidates.definition: A Litigation Hold Event is a specific event within the context of a legal dispute where a party is required to preserve relevant evidence.

candidates.label: General Discharge Under Honorable Conditions
candidates.definition: Often referred to simply as a General Discharge, this type indicates satisfactory service but the individual fell short of meeting all expectations.

candidates.label: Closing Event
candidates.definition: The Closing Event is a specific type of event that occurs during a merger and acquisition or transaction, marking the completion of the deal.
synonyms: クロージングイベント

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: Financial Obligations
candidates.definition: Financial Obligations (Financial Liabilities) refer to any monetary commitments or liabilities an individual or entity is responsible for, such as paying off debts, fulfilling contract terms, or covering recurring expenses. These obligations can be short-term or long-term and failing to meet them could result in financial penalties or legal consequences

candidates.label: Civil Court
candidates.definition: A Civil Court is a type of court forum that has jurisdiction over non-criminal cases involving private disputes between individuals or organizations.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court
candidates.definition: A court in New York State that handles civil legal matters.

candidates.label: New York City - Civil Court
candidates.definition: N.Y. City Civ. Ct.

candidates.label: Texas Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: The Texas Court of Civil Appeals is a specific court within the Texas State Courts system that hears appeals in civil cases.

candidates.label: Alabama Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Ala. Civ. App.

candidates.label: Oklahoma Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Okla. Civ. App.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County
candidates.definition: A court located in Bronx County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Kings County
candidates.definition: A court located in Kings County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Kings County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Queens County
candidates.definition: A court located in Queens County, New York that handles civil legal disputes within its jurisdiction.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Queens County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - New York County
candidates.definition: A court located in New York County that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - New York County

candidates.label: Civil Rights Center
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor Civil Rights Center (CRC) develops, administers, and enforces Departmental policies, practices, and procedures pursuant to Title VI and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Section 188 of the Workforce Investment Act (WIA), as amended; the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended; the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, as amended; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; the Equal Pay Act of 1963; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, as amended; Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, as amended; the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978; the Civil Rights Act of 1991; the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation (No FEAR) Act; and related statutes and Executive Orders.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Division
candidates.definition: The Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice, created in 1957 by the enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1957, works to uphold the civil and constitutional rights of all Americans, particularly some of the most vulnerable members of our society. The Division enforces federal statutes prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, disability, religion, familial status and national origin.

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
candidates.definition: The Office for Civil Rights (OCR) is a sub-agency of the U.S. Department of Education that is primarily focused on enforcing civil rights laws prohibiting schools from engaging in discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or membership in patriotic youth organizations.
synonyms: Bureau des droits civils - Ministère de l'Éducation
Escritório de Direitos Civis - Departamento de Educação
Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
Oficina de Derechos Civiles - Departamento de Educación

hierarchy_path.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - HHS
candidates.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) enforces federal civil rights laws, conscience and religious freedom laws, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules, and the Patient Safety Act and Rule, which together protect fundamental rights of nondiscrimination, conscience, religious freedom, and health information privacy.

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
candidates.definition: The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is a bipartisan, independent commission of the United States federal government, created in 1957, that is charged with the responsibility for investigating, reporting on, and making recommendations concerning civil rights issues in the United States.
synonyms: Comisión de Derechos Civiles de los Estados Unidos
Comissão de Direitos Civis dos EUA

candidates.definition: The Office of the U.S. Intellectual Property Enforcement Coordinator is dedicated to the protection of the American intellectual property that powers our economy.

candidates.definition: The Defense Contract Management Agency (DCMA) is an agency of the United States federal government reporting to the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment.

candidates.definition: The Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs (OFCCP) is part of the U.S. Department of Labor.

candidates.label: Farm Service Agency
candidates.definition: The Farm Service Agency (FSA) is the United States Department of Agriculture agency into which were merged several predecessor agencies, including the Agricultural Stabilization and Conservation Service (ASCS). The ASCS was, as the FSA is now, primarily tasked with the implementation of farm conservation and regulation laws around the country.

candidates.label: UK Legal Aid Agency
candidates.definition: The Legal Aid Agency, formerly known as the Legal Services Commission, is an organization responsible for providing legal aid and ensuring that individuals who cannot afford legal representation have access to legal assistance. It oversees the funding and administration of legal aid services, promoting equal access to justice.

candidates.label: All Other Personal Services
candidates.definition: See industry description for 812990.

candidates.label: All Other Personal Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal services (except personal care services, death care services, drycleaning and laundry services, pet care services, photofinishing services, or parking space and/or valet parking services).

candidates.label: Other Personal Care Services
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal care services (except hair, nail, facial, nonpermanent makeup, or non-medical diet and weight reducing services).

candidates.label: Other Personal Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal care services (except hair, nail, facial, or nonpermanent makeup services).

candidates.label: Personal and Laundry Services Industry
candidates.definition: Industries in the Personal and Laundry Services industry group provide personal and laundry services to individuals, households, and businesses. Services performed include: personal care services; death care services; laundry and dry cleaning services; and a wide range of other personal services, such as pet care (except veterinary) services, photofinishing services, temporary parking services, and dating services.,Industries in the Personal and Laundry Services industry group provide personal and laundry services to individuals, households, and businesses. Services performed include: personal care services; death care services; laundry and drycleaning services; and a wide range of other personal services, such as pet care (except veterinary) services, photofinishing services, temporary parking services, and dating services.

candidates.label: Health and Personal Care Retailers Industry
candidates.definition: Industries in the Health and Personal Care Retailers subsector retail health and personal care merchandise.  Establishments in this subsector are characterized principally by the products they retail, and some health and personal care retailers have specialized staff including pharmacists, opticians, and other professionals engaged in retailing, advising customers, and/or fitting the product sold to the customer's needs.

candidates.definition: See industry description for 811490.

Other Personal and Household Goods Repair and Maintenance

candidates.label: Plastics Product Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in processing new or spent (i.e., recycled) plastics resins into intermediate or final products, using such processes as compression molding; extrusion molding; injection molding; blow molding; and casting. Within most of these industries, the production process is such that a wide variety of products can be made.

hierarchy_path.label: Plastics and Rubber Products Manufacturing Industry

candidates.label: All Other Textile Product Mills
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing nonapparel textile products (except carpet, rugs, curtains, linens, bags, and canvas products) from purchased materials.  This industry includes establishments primarily engaged in decorative stitching such as embroidery or other art needlework on textile products, including apparel.

candidates.label: Wood Product Manufacturing Industry
candidates.definition: Establishments in the Wood Product Manufacturing subsector manufacture wood products, such as lumber, plywood, veneers, wood containers, wood flooring, wood trusses, manufactured homes (i.e., mobile homes), and prefabricated wood buildings.  The production processes of the Wood Product Manufacturing subsector include sawing, planing, shaping, laminating, and assembling wood products starting from logs that are cut into bolts, or lumber that then may be further cut, or shaped by lathes or other shaping tools.  The lumber or other transformed wood shapes may also be subsequently planed or smoothed, and assembled into finished products, such as wood containers.  The Wood Product Manufacturing subsector includes establishments that make wood products from logs and bolts that are sawed and shaped, and establishments that purchase sawed lumber and make wood products.  With the exception of sawmills and wood preservation establishments, the establishments are grouped into industries mainly based on the specific products manufactured.
synonyms: Industria de la fabricación de madera y papel
Industrie de la fabrication du bois et du papier
Indústria de fabricação de madeira e papel

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Civil Law System
candidates.definition: Civil Law is a legal system that is primarily based on written codes and statutes, rather than case law. This system emphasizes comprehensive sets of laws that are codified and accessible, guiding judges in applying the law to individual cases.

candidates.label: Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: A court's body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing civil actions.

candidates.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation
candidates.definition: U.S. federal courts' body of procedural rules governing Multidistrict Litigation (MDLs).
synonyms: Regeln des Justizausschusses für multidisziplinäre Rechtsstreitigkeiten
Regras do Painel Judicial de Litígios Multidistritais

多地区訴訟司法パネルの規則

hierarchy_path.label: Rules of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation

candidates.label: Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in normal brain function caused by an external mechanical force, such as a blow or jolt to the head. TBI can result in temporary or permanent cognitive, physical, and psychological impairments, requiring immediate medical attention and often long-term rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
candidates.definition: Severe traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a critical condition caused by a significant impact or force to the head, resulting in extended loss of consciousness or amnesia, severe cognitive impairments, and potential long-term disabilities. It often necessitates emergency medical intervention, intensive care, and prolonged rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Tort Claimants Committee
candidates.definition: A Tort Claimants Committee is typically established within bankruptcy proceedings or large-scale litigation contexts to represent the interests of individuals who have tort claims against the debtor or defendant. This committee works to ensure that the rights and recovery opportunities of tort claimants are protected and maximized during the legal process.

candidates.label: Civil Union
candidates.definition: A civil union is a legally recognized arrangement similar to marriage, which provides some of the legal and social protections afforded to married couples. However, it may lack some of the federal benefits and recognition that come with marriage.

candidates.label: Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A limited liability company (LLC) is a corporate structure in the United States whereby the owners are not personally liable for the company's debts or liabilities. Limited liability companies are hybrid entities that combine the characteristics of a corporation with those of a partnership or sole proprietorship.

candidates.label: Benefit Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A Benefit Limited Liability Company (BLLC) is a type of limited liability company that combines the liability protection of a traditional LLC with a commitment to pursuing public benefits alongside profit. These companies are structured to focus on social and environmental goals while still operating as for-profit entities.

candidates.label: Professional Limited Liability Company
candidates.definition: A professional limited liability company ('PLLC') is a U.S. business entity designed for licensed professionals, such as lawyers, doctors, architects, engineers, accountants, and chiropractors. While many businesses choose to form a limited liability company ('LLC') because of the tax, limited liability, and other benefits, some states don't allow LLCs to be owned by professionals whose occupation requires a license. In these states, licensed professionals who want the benefits of an LLC must form a PLLC instead.
synonyms: Gesellschaft mit beschränkter Haftung für Freiberufler
プロフェッショナル有限責任会社

candidates.label: Down Syndrome
candidates.definition: Down Syndrome is a genetic disorder caused by the presence of an extra chromosome 21, known as Trisomy 21. This condition leads to developmental delays, intellectual disability, and characteristic physical features.

candidates.label: Mosaic Down Syndrome
candidates.definition: Mosaic Down syndrome is a genetic condition in which an individual has a mixture of cells, some with the usual two copies of chromosome 21 and others with three copies (trisomy 21). This results in a mosaic pattern of normal and abnormal cells.

candidates.label: Non-Mosaic Down Syndrome
candidates.definition: Non-mosaic Down syndrome is a genetic disorder where individuals have three copies of chromosome 21 in all their cells (chromosome 21 trisomy) or an extra copy of chromosome 21 attached to another chromosome (chromosome 21 translocation). This condition results in characteristic physical features, developmental delays, and intellectual disability.

hierarchy_path.label: Congenital Disorders that Affect Multiple Body Systems

candidates.label: Intellectual Property Management
candidates.definition: Intellectual Property Management refers to the process of managing and protecting intangible assets such as patents, trademarks, copyrights, and domain names.

candidates.label: Legal Use Cases
candidates.definition: Legal Use Cases refers to a set of various categories of tasks within the legal industry, which can be completed by humans, machines, or both. Examples include legal research, contract management, intellectual property management, compliance management, and other organizational' use cases for legal work.

candidates.label: Contract Management
candidates.definition: Contract Management refers to the process of overseeing and administering contracts from creation to execution, including tasks such as review, negotiation, e-signatures, smart contracts, intake, notarization, and automation.

candidates.label: CRM Management
candidates.definition: CRM Management refers to the process of managing customer relationships and interactions with a law firm, including the use of technology and data analysis to improve client satisfaction and retention.

candidates.label: Fund Formation Management Products and Services
candidates.definition: Fund Formation Management (Products and Services) refers to the legal services and products related to the creation and management of investment funds.

hierarchy_path.label: Fund Formation Management Products and Services

candidates.label: Litigation Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Management involves the management of various aspects of a legal case, including but not limited to, retention and litigation hold management, redaction management, expert witness management, brief analysis management, jury selection management, citation check management, remote trial management, eDiscovery services, exhibit management, and costs and settlement management.

candidates.label: Litigation Finance Management
candidates.definition: Litigation finance management refers to the process of managing the financial aspects of a legal case, including funding, budgeting, and accounting, in order to maximize the potential return on investment for the parties involved.

candidates.label: Litigation Analytics Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Analytics Management is the systematic process of collecting, analyzing, and utilizing data and insights from legal disputes to inform and improve decision-making, strategy, and performance in legal practice, often through the use of specialized software and tools.

candidates.label: Litigation Collaboration Management
candidates.definition: Litigation Collaboration Management refers to the process of managing and coordinating the efforts of multiple parties involved in a legal dispute, with the goal of achieving a successful outcome.

candidates.label: Retention and Litigation Hold Management
candidates.definition: Retention and Litigation Hold Management refers to the process of preserving and managing electronic data that may be relevant to a legal matter, in order to comply with legal obligations and avoid spoliation sanctions.

candidates.label: Vila Real
candidates.definition: Vila Real is a city in Portugal.

candidates.label: Product Liability Claims
candidates.definition: Product Liability Claims refer to civil tort claims that arise when a consumer is injured or suffers damages due to a defective or dangerous product, and seeks compensation from the manufacturer or seller of the product.

candidates.label: Strict Product Liability
candidates.definition: Strict Product Liability is a legal concept that holds manufacturers and sellers responsible for any harm caused by their products, regardless of fault or negligence, if the product is found to be defective.

candidates.label: Product Liability Defenses
candidates.definition: Product Liability Defenses refer to the legal arguments and strategies used by defendants in a product liability case to defend against claims of liability for harm caused by their product, including arguments related to the inherent safety of the product, the scope of its intended use, the knowledge and experience of the user, and the balance of risks and benefits associated with the product.
synonyms: Défenses en matière de responsabilité du produit
הגנות בפשיטת רגל בגין אחריות מוצר

candidates.label: Product Liability Representation
candidates.definition: Product Liability Representation is a clause in merger and acquisition agreements where the selling party attests to the absence of product defects or claims arising from sold or distributed products of the target company. It serves to protect the buyer from potential undisclosed liabilities or claims related to product malfunctions, damages, or injuries that may arise after the acquisition.

candidates.label: Agent Liability
candidates.definition: Agent Liability refers to the legal responsibility of an individual or entity acting as an agent for another party, for any harm caused to a third party due to their negligence or breach of fiduciary duty.

candidates.label: Family Liability
candidates.definition: Family Liability refers to the legal responsibility of family members for the actions or debts of other family members, including spousal and parent-child liability, as well as agency and vicarious liability.

candidates.label: Spousal Liability
candidates.definition: Spousal Liability refers to the legal responsibility of one spouse for the debts incurred by the other spouse.

candidates.label: Education Liability
candidates.definition: Education Liability refers to the legal responsibility and accountability of educational institutions and their staff for any harm or injury caused to students or their families, including civil claims related to privacy of school records, corporal punishment, compulsory education, and prayer in public school.

candidates.label: Construction Liability
candidates.definition: Construction Liability refers to the legal responsibility of parties involved in construction projects for damages or injuries resulting from their actions or inactions, including claims related to construction defects or negligence.

candidates.label: Liability Clause
candidates.definition: A Liability Clause is a provision in a contract that outlines the responsibility of one party to compensate the other party for any losses or damages incurred as a result of a breach of the contract.

candidates.label: Civil Court Proceeding
candidates.definition: A lawsuit; all or some part of a cause heard and determined by a court. Any legal step or action taken at the direction of, or by the authority of a court.

candidates.label: Bankruptcy Claims Practice
candidates.definition: Bankruptcy Claims Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a bankruptcy matter, specifically related to the filing, objection, and review of claims.

candidates.label: Claims Filing Practice
candidates.definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

candidates.label: Real Estate Permit Review
candidates.definition: Real Estate Permit Review consists of legal services related to relevant regulatory authorities' formal evaluation process to assess and approve or deny permit applications related to real estate development or modification. The review ensures that the proposed activities comply with existing land use regulations, building codes, and other applicable laws and policies.

candidates.label: Real Estate Design Review
candidates.definition: Real Estate Design Review is the process by which proposed architectural and site designs for a development project are evaluated for their adherence to local design standards, aesthetics, and land use compatibility. This review is usually conducted by a designated committee or governing body and may involve public hearings, expert consultations, and revisions to the initial plans.

candidates.label: M&A F6 Real Property Practice
candidates.definition: M&A F6 Real Property Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a merger and acquisition transaction that involves real estate property.
synonyms: M&A F6 Prática de Propriedade Imobiliária
M&A F6 פרקטיקת נכסי נדלן אמיתיים
Práctica de propiedad inmobiliaria real M&A F6
Práctica inmobiliaria de propiedad real M&A F6

hierarchy_path.label: M&A F Regulatory and Specialty Matters Practice

candidates.label: Real Estate Special Exception
candidates.definition: A Real Estate Special Exception is a provision in zoning law that allows a property to be used in a way that deviates from the general zoning regulations of that area, usually granted after review and approval by a local governing body. This exception is subject to specific conditions and limitations aimed at minimizing adverse effects on surrounding properties.

candidates.label: Real Estate Zoning Modifications
candidates.definition: Real Estate Zoning Modifications refer to changes in the zoning classifications or rules governing the use, development, or characteristics of a specific parcel of land. These modifications are generally initiated by property owners and require approval from local zoning authorities, often after public hearings, assessments, and legal evaluations.

candidates.label: Dispute Service
candidates.definition: A process that describes a conflict/adversarial event potentially or actually involving a second party. This can include government entity or internal investigation

candidates.label: Transactional Practice
candidates.definition: Any process that involves two or more parties in creation of a contract.
synonyms: トランザクション実務

candidates.label: Litigation Docket Number
candidates.definition: A Litigation Docket Number is a unique identifier assigned to a legal case that is used to track and manage the case within the court system.

candidates.label: Governmental Civil Law Division
candidates.definition: A Governmental Civil Law Division is a governmental entity that handles civil legal matters involving the government. It provides legal advice to government agencies, represents the government in civil litigation, and ensures compliance with civil laws and regulations.

candidates.label: Claims Agent
candidates.definition: A claims agent is a person who acts on behalf of an individual or organization to manage and process insurance claims.
synonyms: クレームエージェント

candidates.label: Real Estate Operator
candidates.definition: A Real Estate Operator is a professional or entity responsible for the day-to-day management, maintenance, and operations of real estate properties. This role includes overseeing property functions, ensuring tenant satisfaction, handling leasing activities, and maintaining the physical condition of the property.

candidates.label: Real Estate Developer
candidates.definition: A Real Estate Developer is a professional or company that acquires land, finances real estate deals, builds or renovates properties, and arranges for their sale or lease. This role involves various stages of development, including market research, site selection, obtaining permits, and managing construction projects.

candidates.label: Single Asset Real Estate Debtor
candidates.definition: A Single Asset Real Estate Debtor (SARE Debtor) refers to a specific classification in bankruptcy proceedings for debtors whose primary business involves a single real estate property. This classification impacts how bankruptcy cases are handled, particularly by expediting the process and applying unique rules concerning automatic stays and reorganization plans.

candidates.label: Buyer
candidates.definition: A buyer or purchaser generally means a person, company or entity contracting with a seller for services or merchandise to be provided or delivered for a named individual. In a strict legal sense, a buyer may also include someone who acquires title to something by deed, devise, execution, prescription, possession, occupancy, or escheat.

candidates.label: Agency Service Provider
candidates.definition: An Agency Service Provider in project finance transactions acts as an intermediary between the various parties involved, ensuring smooth communication and coordination. This entity manages administrative tasks, facilitates financial transactions, and ensures compliance with contractual obligations.

candidates.label: Product Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to liability for injuries caused by defective or unsafe products.
synonyms: Droit de la responsabilité du produit

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Personal Property Law
candidates.definition: Matters relating to personal property — not real property — such as damage to vehicles, ownership of valuables (e.g., jewelry), trespass to chattels, etc.

candidates.label: Personal Insolvency Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to debt relief for individuals.

candidates.label: Personal and Family Law
candidates.definition: Law that deals with family matters and domestic relations.

candidates.label: Lender Liability Law
candidates.definition: Law which holds lender liable for a borrower's financial losses that are directly or indirectly related to the lender's actions.

candidates.label: Civil Contract Law
candidates.definition: Civil Contract Law refers to a subset of Civil Caw (e.g., civil law countries) that deals with agreements between private entities, as opposed to public or governmental entities. This body of law encompasses the creation, interpretation, and enforcement of these agreements, as well as resolution of disputes related to contract performance, breach, or termination.

candidates.label: Constitutional and Civil Rights Law
candidates.definition: Rights that protect individuals' freedom from infringement by governments, social organizations, and private individuals.
synonyms: Derecho Constitucional y de Derechos Civiles
Direito Constitucional e dos Direitos Civis
Droit constitutionnel et des droits civils

candidates.label: Property Rights and Transactions Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to the acquisition and transfer of real property.
synonyms: Derecho de Propiedad y Ley de Transacciones
Direito de Propriedade e Lei de Transações
Droit des droits de propriété et des transactions

candidates.label: Real Property Law
candidates.definition: Law that governs the various forms of ownership and tenancy in real property.

candidates.label: Personal Goods
candidates.definition: Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

candidates.label: Intellectual Property Assets
candidates.definition: Intellectual property is a broad categorical description for the set of intangibles owned and legally protected by a company from outside use or implementation without consent. Intellectual property can consist of patents, trade secrets, copyrights, franchises, and trademarks or simply ideas.

candidates.label: Religious Property - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A Religious Property is a property and real estate asset used by a religious organization for worship and religious activities. This property can include the main worship building, additional structures, and the surrounding land.

candidates.label: Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate asset type

candidates.label: Office Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate office buildings.

candidates.label: Retail Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: Real estate retail properties including malls, stores, and restaurants.
synonyms: Activo inmobiliario de venta al por menor

candidates.label: Logging Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A logging real estate asset refers to a property that contains or is primarily used for the extraction of timber through logging activities. This asset is crucial in the context of forestry law, environmental law, and property law, as it involves the rights to harvest, manage, and sell timber resources.

candidates.label: Coastal Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A Coastal Real Estate Asset refers to property located along the coastlines of oceans or seas. These assets typically offer scenic views, access to water-based activities, and come with unique environmental and regulatory considerations related to coastal management and conservation.

candidates.label: Mining - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: A mining real estate asset is a type of property that includes land and any minerals or resources that can be extracted from it, such as coal or gold.

hierarchy_path.label: Natural Resources Extraction Real Estate Asset

candidates.label: Airport - Real Estate Asset
candidates.definition: An Airport - Real Estate Asset refers to the land, buildings, and infrastructure associated with an airport, including runways, terminals, and other facilities. This type of asset involves both operational and non-operational properties that contribute to the airport's functionality and revenue generation.

hierarchy_path.label: Industrial and Logistics - Real Estate Asset

candidates.label: Brazilian real
candidates.definition: Locations used: Brazil

candidates.label: Unidad de Valor Real (funds code)
candidates.definition: Locations used: Colombia
synonyms: Unidad de Valor Real (código de fondos)

candidates.label: Personal Information
candidates.definition: Personal Information refers to any data that relates to an identified or identifiable individual. This includes both sensitive personal information, which requires greater protection due to its potential to cause harm if disclosed, and personally identifiable information (PII), which can be used to identify, contact, or locate a specific individual.

candidates.label: Sensitive Personal Information
candidates.definition: Sensitive Personal Information refers to personal data that requires greater protection due to its nature and the potential harm that could result from its unauthorized disclosure. This includes data such as health records, financial information, racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, and biometric data.

candidates.label: Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction
candidates.definition: A Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is a legal request made by a defendant to dismiss a case on the grounds that the court does not have the authority to hear the case due to the defendant not having sufficient contacts with the jurisdiction.
synonyms: Antrag auf Abweisung wegen fehlender persönlicher Zuständigkeit
Moción de Desestimación por Falta de Jurisdicción Personal
Moción de Inadmisión por Falta de Jurisdicción Personal

Requête en rejet pour absence de juridiction personnelle

व्यक्तिगत अधिकरण की कमी के कारण खारिजी के लिए आवेदन

因缺乏个人管辖权而驳回动议

hierarchy_path.label: Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Jurisdiction
Motion to Dismiss for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction

candidates.label: Catalogue
candidates.definition: A Catalogue is a type of directory that systematically lists, describes, and often categorizes items or services, typically for the purpose of identification, reference, or purchase. Unlike a simple directory, a catalogue often includes additional information about the listed items, such as specifications, prices, or images.

candidates.label: Requests for Production
candidates.definition: Requests for Production are a type of Discovery Document that require a party to provide specific documents or items for inspection and copying by the opposing party.

candidates.label: Civil Cover Sheet
candidates.definition: A Civil Cover Sheet is a document used in trial court practice to provide basic information about a civil case, such as the parties involved and the nature of the case.
synonyms: 民事訴訟カバーシート

candidates.label: Motion to Limit Claims
candidates.definition: A Motion to Limit Claims is a formal request made during a trial to restrict the scope of claims or issues that can be raised by a party, typically to prevent undue prejudice or confusion.
synonyms: दावों को सीमित करने के लिए याचिका

candidates.label: Answer with Cross-Claims
candidates.definition: Answer with Cross-Claims refers to a legal response to a complaint or claim made by a defendant against a co-defendant in a lawsuit, asserting that the co-defendant is responsible for all or part of the damages being claimed.

candidates.label: Omnibus Claims Objections
candidates.definition: Omnibus Claims Objections are procedural tools used in bankruptcy proceedings to object to multiple claims simultaneously through a single motion or filing. This method is efficient for addressing numerous similar claims or issues in a streamlined manner, helping to expedite the resolution process in complex bankruptcy cases.

candidates.label: General Business Liability Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: General Business Liability Insurance, also known simply as general liability insurance, provides coverage for common business risks, including bodily injury and property damage that occur at the business premises or as a result of business operations. It also often covers legal costs related to these risks. This type of insurance is foundational for most businesses.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Day
candidates.definition: A Personal Day is a type of paid time off provided by employers that allows employees to take leave for personal reasons without specifying the nature of the absence. This benefit is typically separate from vacation days or sick leave, offering flexibility for employees to address personal matters, appointments, or mental health needs without detailed explanation.

candidates.label: Civil Arrest
candidates.definition: The concept of Civil Arrest refers to the act of detaining a person by legal authority in order to enforce a civil judgment against them.

candidates.label: Property Tax Payment
candidates.definition: A Property Tax Payment refers to the payment made by property owners to local government authorities, based on the assessed value of their property. This payment is used to fund various public services such as education, infrastructure, and emergency services within the community.

candidates.label: Recurrent Taxes on Property
candidates.definition: Recurrent Taxes on Property are regular, ongoing taxes imposed on specific types of property or goods, typically assessed annually. Although rare in OECD member countries, these taxes can include levies on cattle, jewelry, windows, and other visible signs of wealth.

candidates.label: Real Estate Tax Payment
candidates.definition: Payment of Recurrent Taxes on Immovable Property refers to the regular, often annual, tax payments made by property owners to local or national governments based on the assessed value of their real estate. These taxes fund public services such as education, infrastructure, and emergency services.

candidates.label: Personal Income
candidates.definition: Personal income refers to the total earnings an individual receives from various sources, including wages, salaries, investments, and business activities. It encompasses all monetary inflows before taxes and deductions and serves as the basis for calculating tax liabilities and evaluating financial well-being.

candidates.label: Financial Obligations
candidates.definition: Financial Obligations (Financial Liabilities) refer to any monetary commitments or liabilities an individual or entity is responsible for, such as paying off debts, fulfilling contract terms, or covering recurring expenses. These obligations can be short-term or long-term and failing to meet them could result in financial penalties or legal consequences

candidates.label: Civil Court
candidates.definition: A Civil Court is a type of court forum that has jurisdiction over non-criminal cases involving private disputes between individuals or organizations.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court
candidates.definition: A court in New York State that handles civil legal matters.

candidates.label: New York City - Civil Court
candidates.definition: N.Y. City Civ. Ct.

candidates.label: Texas Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: The Texas Court of Civil Appeals is a specific court within the Texas State Courts system that hears appeals in civil cases.

candidates.label: Alabama Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Ala. Civ. App.

candidates.label: Oklahoma Court of Civil Appeals
candidates.definition: Okla. Civ. App.

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County
candidates.definition: A court located in Bronx County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Bronx County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Kings County
candidates.definition: A court located in Kings County, New York that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Kings County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - Queens County
candidates.definition: A court located in Queens County, New York that handles civil legal disputes within its jurisdiction.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - Queens County

candidates.label: New York Civil Court - New York County
candidates.definition: A court located in New York County that handles civil legal matters.

hierarchy_path.label: New York Civil Court - New York County

candidates.label: Civil Rights Center
candidates.definition: The Department of Labor Civil Rights Center (CRC) develops, administers, and enforces Departmental policies, practices, and procedures pursuant to Title VI and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Section 188 of the Workforce Investment Act (WIA), as amended; the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended; the Age Discrimination Act of 1975, as amended; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; the Equal Pay Act of 1963; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, as amended; Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990, as amended; the Civil Service Reform Act of 1978; the Civil Rights Act of 1991; the Notification and Federal Employee Antidiscrimination and Retaliation (No FEAR) Act; and related statutes and Executive Orders.

candidates.label: Civil Rights Division
candidates.definition: The Civil Rights Division of the Department of Justice, created in 1957 by the enactment of the Civil Rights Act of 1957, works to uphold the civil and constitutional rights of all Americans, particularly some of the most vulnerable members of our society. The Division enforces federal statutes prohibiting discrimination on the basis of race, color, sex, disability, religion, familial status and national origin.

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
candidates.definition: The Office for Civil Rights (OCR) is a sub-agency of the U.S. Department of Education that is primarily focused on enforcing civil rights laws prohibiting schools from engaging in discrimination on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, or membership in patriotic youth organizations.
synonyms: Bureau des droits civils - Ministère de l'Éducation
Escritório de Direitos Civis - Departamento de Educação
Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education
Oficina de Derechos Civiles - Departamento de Educación
市民権事務局 - 教育省
民权办公室 - 教育部

hierarchy_path.label: Office for Civil Rights - Department of Education

candidates.label: Office for Civil Rights - HHS
candidates.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) enforces federal civil rights laws, conscience and religious freedom laws, the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules, and the Patient Safety Act and Rule, which together protect fundamental rights of nondiscrimination, conscience, religious freedom, and health information privacy.

hierarchy_path.label: U.S. Dept. of Health and Human Services

candidates.label: U.S. Commission on Civil Rights
candidates.definition: The U.S. Commission on Civil Rights is a bipartisan, independent commission of the United States federal government, created in 1957, that is charged with the responsibility for investigating, reporting on, and making recommendations concerning civil rights issues in the United States.
synonyms: Comisión de Derechos Civiles de los Estados Unidos
Comissão de Direitos Civis dos EUA
アメリカ合衆国市民権委員会

candidates.definition: The Office of the U.S. Intellectual Property Enforcement Coordinator is dedicated to the protection of the American intellectual property that powers our economy.

candidates.definition: The Defense Contract Management Agency (DCMA) is an agency of the United States federal government reporting to the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment.

candidates.definition: The Office of Federal Contract Compliance Programs (OFCCP) is part of the U.S. Department of Labor.

candidates.label: Farm Service Agency
candidates.definition: The Farm Service Agency (FSA) is the United States Department of Agriculture agency into which were merged several predecessor agencies, including the Agricultural Stabilization and Conservation Service (ASCS). The ASCS was, as the FSA is now, primarily tasked with the implementation of farm conservation and regulation laws around the country.

candidates.label: UK Legal Aid Agency
candidates.definition: The Legal Aid Agency, formerly known as the Legal Services Commission, is an organization responsible for providing legal aid and ensuring that individuals who cannot afford legal representation have access to legal assistance. It oversees the funding and administration of legal aid services, promoting equal access to justice.

candidates.label: All Other Personal Services
candidates.definition: See industry description for 812990.

candidates.label: All Other Personal Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal services (except personal care services, death care services, drycleaning and laundry services, pet care services, photofinishing services, or parking space and/or valet parking services).

candidates.label: Other Personal Care Services
candidates.definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal care services (except hair, nail, facial, nonpermanent makeup, or non-medical diet and weight reducing services).

candidates.label: Other Personal Care Services
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in providing personal care services (except hair, nail, facial, or nonpermanent makeup services).

candidates.label: Personal and Laundry Services Industry
candidates.definition: Industries in the Personal and Laundry Services industry group provide personal and laundry services to individuals, households, and businesses. Services performed include: personal care services; death care services; laundry and dry cleaning services; and a wide range of other personal services, such as pet care (except veterinary) services, photofinishing services, temporary parking services, and dating services.,Industries in the Personal and Laundry Services industry group provide personal and laundry services to individuals, households, and businesses. Services performed include: personal care services; death care services; laundry and drycleaning services; and a wide range of other personal services, such as pet care (except veterinary) services, photofinishing services, temporary parking services, and dating services.

candidates.label: Health and Personal Care Retailers Industry
candidates.definition: Industries in the Health and Personal Care Retailers subsector retail health and personal care merchandise.  Establishments in this subsector are characterized principally by the products they retail, and some health and personal care retailers have specialized staff including pharmacists, opticians, and other professionals engaged in retailing, advising customers, and/or fitting the product sold to the customer's needs.
synonyms: Health and Personal Care Store Industry

candidates.definition: See industry description for 811490.
